definitions: The indefinite article; one, any, or a single.
A short time ago; recently.
Aaron; the brother of Moses and first high priest of Israel.
Aaronites; the priestly family descended from Aaron, set apart for the high-priestly and priestly service of Israel.
Abaddon; the destroyer, named in Revelation.
Abagtha; one of the chamberlains or court officers of Ahasuerus in the book of Esther.
Abana; one of the rivers of Damascus, praised by Naaman.
abana.note: Location: Usually identified with Barada River.

definitions: Abarim; a mountain range or chain of heights mentioned in Scripture.
To abase is to bring low, humble, or cast down in condition or esteem.
Abased; past tense or past participial form of 'abase'.
Abasing; present participial form of 'abase', denoting ongoing action or process.
Abated; past tense or past participial form of 'abat'.
Abba; an Aramaic form of address meaning father, used with warmth and filial nearness.
Abda; a biblical personal name, especially borne by a Levite descendant in the line of Jeduthun and by a Benjamite dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Abdeel; the father of Shelemiah in the days of Jeremiah.
Abdi; a biblical personal name, especially borne by Levites named in the histories of worship and reform.
Abdiel; a Gadite chief, son of Guni.
Abdon; a judge of Israel from Ephraim, and also other men or places bearing the same name in Scripture.
Abed-Nego; the Babylonian name given to Azariah, one of the three Hebrew companions of Daniel.
Abel; the son of Adam and Eve, righteous before God and slain by his brother Cain.
Abel-Beth-Maachah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
abel-beth-maachah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Abel-Maim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
abel-maim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Abel-Meholah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
abel-meholah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Abu Sus.

definitions: Abel-Mizraim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
abel-mizraim.note: Location: Often identified with near the mouth of the Jordan River.

definitions: Abel-Shittim; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
abel-shittim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Matabi.

definitions: Abez; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
abez.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Ain el Habus.

definitions: To abhor is to regard with loathing, detestation, or strong aversion.
Abhorred; past tense or past participial form of 'abhor'.
Abhorrest; older second-person singular form of 'abhor', used with 'thou'.
Abhorreth; older third-person singular form of 'abhor'.
Abhorring; present participial form of 'abhor', denoting ongoing action or process.
Abi; the mother of king Hezekiah, elsewhere called Abijah.
Abi-Albon; one of David's mighty men, elsewhere called Abiel the Arbathite.
Abi-Ezer; a clan or family division within Manasseh, especially associated with Gideon.
Abi-Ezrite; a member of the house or clan of Abiezer.
Abi-Ezrites; the clan descended from or associated with Abi-Ezer, especially in the story of Gideon.
Abia; a biblical personal name, used of men in Scripture and also as the Greek form of Abijah in genealogical and priestly references.
Abiah; a biblical personal name borne by both men and women in Scripture, especially in priestly and royal family lines.
Abiasaph; a Korhite in the Levitical line, an ancestor among the keepers of the sanctuary.
Abiathar; a priest, son of Ahimelech, who fled to David and afterward served in the priesthood during his reign.
Abib; the first month of the sacred Hebrew year, corresponding roughly to early spring.
Abida; a son of Midian and grandson of Abraham by Keturah, named among the tribal ancestors descending from Midian.
Abidah; a son of Midian and grandson of Abraham by Keturah, named among the chiefs descended from Midian.
Abidan; the son of Gideoni and prince of the tribe of Benjamin in the wilderness.
To remain, continue, or stay.
Often to endure or bear with firmness.
Abiding; present participial form of 'abide', denoting ongoing action or process.
Abiel; a biblical personal name, especially borne by an ancestor in the house of Saul.
Abiezer; the name of a Manassite clan, especially the family of Gideon, and also of a warrior among David's mighty men.
Abigail; the wise wife of Nabal who afterward became one of David's wives.
Abihail; a biblical personal name borne by both men and women in Scripture, including figures in tribal and royal family lines.
Abihu; one of the sons of Aaron, slain with Nadab for offering strange fire before the LORD.
Abihud; a man named in the genealogies of Israel, and in the New Testament genealogy of Christ.
Abijah; especially the king of Judah son of Rehoboam, and also the name of a priestly course and other biblical figures.
Abijam; a man named in Scripture, son of Rehoboam, father of Asa.
Abilene; a small district in the region of Anti-Lebanon, known in the New Testament as the tetrarchy of Lysanias.
abilene.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Souq Wadi Barada.

definitions: Power, strength, or fitness to do a thing.
Abimael; a descendant of Joktan in the early post-Flood genealogies.
Abimelech; a royal title or throne-name borne by Philistine kings, and also the name of Gideon's son who usurped rule at Shechem.
Abinadab; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the man in whose house the ark remained at Kirjath-jearim, and also by one of Jesse's sons.
Abinoam; the father of Barak, the deliverer associated with Deborah.
Abiram; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the rebel with Dathan against Moses and by a son born in the days of Hiel.
Abishag; the Shunammite woman who ministered to David in his old age.
Abishai; son of Zeruiah and one of David's chief warriors, brother of Joab and Asahel.
Abishalom; a man named in Scripture, son of David, father of Tamar.
Abishua; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the priestly descendant of Aaron through Phinehas.
Abishur; a man of the tribe of Judah named in the genealogies.
Abital; one of David's wives and the mother of Shephatiah.
Abitub; a man in the genealogies of Benjamin and Saul's house.
Abiud; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Matthew.
A plural form referring to more than one abject.
Able; having power, means, or strength to do a thing.
Abner; son of Ner and captain of Saul's host, prominent in the struggle that followed Saul's death.
Aboard; on board a ship or vessel.
Abodest; older second-person singular form of 'abod', used with 'thou'.
To abolish is to do away with, make void, or bring to an end.
Abolished; past tense or past participial form of 'abolish'.
Abominable; hateful, detestable, or loathsome, especially in a moral or religious sense.
In a abominab manner or degree.
A thing greatly detested as unclean, idolatrous, or morally hateful.
Often used of what is especially offensive before God.
To overflow, be plentiful, or increase richly.
Abounding; present participial form of 'abound', denoting ongoing action or process.
Around; concerning; near; in the matter of.
Higher than; over; beyond in place or degree.
Abraham; the patriarch called by God and father of the covenant line.
Abram; the earlier name of Abraham, the patriarch called by God out of Ur and made heir of the covenant promise.
Abroad; out of doors, away from home, or widely in different places.
Absalom; the son of David who rebelled against his father.
The state of being away or not present.
Absent; not present, away, or lacking from the place or company in view.
To abstain is to refrain or hold oneself back from something.
Self-restraint from food, drink, lust, or other indulgence.
Plenty; fullness; more than enough.
Abundant; plentiful, overflowing, or more than sufficient.
In a abundant manner or degree.
To abuse is to misuse or treat wrongfully; abuse is wrongful use or treatment.
Abused; past tense or past participial form of 'abuse'.
A plural form referring to more than one abuser.
Abusing; present participial form of 'abuse', denoting ongoing action or process.
Accad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
accad.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tell Sheshubar.

definitions: To receive willingly or with approval.
Sometimes to regard favorably or admit as pleasing.
Well-pleasing; received with favor; fit to be admitted or approved.
In a acceptab manner or degree.
Favorable receiving, approval, or welcome.
Respect of persons; partial regard based on outward standing.
In some contexts, the receiving or taking of a thing in a certain sense.
Accepted; past tense or past participial form of 'accept'.
Acceptest; older second-person singular form of 'accept', used with 'thou'.
Accepteth; older third-person singular form of 'accept'.
Accepting; present participial form of 'accept', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one acces.
Accho; a northern coastal city in the tribal allotments, later known as an important port.
accho.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Akko.

definitions: Accompanied; past tense or past participial form of 'accompany'.
To accompany is to go with or attend another.
Accompanying; present participial form of 'accompany', denoting ongoing action or process.
To accomplish is to complete, fulfill, or bring to pass.
Completed; fully carried out; brought to fulfillment.
In some contexts, well-furnished or skilled.
Accomplishing; present participial form of 'accomplish', denoting ongoing action or process.
Fulfillment, completion, or bringing to pass.
Accord is agreement or harmony; to accord is to agree or correspond.
In agreement with; in proportion to; as suits or answers to a rule, word, or purpose.
In a according manner or degree.
Counted, reckoned, or regarded as being such.
Accounting; present participial form of 'account', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one account.
Accursed; past tense or past participial form of 'accurse'.
A charge brought against a person; an imputation of fault or crime.
To accuse is to charge with fault, crime, or offense.
Accused; past tense or past participial form of 'accuse'.
An accuser is one who brings a charge against another.
A plural form referring to more than one accuser.
Accuseth; older third-person singular form of 'accuse'.
Accusing; present participial form of 'accuse', denoting ongoing action or process.
Accustomed; past tense or past participial form of 'accustom'.
Aceldama; a field or tract of ground mentioned in Scripture.
Achaia; the southern Greek region or province so called in New Testament usage.
It often includes Corinth and the churches within that district.
achaia.note: Location: The southern Greek province, especially the district around Corinth and the Peloponnese.

definitions: Achaicus; a Christian known to Paul, associated with the believers of Achaia.
Achan; the Israelite who took of the accursed thing at Jericho and brought trouble upon Israel.
Achar; the man who troubled Israel by taking the accursed thing after Jericho, elsewhere called Achan.
Achaz; a man named in Scripture, son of Jotham, husband of Abi, father of Hezekiah.
Achbor; the father of Baal-hanan king of Edom, and also a court official in the days of Josiah.
Achim; an ancestor in the genealogy of Jesus given by Matthew.
Achish; a man named in Scripture, son of Maoch.
Achmetha; the royal city where the decree concerning the rebuilding of the temple was found in the Persian archives.
achmetha.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Hagmatana.

definitions: Achor; the valley so named near the place where Achan was judged, later used figuratively as a place of trouble turned to hope.
Achsah; the daughter of Caleb, given in marriage to Othniel and noted for asking a field and springs.
Achshaph; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
achshaph.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Regev.

definitions: Achzib; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
achzib.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Achziv.

definitions: To own, confess, or recognize openly as true or due.
Recognition, confession, or open admission of something as true.
Acknowledging; present participial form of 'acknowledge', denoting ongoing action or process.
To acquaint is to make known, inform, or cause one to become familiar with a matter.
Knowledge, familiarity, or a person known to another.
Acquainted; past tense or past participial form of 'acquaint'.
Acquainting; present participial form of 'acquaint', denoting ongoing action or process.
To acquit is to declare free from charge, clear, or release from guilt.
Acre; a measure of land.
A plural form referring to more than one acr.
An act is a deed or doing; to act is to do or perform.
A plural form referring to more than one action.
Activeness, vigor, or energetic operation.
Acts; deeds, doings, or recorded acts.
Adadah; a southern town listed in the inheritance of Judah.
adadah.note: Location: In the southern Negev of Judah, sometimes linked with ruins in the Aroer region.

definitions: Adah; the name of women in early Genesis, especially a wife of Lamech and a wife of Esau.
Adaiah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by priests and returned exiles listed in post-exilic records.
Adalia; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
Adam; the first man, formed by God and set in Eden as the head of the human race.
His fall brought sin and death into the world, and he stands in Scripture as the first man in contrast with Christ.
Adamah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
adamah.note: Location: Often identified with Qarn Hattin.

definitions: Adamant; very hard, unyielding, or stubborn like a hard stone.
Adami; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
adami.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Adami.

definitions: Adar; a southern border town named in the territorial description of Judah.
adar.note: Location: In the far south near the border toward Kadesh-barnea, probably in the northern Sinai or Negev region.

definitions: Adbeel; one of the sons of Ishmael and ancestor of an Ishmaelite line.
To add is to join, increase, or put one thing to another.
Addan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Addar; a genealogical name in the tribe of Benjamin.
Added; past tense or past participial form of 'add'.
An adder is a venomous serpent.
A plural form referring to more than one adder.
Addeth; older third-person singular form of 'add'.
Addi; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Addicted; past tense or past participial form of 'addict'.
Something added, increase, or the act of adding.
A plural form referring to more than one addition.
Addon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Ader; an Edomite named among the chiefs or lines of Edom.
Adiel; especially a Simeonite leader in the days of Hezekiah, and also the name of other men in biblical genealogies.
Adin; the head of a family whose descendants returned from the exile.
Adina; a Reubenite captain or warrior named among David's mighty men.
Adino; the title associated with Adino the Eznite, one of David's mighty men.
Adithaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
adithaim.note: Location: Often identified with Al Haditha.

definitions: To charge solemnly, bind by oath, or earnestly command.
Adjured; past tense or past participial form of 'adjure'.
Adlai; the father of Shaphat, one of David's overseers.
Admah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
admah.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Admatha; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
Administered; past tense or past participial form of 'administer'.
Management, service, or ordered discharge of an office or duty.
A plural form referring to more than one administration.
Wonder, astonishment, or high esteem.
Admired; past tense or past participial form of 'admir'.
To warn, counsel, or gently charge another toward right conduct.
Admonishing; present participial form of 'admonish', denoting ongoing action or process.
Adna; a priest in Ezra's day, and also the name of other post-exilic heads of families.
Adnah; a captain or warrior who came to David, according to the chronicler.
Ado; trouble, stir, bustle, or needless disturbance.
Adoni-Bezek; a Canaanite ruler of Bezek defeated in the days of Judah and Simeon.
Adoni-Zedek; the king of Jerusalem who led a confederacy against Joshua.
Adonijah; especially the son of David who sought the throne near the end of David's reign.
Adonikam; the head of a family whose descendants returned from Babylon.
Adoniram; Solomon's officer over tribute or forced labour, later stoned when sent by Rehoboam.
The placing of one in the standing and privileges of a son.
Adoraim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Adoram; a royal officer over tribute or forced labour, commonly taken as the same man elsewhere called Adoniram.
To adorn is to deck, beautify, or set forth with fitting ornament.
Adorned; past tense or past participial form of 'adorn'.
Adorneth; older third-person singular form of 'adorn'.
Adorning; present participial form of 'adorn', denoting ongoing action or process.
Adrammelech; a son of Sennacherib who took part in his assassination.
Adramyttium; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Adria; a sea or body of water mentioned in Scripture.
adria.note: Location: Usually identified with Adriatic and Ionian Seas.

definitions: Adriel; the son of Barzillai the Meholathite and the husband of Merab, Saul's daughter.
Adullam; a city associated especially with David's refuge and the cave near it.
adullam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet esh Sheikh Madhkur.

definitions: Adullamite; an inhabitant of Adullam, or one belonging to that place.
adullamite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet esh Sheikh Madhkur.

definitions: An adulterer is one guilty of adultery.
A plural form referring to more than one adulterer.
A plural form referring to more than one adulteres.
A plural form referring to more than one adulteress.
A plural form referring to more than one adultery.
Adulterous; guilty of adultery, or figuratively unfaithful in covenant or spiritual allegiance.
Adultery is the sin of violating the marriage bond by unlawful sexual union.
Adummim; a road, ascent, or route mentioned in Scripture.
Advanced; past tense or past participial form of 'advance'.
Profit, benefit, superiority, or favorable position.
Advantaged; past tense or past participial form of 'advantage'.
Advantageth; older third-person singular form of 'advantage'.
Risk, hazard, or a venture upon uncertainty.
Adventured; past tense or past participial form of 'adventure'.
An opponent, enemy, or one who stands against another.
At times an accuser in a legal or spiritual sense.
A plural form referring to more than one adversity.
Trouble, hardship, or an afflicted condition.
To advertise is to give notice, inform, or make known.
Advice is counsel, opinion, or guidance given for direction.
To advise is to counsel, inform, or give direction.
Advised; past tense or past participial form of 'advise'.
Deliberation, consideration, or counsel taken beforehand.
An advocate is one who pleads the cause of another or speaks in his defense.
Aeneas; the man at Lydda whom Peter healed after he had long been bedridden.
Aenon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aenon.note: Location: Often identified with Umm el Umdan.

definitions: Afar; at a distance or from far away.
Far away; at a distance.
A plural form referring to more than one affair.
To affect is to move, influence, or touch the feelings or condition of something.
In some contexts it means to seek after or set affection upon.
Affected; past tense or past participial form of 'affect'.
Affecteth; older third-person singular form of 'affect'.
An inward inclination, desire, or set of the heart.
Often strong feeling directed toward a person or thing.
In a affectionate manner or degree.
Affectioned; past tense or past participial form of 'affection'.
Relationship by marriage or close connection.
To affirm is to declare positively, maintain, or assert as true.
Affirmed; past tense or past participial form of 'affirm'.
To afflict is to trouble, oppress, or bring low with pain or sorrow.
Afflicted; troubled, oppressed, or brought low by suffering.
Afflictest; older second-person singular form of 'afflict', used with 'thou'.
Trouble, distress, or suffering laid upon a person.
Often outward hardship, but sometimes inward grief as well.
Affording; present participial form of 'afford', denoting ongoing action or process.
To affright is to fill with sudden fear or terror.
Affrighted; past tense or past participial form of 'affright'.
Afoot; on foot, or already in motion and underway.
Before; beforehand; previously.
Beforehand; in advance; previously.
In former time; previously; in earlier days.
Afrai; a man of Netophah named among David's mighty warriors.
Afraid; struck with fear or apprehension.
Afresh; anew or again from the beginning.
Following in time, place, order, or manner; afterward to or behind.
Afternoon is the latter part of the day after noon.
After this; later in time; following upon what went before.
A plural form referring to more than one afterward.
Agabus; a New Testament prophet who foretold a great famine and later Paul's bonds.
Agag; the royal name or title of Amalekite kings, especially the king spared by Saul and hewn by Samuel.
Agagite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Agag in Scripture.
Once more; another time; in return.
A plural form referring to more than one again.
In opposition to; toward so as to meet or resist; over against.
Agar; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Abram, mother of Ishmael.
An agate is a precious or ornamental stone.
A plural form referring to more than one agat.
Length of life, a period of time, or a generation.
Aged; past tense or past participial form of 'age'.
Agee; the father of Shammah, one of David's mighty men.
A plural form referring to more than one ag.
Ago; in time past, before now, or long since.
Agone; gone by, past, or already departed.
Agony; intense inward anguish, struggle, or distress.
Agree; to be of one mind, to consent, or to come into accord.
Agreed; past tense or past participial form of 'agree'.
Harmony, consent, or a settled compact between parties.
Agreeth; older third-person singular form of 'agree'.
Agrippa; the name of Herodian rulers in the New Testament, especially the king before whom Paul made his defence in Acts.
Aground; run upon the ground or shallows, especially of a ship.
Ague; a feverish shaking sickness with chills.
Agur; the son of Jakeh, associated with the wise sayings in Proverbs 30.
An exclamation of feeling such as sorrow, surprise, or desire.
An exclamation expressing feeling or calling attention.
Ahab; especially the king of Israel, son of Omri.
Aharah; a Benjamite named in the tribal genealogies, apparently the same figure elsewhere called Ehi.
Aharhel; a Judahite name occurring in the genealogies.
Ahasai; a post-exilic man of Jerusalem named in the genealogical records.
Ahasbai; the father of Eliphelet, one of David's mighty men.
Ahasuerus; the royal name used of a Persian king, especially in the book of Esther.
Ahava; a canal or river-side gathering place near Babylon where Ezra assembled the returning company.
ahava.note: Location: Near Babylon in Mesopotamia; the exact canal or settlement is uncertain.

definitions: Ahaz; king of Judah, noted for idolatry and unbelief in the days of Isaiah.
Ahaziah; the name of kings in Israel and Judah, most commonly the son of Ahab who reigned in Israel.
Ahban; a man of Judah named in the genealogies.
Aher; a genealogical name in the tribe of Benjamin.
Ahi; a genealogical name occurring among the families of Israel.
Ahiah; a priest in Saul's day, and also the name of other men in royal and family lines.
Ahiam; one of David's mighty men.
Ahian; a Benjamite name in the tribal genealogies.
Ahiezer; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the prince of Dan in the wilderness and by a Benjamite who came to David.
Ahihud; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the prince of Asher appointed in the division of the land.
Ahijah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the prophet who foretold the division of the kingdom to Jeroboam.
Ahikam; a man named in Scripture, son of Shaphan, father of Gedaliah.
Ahilud; the father of Jehoshaphat the recorder in the reigns of David and Solomon.
Ahimaaz; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the priest's son who ran with tidings to David.
Ahiman; a biblical personal name, especially borne by an Anakite of Hebron before Israel's conquest.
Ahimelech; the name of priests in Scripture, especially the priest of Nob who aided David and was slain by Saul.
Ahimoth; a Levite in the line of Kohath named in the genealogies of temple service.
Ahinadab; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including one connected with Solomon's officers.
Ahinoam; the name of two women in Scripture, including Saul's wife and David's wife of Jezreel.
Ahio; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including one associated with the ark in the days of David.
Ahira; the son of Enan and prince of the tribe of Naphtali in the wilderness.
Ahiram; a Benjamite ancestor from whom the Ahiramites were reckoned.
Ahiramites; descendants of Ahiram reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Ahisamach; the father of Aholiab, the craftsman associated with the tabernacle work.
Ahishahar; a Benjamite named in the genealogies.
Ahishar; an officer over Solomon's household.
Ahithophel; a man named in Scripture, father of Eliam.
Ahitub; the name of several priests in Scripture, especially in the high-priestly line connected with Eli and later with Zadok.
Ahlab; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ahlab.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet al Mahalib.

definitions: Ahlai; a name in the genealogies, borne in the house of Sheshan.
Ahoah; an ancestral name from which the Ahohite line was reckoned.
Ahohite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Ahoh in Scripture.
Aholah; the symbolic name for Samaria in Ezekiel's prophecy.
Aholiab; the son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, gifted to help make the tabernacle and its furnishings.
Aholibah; the symbolic name for Jerusalem in Ezekiel's prophecy.
Aholibamah; a wife of Esau and also a chief among the Edomites in the genealogical records.
Ahumai; a Judahite named in the genealogies of the families of Bethlehem.
Ahuzam; a descendant in the line of Ashur named in the Judahite genealogies.
Ahuzzath; the friend or companion of Abimelech king of Gerar.
Ai; the city whose capture and later defeat of Israel exposed Achan's sin in the days of Joshua.
ai.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tell.

definitions: Aiah; the father of Rizpah and also a Horite name in the genealogies.
Aiath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aiath.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tell.

definitions: Aided; past tense or past participial form of 'aid'.
Aija; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aija.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Turmus Ayya.

definitions: Aijalon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Ailed; past tense or past participial form of 'ail'.
Ails; troubles; is the matter with.
Ain; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ain.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Asan.

definitions: Air; the atmosphere or open space above the earth, and sometimes the unseen region above.
Ajah; father of Rizpah, Saul's concubine, and also a name appearing in the tribal genealogies.
Ajalon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ajalon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ayalon Valley.

definitions: Akan; a Horite descendant named in the Edomite genealogies, elsewhere called Jakan.
Akkub; a biblical personal name, especially borne by temple porters and post-exilic servants.
Akrabbim; a named place or feature mentioned in Scripture.
akrabbim.note: Location: Commonly placed at or identified with Ma’ale Akrabbim.

definitions: Alabaster is a fine smooth stone, often worked into vessels or boxes for precious ointment.
Alameth; older third-person singular form of 'alams'.
Alammelech; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
alammelech.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Alil.

definitions: Alamoth; a musical direction or title, often understood to refer to maiden voices or a high register.
Alarm; a cry, signal, or trumpet-sound warning of danger or summoning to action.
An exclamation expressing feeling or calling attention.
Although; even though; notwithstanding.
Alemeth; older third-person singular form of 'alems'.
Alexander; a New Testament personal name, especially borne by the man associated with Ephesus and by another noted by Paul.
Alexandria; the great city of Egypt associated with Hellenistic Judaism, learning, and Alexandrian ships in the New Testament.
A plural form referring to more than one alexandrian.
Algum; a precious wood used for building and instruments, probably the same as almug.
Aliah; an Edomite chief descended from Esau; the name also appears in the form Alvah.
Alian; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Alvan.
An alien is a foreigner or one belonging to another people; alien may also describe what is foreign or estranged.
Alienate; to turn away, estrange, or remove from rightful relation or possession.
Alienated; past tense or past participial form of 'alienate'.
A plural form referring to more than one alien.
Alike; similar, equal, or in the same manner.
Alive; living, not dead.
The whole number, amount, or company; every one.
Alleging; present participial form of 'alleg', denoting ongoing action or process.
Allegory; a figurative representation in which one thing is used to signify another deeper meaning.
Alleluia; a cry of praise meaning Praise ye the LORD.
Allied; past tense or past participial form of 'alli'.
Allon; a name meaning oak, borne by the nurse of Rebekah in the place-name Allon-bachuth.
Allon-Bachuth; the oak near Bethel where Deborah Rebekah's nurse was buried.
allon-bachuth.note: Location: Near Bethel in the central hill country of Benjamin or Ephraim, though the exact spot is uncertain.

definitions: To allow is to permit, acknowledge, approve, or admit according to context.
approval, permission, or an allotted portion.
Allowed; past tense or past participial form of 'allow'.
Alloweth; older third-person singular form of 'allow'.
to entice or attract strongly; also the power of such attraction.
Almighty means all-powerful; as a title it belongs supremely to God.
Almodad; one of the sons of Joktan in the early genealogies after the Flood.
Almon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
almon.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Almit.

definitions: Almon-Diblathaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
almon-diblathaim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Libb.

definitions: An almond is the nut-bearing tree or its fruit, notable in Scripture for early budding and watchful symbolism.
A plural form referring to more than one almond.
Almost; nearly or well nigh, but not altogether.
Merciful gifts to the poor; charitable relief bestowed on the needy.
A plural form referring to more than one almsdeed.
Almug; a precious imported wood used for building and instruments, probably the same as algum.
A plural form referring to more than one alo.
Alone; by oneself; without another.
A preposition marking relation in place, time, manner, cause, or degree according to context.
Aloof; at a distance, apart, or away from close contact.
Aloth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aloth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Beer Yeroham.

definitions: Aloud; with an audible voice, openly, or so as to be heard.
Alpha; the first letter of the Greek alphabet, used figuratively for the beginning.
Alphaeus; the father of James the less, and also the name of Levi's father according to one Gospel listing.
Already; by this time, before now, or sooner than expected.
Likewise; in addition; besides.
An altar; a raised place or structure appointed for sacrifice, offering, or sacred service.
According to context it may stand not only for the structure itself but for the sacrificial service connected with it.
To alter is to change or make otherwise.
Altered; past tense or past participial form of 'alter'.
Altereth; older third-person singular form of 'alter'.
Although; notwithstanding that, even if, or though.
Altogether; wholly, entirely, or upon the whole according to context.
Alush; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
alush.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi el Esh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Alvah; an Edomite chief descended from Esau; the name also appears in the form Aliah.
Alvan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Alian.
Always; at all times.
A plural form referring to more than one alway.
The first-person singular present form of be.
Amad; a town in the inheritance of Asher.
amad.note: Location: In the northern territory of Asher, probably in the Carmel-to-Akko region.

definitions: Amal; a descendant of Asher named in the tribal genealogies.
Amalek; a man named in Scripture, son of Eliphaz.
Amalekite; a member of the people of Amalek.
amalekite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Amalekites; a hostile desert people often at war with Israel from the days of the Exodus onward.
Amam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
amam.note: Location: Often identified with Be’er Nevatim.

definitions: Amana; a height or mountain range named with Lebanon and Hermon in the Song.
amana.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jebel Zabadani, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Amariah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by priests and Levites in the lines of Judah and Levi.
Amasa; a captain appointed over the host, first by Absalom and later by David.
Amasai; a biblical personal name, especially borne by one who joined David and by others among priests and Levites.
Amashai; a priestly man dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Amasiah; a leader or captain of Judah in the chronicler's history.
Amazed; past tense or past participial form of 'amaze'.
bewilderment, astonishment, or stunned confusion of mind.
Amaziah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the king of Judah son of Joash.
A messenger sent in another's name with authority to represent him.
an embassy or message sent by ambassadors.
Amber is a glowing appearance or substance of bright, fiery golden color, especially in prophetic description.
An ambush is a hidden attack or a body lying in wait; to ambush is to attack from concealment.
A plural form referring to more than one ambush.
an ambush or concealed attack.
A plural form referring to more than one ambushment.
A solemn affirmation meaning truly, surely, or so be it.
To correct, reform, or make better.
A plural form referring to more than one amend.
Amerce; to punish by a fine or pecuniary penalty.
An amethyst is a precious stone of violet or purple hue.
Ami; the name attached to a family of Solomon's servants in the post-exilic return lists; it also appears in the form Amon.
Amiable; lovely, pleasant, or worthy of affection.
Aminadab; the Greek form of Amminadab, an ancestor in the line leading to David and Christ.
A plural form referring to more than one amis.
Amittai; the father of Jonah the prophet.
Ammah; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
ammah.note: Location: East of Gibeon in Benjamin, on the line of Abner's retreat, though the exact hill is uncertain.

definitions: Ammi; a symbolic name meaning my people.
Ammiel; a biblical personal name borne by several men in Scripture, including the father of Machir's wife and others in tribal or royal records.
Ammihud; borne by several biblical men, especially tribal princes and other family heads in Israel.
Amminadab; a notable man in Scripture, especially the prince of Judah in the wilderness generation and the ancestor in the royal line of David.
Amminadib; a difficult term in the Song of Solomon, often taken as a proper name or princely designation in the imagery of the passage.
Ammishaddai; the father of Ahiezer, prince of the tribe of Dan in the wilderness.
Ammizabad; the son of Benaiah and an officer in David's service.
Ammon; the people and land descended from Lot by that name.
ammon.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Amman.

definitions: Ammonite; a member of the people of Ammon, east of the Jordan.
Ammonites; the people descended from Ammon, dwelling east of Jordan and frequently appearing as neighbours, foes, or political rivals of Israel.
A plural form referring to more than one ammonites.
ammonitess.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Amman.

definitions: Amnon; the son of David who defiled Tamar and was afterward slain.
Amok; the head of a priestly family in post-exilic records.
Amon; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the king of Judah father of Josiah.
In the midst of; mingled with; included within a company or number.
Among; in the midst of.
Amorite; a member of the Amorite people, one of the ancient peoples of Canaan and the surrounding lands.
Amorites; one of the chief Canaanite peoples, often representing the older inhabitants of the land.
Amos; the prophet of Tekoa who prophesied against Israel and the nations, and also a name in the genealogy of Christ.
Amounting; present participial form of 'amount', denoting ongoing action or process.
Amoz; the father of Isaiah the prophet.
A plural form referring to more than one amphipoli.
Amplias; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Amram; the father of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, of the tribe of Levi.
Amramites; descendants of Amram reckoned among the Levitical families.
Amraphel; king of Shinar in the days of Abram.
Amzi; the name of men in the genealogies, especially in Levitical lines.
The indefinite article.
Anab; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
anab.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Anab es Seghireh.

definitions: Anah; the name of persons in Edomite genealogy, including the one noted for finding the mules in the wilderness.
Anaharath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
anaharath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Rekhesh.

definitions: Anaiah; the name of men in post-exilic records, including one who stood with Ezra.
Anak; ancestor of the Anakim, the giant-like people feared by Israel's spies.
Anakims; the giant-like people of Canaan feared by Israel's spies.
Anamim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Anammelech; a god of the Sepharvites, worshipped in the mixed idolatry introduced into Samaria.
Anan; a post-exilic name in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Anani; a descendant of David in the later genealogies.
Ananiah; a post-exilic name, and also the name of a locality associated with returned exiles.
Ananias; a New Testament personal name, especially borne by the husband of Sapphira, the disciple at Damascus, and the high priest before whom Paul spoke.
Anath; a name associated with Shamgar as son of Anath, and also known from ancient Near Eastern usage.
An anathema is a thing devoted to destruction or laid under a curse.
Anathoth; a Benjamite ancestor from whom the Anetothite line was reckoned.
A plural form referring to more than one ancestor.
An anchor is that which holds a ship fast; figuratively it is that which gives firm hope and stability.
A plural form referring to more than one anchor.
Ancient; old, long-standing, or belonging to former times.
A plural form referring to more than one ancient.
A conjunction joining words, clauses, or sentences.
Andrew; one of the twelve apostles, the brother of Simon Peter, often seen bringing others to Jesus.
Andronicus; a Christian greeted by Paul as his kinsman and fellow prisoner.
Anem; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
anem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Beit Jann.

definitions: Aner; an Amorite ally of Abram, and also a Manassite name in the genealogies.
Anethothite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Anethoth in Scripture.
anethothite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras el Kharruba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Anetothite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Anetoth in Scripture.
anetothite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras el Kharruba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: An angel; a messenger, especially a heavenly messenger of God.
Angels; heavenly messengers or ministers of God.
Anger; wrath, indignation, or strong displeasure.
Angered; past tense or past participial form of 'anger'.
Angle; a fishhook or hooked instrument for catching fish.
Angry; moved with anger or displeasure.
Anguish is deep distress, pain, or inward trouble.
Aniam; a descendant of Ephraim in the tribal genealogies.
Anim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
anim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Horbat Anim.

definitions: Anise is a small aromatic plant or seed used as a herb or seasoning.
Ankle; the joint between the leg and the foot.
A plural form referring to more than one ankl.
Anna; the aged prophetess who gave thanks at the presentation of Jesus in the temple.
Annas; the former high priest who retained great influence in the time of Jesus and the apostles.
To apply oil to; to consecrate, set apart, or mark for office or favor.
Anointedst; an old second-person singular form of anoint, used with thou.
Anointest; older second-person singular form of 'anoint', used with 'thou'.
Soon; shortly; after only a brief delay.
One more; one besides; one of the same kind but distinct.
A reply or response.
Sometimes the thing answering to another, whether by correspondence, outcome, or return.
Answerable; corresponding, accountable, or bound to answer according to context.
Answeredst; an old second-person singular form of answer, used with thou.
Answerest; thou answerest; you answer, reply, or respond.
Answering; present participial form of 'answer', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one answer.
Ant; the small industrious insect used in Scripture as an example of diligence and foresight.
one who opposes Christ or sets himself in Christ’s place; in John, a deceiver denying the Father and the Son.
A plural form referring to more than one antichrist.
Antioch; the important city associated with the early Gentile church and with Paul’s missionary work.
Several cities bore the name, so the exact one must be determined from the passage, especially Syrian Antioch or Antioch in Pisidia.
antioch.note: Location: Usually Antioch on the Orontes in Syria, though some passages speak of Antioch in Pisidia.

definitions: Antipas; the faithful martyr named in the message to Pergamos.
A plural form referring to more than one antipatri.
antipatris.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Ras el Ain.

definitions: ancient times or remote age.
Antothijah; a Benjamite named in the genealogies.
Antothite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Antoth in Scripture.
antothite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras el Kharruba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one ant.
Anub; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Anvil; the iron block on which metal is beaten and shaped.
Any one, some, or whatever kind without restriction.
Apace; swiftly, quickly, or at a rapid rate.
Apart; separately, by itself, or at a distance from others.
Apelles; a Christian greeted by Paul as approved in Christ.
A plural form referring to more than one ap.
Apharsachites; an eastern people settled in Samaria by the Assyrian kings.
Apharsathchites; an eastern people transplanted into the land by Assyrian rule.
A plural form referring to more than one apharsit.
apharsites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell Hagmatana.

definitions: Aphek; the name of cities in Scripture, especially places of major battles involving Israel, the Philistines, and Syria.
aphek.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ras el Ain.

definitions: Aphekah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aphekah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet al Hadab.

definitions: Aphiah; an ancestor in Saul's Benjamite line.
Aphik; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Aphrah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aphrah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet et Taiyiba.

definitions: Aphses; the head of one of the priestly courses in the days of David.
Apiece; to each one separately or for each one.
Apollonia; a coastal city passed in the apostolic journeying of Acts.
Apollos; a man named in Scripture.
Apollyon; the destroyer, the Greek name corresponding to Abaddon.
An apostle; one sent with commission.
In the New Testament the word especially refers to the chief messengers of Christ, entrusted with foundational witness and authority.
Apostles; those sent forth with authority, especially the chosen messengers of Christ.
the office, calling, or ministry of an apostle.
A plural form referring to more than one apothecary.
Apothecary; one who prepares perfumes, ointments, or medicinal compounds.
Appaim; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Apparel is clothing or attire; to apparel is to clothe or dress.
Apparelled; past tense or past participial form of 'apparell'.
In a apparent manner or degree.
an earnest request or resort to a higher judgment; also to make such a request.
Appealed; past tense or past participial form of 'appeal'.
Outward aspect, visible form, or what presents itself to the eye.
Sometimes the coming into sight or manifestation of a person or thing.
Came into sight; was seen; became manifest.
Appears; comes into sight; becomes manifest.
Appearing; present participial form of 'appear', denoting ongoing action or process.
To appease is to pacify, quiet, or turn away anger.
Appeased; past tense or past participial form of 'appease'.
Appeaseth; older third-person singular form of 'appease'.
Appertain; to belong, relate, or properly pertain to someone or something.
Appertained; past tense or past participial form of 'appertain'.
Appertaineth; older third-person singular form of 'appertain'.
Appetite is desire for food, or more broadly an inward craving or inclination.
Apphia; a Christian woman greeted in Paul's Epistle to Philemon.
Appii; a station on the Appian Way, known from the meeting of believers with Paul on the road to Rome.
Apple; a named place or feature mentioned in Scripture.
The exact species may not always answer to the modern apple, but the sense is of a valued fruit.
A plural form referring to more than one appl.
Applied; past tense or past participial form of 'apply'.
In a app manner or degree.
To fix, set, assign, or ordain for a purpose or time.
Appointeth; older third-person singular form of 'appoint'.
an arrangement, assignment, or fixed meeting.
Apprehend; to seize, arrest, or lay hold of; also to understand or grasp mentally according to context.
Apprehended; past tense or past participial form of 'apprehend'.
To approach is to draw near; approach is the act of drawing near.
Approached; past tense or past participial form of 'approach'.
Approacheth; older third-person singular form of 'approach'.
Approaching; present participial form of 'approach', denoting ongoing action or process.
To prove, test, and show to be sound.
Often more than merely liking something.
Approvest; older second-person singular form of 'approve', used with 'thou'.
Approveth; older third-person singular form of 'approve'.
Approving; present participial form of 'approve', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one apron.
Fit, suitable, or well-disposed for a task.
Ready and able, not merely naturally clever.
Aquila; the Jewish Christian husband of Priscilla and a fellow worker of Paul.
Ar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ar.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet al Balua.

definitions: Ara; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Arab; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
arab.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet er Rabiyeh.

definitions: Arabah; the great rift valley or plain running south from the Sea of Galilee and especially toward the Dead Sea and beyond.
Arabia; the broad desert and tribal region east and south of Canaan, associated with Ishmaelite and other desert peoples.
arabia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Arabian Peninsula.

definitions: Arabian; belonging to Arabia or one of its desert peoples.
arabian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Arabian Peninsula.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one arabian.
arabians.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Arabian Peninsula.

definitions: Arad; a Canaanite king of the south who opposed Israel.
Arah; the head of a family whose descendants returned from Babylon.
Aram; a son of Shem in the table of nations, and also a personal name appearing in genealogical form as Ram / Aram.
A plural form referring to more than one aramites.
Aran; a descendant in the line of Seir the Horite.
Ararat; the land or mountainous region where the ark rested after the flood.
It also appears as a kingdom or territorial name in later history.
ararat.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Urartu.

definitions: Araunah; a man named in Scripture.
Arba; the great man from whom Kirjath-arba, later Hebron, took its name.
Arbah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
arbah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Arbathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Arbath in Scripture.
Arbite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Arb in Scripture.
An archangel is a chief or principal angel.
Archelaus; the son of Herod the Great who ruled in Judaea after his father.
An archer is one who shoots with a bow.
A plural form referring to more than one archer.
A plural form referring to more than one arch.
A plural form referring to more than one archevit.
Archi; the district or borderland connected with the Archites.
Archippus; a Christian worker mentioned in Colossians and Philemon.
Archite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Arch in Scripture.
A plural form referring to more than one arcturu.
Ard; a Benjamite ancestor from whom the Ardites were reckoned.
Ardites; descendants of Ard reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Ardon; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
The present plural form of be.
Areli; a son of Gad from whom the Arelites were reckoned.
Arelites; descendants of Arel reckoned among the families of Gad.
Areopagite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Areopag in Scripture.
A plural form referring to more than one areopagu.
Aretas; the name of Arabian kings, especially the ruler connected with Damascus in Paul's account.
Argob; one of the officers of Solomon over a district in Bashan.
Arguing; present participial form of 'argu', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one argument.
Aridai; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
Aridatha; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
Arieh; the name of men in royal and genealogical contexts, including one of the officers slain by Zimri.
Ariel; a biblical personal name, and also a symbolic name applied to Jerusalem in Isaiah.
Aright; rightly, correctly, or in proper order.
Arimathaea; the town of Joseph of Arimathaea, who begged the body of Jesus.
Arioch; the name of kings and officials in Scripture, especially the captain of Nebuchadnezzar's guard over the wise men.
Arisai; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
Arises; rises up; springs forth.
It may also mean comes into being or comes forward in action.
Arising; present participial form of 'arise', denoting ongoing action or process.
Aristarchus; a Macedonian believer and companion of Paul in travel and imprisonment.
A plural form referring to more than one aristobulu.
An ark; a chest or vessel, especially Noah's ark or the ark of the covenant according to context.
Arkite; one belonging to the people or clan of Arka, named among the Canaanite families.
The arm; the limb of the body, and by extension strength or power.
Armageddon; the symbolic place-name in Revelation associated with the gathering for the final conflict, linked by name with Megiddo.
armageddon.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Tel Megiddo, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Armed; past tense or past participial form of 'arm'.
Armenia; the northern highland region associated with Ararat and the lands beyond Mesopotamia.
armenia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Urartu.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one armhol.
A plural form referring to more than one army.
Armoni; a son of Saul by Rizpah.
Defensive equipment worn for protection in battle.
An armourbearer is one who carries the armor of a warrior or chief and attends him in battle.
Armoury; a place where weapons or military equipment are stored.
A plural form referring to more than one arm.
An army; an organized host prepared for war.
Arnan; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Arnon; the river gorge marking an important boundary of Moab.
arnon.note: Location: Usually identified with Wadi Mujib.

definitions: Arod; a son of Gad whose descendants formed the Arodite clan.
Arodi; another form tied to Arod, son of Gad, and the clan descending from him.
Arodites; descendants of Arod reckoned among the families of Gad.
Aroer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Aroerite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Aroer in Scripture.
aroerite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Aro’er.

definitions: The older past tense of arise; rose up, came forth, or began.
Arpad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
arpad.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Rifat.

definitions: Arphad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
arphad.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Rifat.

definitions: Arphaxad; a patriarch in the line of Shem after the flood.
Array is order, arrangement, or splendid clothing; to array is to set in order or clothe.
Arrayed; past tense or past participial form of 'array'.
Arrived; past tense or past participial form of 'arriv'.
Arrogancy; pride, haughtiness, or overbearing self-exaltation.
Arrow; a missile shot from a bow, often used literally or figuratively for swift or piercing judgment.
A plural form referring to more than one arrow.
The older second-person singular present form of be.
Artaxerxes; the royal name of Persian kings mentioned in Ezra and Nehemiah.
Artemas; a Christian worker whom Paul considered sending to Crete.
An artificer is a skilled craftsman or workman in art or manufacture.
A plural form referring to more than one artificer.
Artillery; military weapons or engines of war, especially missile weapons in older usage.
A plural form referring to more than one art.
Aruboth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aruboth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Hamam.

definitions: Arumah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
arumah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Urmah.

definitions: Arvad; the Phoenician island city whose mariners and warriors appear in the prophets.
Arvadite; one belonging to Arvad, named among the Canaanite families.
Arza; the steward of Elah king of Israel, in whose house Elah was slain.
As; in the manner that; while; because; according as the context requires.
Asa; especially the king of Judah who sought the LORD, removed idols, and strengthened the kingdom.
Asahel; the swift-footed brother of Joab and Abishai, slain by Abner.
Asahiah; a servant of king Josiah sent in the delegation that enquired of Huldah the prophetess.
Asaiah; the Levite associated with bringing up the ark in David's day, and also the name of other men in Scripture.
Asaph; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the chief singer and psalmist connected with temple worship.
Asareel; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Asarelah; one of the sons of Asaph appointed in the courses of temple musicians, also written Jesharelah.
Ascend; to go up, rise, or move upward.
Ascended; past tense or past participial form of 'ascend'.
Ascendeth; older third-person singular form of 'ascend'.
Ascending; present participial form of 'ascend', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ascent; a rising upward, an upward approach, or a going up.
To ascribe is to attribute, reckon, or give over in acknowledgment.
Ascribed; past tense or past participial form of 'ascribe'.
Asenath; the Egyptian wife of Joseph and mother of Manasseh and Ephraim.
Aser; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Beriah.
Ash is the powdery remainder of what has been burned; ashes often signify mourning, ruin, or humiliation.
Ashamed; brought to shame, confounded, or feeling shame.
Ashan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ashan.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Asan.

definitions: Ashbea; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ashbea.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Beit Mirsim.

definitions: Ashbel; a son of Benjamin from whom the Ashbelites were reckoned.
Ashbelites; descendants of Ashbel reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Ashchenaz; another spelling of Ashkenaz, son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth.
Ashdod; a principal Philistine city, associated with the worship of Dagon and the conflict with Israel.
The name may also represent the surrounding district by extension.
ashdod.note: Location: On the southern coastal plain of Philistia, between Joppa and Gaza.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one ashdodit.
ashdodites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Ashdod.

definitions: Ashdoth-Pisgah; the slopes or descents of Pisgah in the Moabite highlands.
ashdoth-pisgah.note: Location: On the Pisgah range east of the Dead Sea in the Moabite highlands, commonly associated with the Nebo region.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one ashdothit.
ashdothites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Ashdod.

definitions: Asher; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Beriah.
Asherites; members of the tribe of Asher, and in some contexts its clan divisions.
A plural form referring to more than one ash.
Ashima; a foreign deity worshipped by the men of Hamath.
Ashkelon; one of the chief Philistine cities upon the coast.
It appears in Scripture as part of Philistine strength and later prophetic judgment.
ashkelon.note: Location: On the southern Mediterranean coast of Philistia, north of Gaza.

definitions: Ashkenaz; a son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth in the table of nations.
Ashnah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Ashpenaz; the master of the eunuchs in Babylon who had charge of Daniel and his companions.
Ashriel; a Manassite ancestor named in the genealogies.
Ashtaroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ashtaroth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ashtara.

definitions: Ashterathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Ashterath in Scripture.
ashterathite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ashtara.

definitions: Ashteroth; a heathen goddess, often associated with the fertility cults of the Canaanites.
Ashtoreth; older third-person singular form of 'ashtors'.
Ashur; a descendant of Judah and the father associated with Tekoa in the genealogies.
Ashurites; a people or territorial group named in the days of David, probably connected with the northern or eastern lands bearing the name Ashur.
Ashvath; a descendant in the line of Ephraim named in the genealogies.
Asia; in the New Testament, the Roman province in western Asia Minor rather than the whole continent in the modern sense.
Aside; to one side, apart, or away from the direct course.
Asiel; a Simeonite name in the genealogies.
Requested, inquired, or demanded.
Askelon; one of the chief Philistine cities on the Mediterranean coast.
askelon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Ashkelon.

definitions: Askest; older second-person singular form of 'ask', used with 'thou'.
Asks; requests; inquires.
Asking; present participial form of 'ask', denoting ongoing action or process.
Asleep; in natural sleep, or figuratively at rest in death or spiritual dullness according to context.
Asmuch; as much, to the same degree, or inasmuch according to the sentence.
Asnah; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants after the exile.
Asnappar; a great and noble ruler named in Ezra, commonly identified with an Assyrian imperial figure involved in deportations and resettlements.
An asp is a venomous serpent.
Aspatha; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
A plural form referring to more than one asp.
Asriel; a son or descendant of Manasseh from whom the Asrielites were reckoned.
Asrielites; descendants of Asriel reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
An ass; a donkey.
An assault is a violent attack; to assault is to attack violently.
Assaulted; past tense or past participial form of 'assault'.
To attempt; to try; to undertake.
Assaying; present participial form of 'assay', denoting ongoing action or process.
To gather together into one place or company.
A plural form referring to more than one assembly.
Assembling; present participial form of 'assemble', denoting ongoing action or process.
Assent; agreement, consent, or concurrence.
As a verb, to agree to or consent.
Assented; past tense or past participial form of 'assent'.
A plural form referring to more than one ass.
Asshur; a son of Shem and ancestor associated with Assyria, and also a name in later genealogical use.
Asshurim; a people or clan named among the descendants of Dedan.
Assigned; past tense or past participial form of 'assign'.
Assir; the name of men in Levitical and royal genealogies.
to help or support; also aid or assistance rendered.
Associate; to join oneself with others, or to connect together.
A plural form referring to more than one asso.
Assur; the ancient Assyrian capital from which the empire took its name.
Confidence, certainty, and settled persuasion.
to make certain, reassure, or convince.
Assured; past tense or past participial form of 'assur'.
In a assured manner or degree.
to lessen, calm, or allay.
Asswaged; past tense or past participial form of 'asswage'.

assyria.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Nineveh.

definitions: Assyrian; one belonging to Assyria or its empire, whether as a person, power, or thing associated with that realm.
Assyrians; the people of Assyria or those belonging to its empire, often appearing as oppressors or conquerors in Scripture.
Astaroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
astaroth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ashtara.

definitions: Astonished, appalled, or struck with overwhelming amazement.
Often carrying a stronger sense than simple surprise.
Astonished; past tense or past participial form of 'astonish'.
Great amazement; overwhelming wonder or dismay.
Astray; out of the right way, wandering, or turned aside.
Astrologer; one who claims to read signs or foretell matters by the stars.
A plural form referring to more than one astrologer.
Asunder; apart, into pieces, or separated from one another.
Asuppim; storehouses, thresholds, or gathering-places attached to the temple area, according to context.
Asyncritus; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
At; in, on, toward, or with respect to a point or occasion.
Atad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
atad.note: Location: Commonly identified with near the mouth of the Jordan River.

definitions: Atarah; a wife of Jerahmeel in the genealogies of Judah.
Ataroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ataroth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Ataruz.

definitions: Ataroth-Adar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ataroth-adar.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Atara.

definitions: Ataroth-Addar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ataroth-addar.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Atara.

definitions: Ate; the past tense of eat.
Ater; the head of a family or house named among those who returned from exile.
Athach; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
athach.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Ater.

definitions: Athaiah; a man of Judah dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Athaliah; a biblical personal name, most notably borne by the queen who usurped the throne of Judah.
A plural form referring to more than one athenian.
A plural form referring to more than one athen.
Athirst; thirsty or desirous as one who thirsts.
Athlai; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
A covering or reconciling work by which offense is dealt with and peace is made.
In Scripture often the sacrificial dealing with sin by which guilt is expiated and fellowship with God is maintained or restored.
A plural form referring to more than one atonement.
Atroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
atroth.note: Location: Often identified with Rujm Ataruz.

definitions: Attai; borne by several biblical men, especially figures in Judah's line and among those who joined David.
To reach, obtain, or come unto by effort or course.
Attalia; the Pamphylian port from which Paul and Barnabas sailed.
Attend; to give heed, wait upon, or be present for service.
attentive presence, service, or waiting upon another.
Attended; past tense or past participial form of 'attend'.
Attending; present participial form of 'attend', denoting ongoing action or process.
Attent; attentive, heedful, or listening with care.
Attentive; watchful, heedful, or giving careful regard.
In a attentive manner or degree.
Attire; clothing, dress, or outward array.
Attired; past tense or past participial form of 'attire'.
a hearing, a formal reception, or those who listen.
to increase or enlarge; also an addition or increase.
Augustus; the Roman emperor Caesar Augustus, under whose decree the nativity account is dated.
Aul; an older form of awl, a pointed tool used to bore through leather or similar material.
Aunt; in older usage, a near female relative, not always limited to the modern sense.
Austere; severe, strict, or hard in manner and demand.
The originator or first cause of a thing.
Lawful power, right to command, or delegated rule.
Also weight and force that demand obedience.
Ava; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ava.note: Location: Often identified with Kafr Aya.

definitions: Availeth; older third-person singular form of 'avails'.
Aven; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aven.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain Shams.

definitions: To vindicate or punish in response to wrong done.
An avenger is one who punishes wrong or exacts vengeance, especially on behalf of another.
Avengeth; older third-person singular form of 'avenge'.
Avenging; present participial form of 'avenge', denoting ongoing action or process.
Averse; turned away in dislike, unwilling, or opposed.
Avim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
avim.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Et Tell.

definitions: Avims; an ancient people of the southwest, later displaced by the Caphtorim.
Avites; inhabitants of Avva or Av, counted among peoples transplanted into Samaria.
Avith; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
avith.note: Location: Often identified with Rujum al Juththa.

definitions: Avoid; to shun, turn away from, or depart out of the way of something.
Avoided; past tense or past participial form of 'avoid'.
Avoiding; present participial form of 'avoid', denoting ongoing action or process.
Avouched; past tense or past participial form of 'avouch'.
Await; to wait for or expect.
To rouse from sleep or inactivity.
Often used figuratively of spiritual stirring or alertness.
Awakest; older second-person singular form of 'awake', used with 'thou'.
Awaking; present participial form of 'awake', denoting ongoing action or process.
Aware; informed, mindful, or conscious of a thing.
From the place in view; gone off; removed.
To away with something is to put it away or remove it; in some places, to endure or bear with it.
Awe is reverent fear, dread, or solemn wonder.
Awoke; the past tense of awake.
An axe is a cutting tool used especially for hewing wood or striking.
A plural form referring to more than one ax.
A plural form referring to more than one axletre.
Azal; a place or valley named in Zechariah's prophecy, probably near Jerusalem though its exact identification is uncertain.
azal.note: Location: Near Jerusalem, often linked with ravines east or southeast of the city, but the exact valley is unknown.

definitions: Azaliah; the father of Shaphan the scribe in the days of Josiah.
Azaniah; the father of Jeshua in post-exilic records.
Azarael; the name of men in the genealogies and Levitical records.
Azareel; a personal name in post-exilic and military genealogical records.
Azariah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially kings, priests, and notable servants of God, and also another name for king Uzziah.
Azaz; a Gadite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Azaziah; the name of men in Levitical and administrative service under David and Hezekiah.
Azbuk; the father of Nehemiah, ruler of part of Beth-zur in the days of rebuilding.
Azekah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
azekah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Azekah.

definitions: Azel; a descendant in the line of Benjamin and Saul.
Azem; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
azem.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Asan.

definitions: Azgad; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Aziel; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including Levite and Benjamite figures.
Aziza; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Azmaveth; older third-person singular form of 'azmavs'.
Azmon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
azmon.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Muweileh.

definitions: Aznoth-Tabor; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
aznoth-tabor.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Govel.

definitions: Azor; a person in the genealogy of Christ according to Matthew.
A plural form referring to more than one azotu.
azotus.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Ashdod.

definitions: Azriel; borne by several biblical men, especially chiefs and officers in Israel.
Azrikam; a palace officer slain when Jerusalem fell, and also the name of other later figures.
Azubah; the name of women in Scripture, including a wife of Caleb and a wife of Asa.
Azur; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including the father of false prophets opposed by Ezekiel and Jeremiah.
Azzah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
azzah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Harube.

definitions: Azzan; the father of Paltiel, prince of Issachar.
Azzur; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, especially the father of Jaazaniah and a leader among returned exiles.
Baal; the title of a false god or lord worshipped among the Canaanites and often opposed in Scripture to the worship of the LORD.
Baal-Berith; a false god worshipped at Shechem, the name meaning lord of the covenant.
Baal-Gad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
baal-gad.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Baal-Hamon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Baal-Hanan; the name of persons mentioned in Scripture, including an Edomite king and one of David's officers.
Baal-Hazor; a height or district with sheep-shearing associated with Absalom's plot.
baal-hazor.note: Location: Commonly placed at or identified with Jebel Asur.

definitions: Baal-Hermon; a height or locality associated with the Hermon range.
baal-hermon.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Baal-Meon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Baal-Peor; the height or locality connected with Peor and the apostasy there.
baal-peor.note: Location: In the Moabite highlands east of the Jordan opposite Jericho, near the Peor range.

definitions: Baal-Perazim; the place where David defeated the Philistines and said the Lord had broken forth upon his enemies.
baal-perazim.note: Location: In the hill country west or northwest of Jerusalem near the Valley of Rephaim.

definitions: Baal-Shalisha; the district from which a man brought firstfruits to Elisha.
baal-shalisha.note: Location: In the hill country north of Jerusalem in the Ephraim-Benjamin border region.

definitions: Baal-Tamar; a place near Gibeah associated with Israel's battle against Benjamin in Judges.
baal-tamar.note: Location: Near Gibeah in Benjamin on the battlefield of Judges 20.

definitions: Baal-Zebub; the false god of Ekron, later used in Scripture as a name associated with satanic power.
Baal-Zephon; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
baal-zephon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Defenneh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Baalah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
baalah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Baalath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
baalath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Maghar.

definitions: Baalath-Beer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
baalath-beer.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Malhata.

definitions: Baale; another name or form connected with Baalah, commonly identified with Kirjath-jearim.
Baali; my lord or my baal, used in contrast with the covenant language God requires from His people.
Baalim; the various Baals or local manifestations of the Canaanite false god Baal.
Baalis; king of the Ammonites in the days of Gedaliah.
Baana; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including one of Solomon's officers.
Baanah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by one of the captains who slew Ish-bosheth.
Baara; a woman named in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Baaseiah; a Levite named in the line associated with temple music.
Baasha; a man named in Scripture, son of Ahijah, father of Elah.

Babylonians; the people of Babylon or those belonging to the Babylonian empire that carried Judah into exile.

definitions: Baca; the valley so named in Psalm 84, perhaps associated with weeping or balsam trees.
Bachrites; descendants of Becher reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
The back; the hinder part of the body or of a thing.
A plural form referring to more than one backbiter.
Backbiteth; older third-person singular form of 'backbite'.
Speaking against another behind his back; slanderous detraction.
A plural form referring to more than one backbiting.
Backbone; the spine or back-bone.
A plural form referring to more than one back.
Backside; the hinder part, rear part, or back.
Backslider; one who turns back from former faithfulness or obedience.
Backsliding; present participial form of 'backslide', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one backsliding.
Backward; toward the back, behind, or turned away from what is before.
It may also mean reluctant or slow according to context.
Bad; evil, harmful, unsound, or of poor quality.
Bade; the past tense of bid, meaning commanded, invited, or told.
Badest; older second-person singular form of 'bad', used with 'thou'.
A plural form referring to more than one badger.
The state, quality, or condition of being badnes.
A bag is a pouch, sack, or purse for carrying or storing something.
A plural form referring to more than one bag.
Baharumite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Baharum in Scripture.
baharumite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Tumeim, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Bahurim; a village near Jerusalem remembered in the history of David's flight and the cursing of Shimei.
bahurim.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Tumeim.

definitions: Bajith; a Moabite town or temple-site named in Isaiah's burden of Moab.
Bakbakkar; a Levite or porter named in the chronicler's records.
Bakbuk; one of the Nethinims or temple servants named after the exile.
Bakbukiah; a Levite associated with praise and thanksgiving in the post-exilic community.

Bakers; those whose work is to prepare and bake bread or baked food.
Baketh; older third-person singular form of 'bake'.
Balaam; son of Beor, the diviner hired by Balak to curse Israel but constrained by God to bless.
Balac; a man named in Scripture, son of Zippor.
Baladan; the father of Merodach-Baladan, or the dynastic name associated with him.
Balah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
balah.note: Location: Often identified with Tulul el Medbah.

definitions: Balak; a man named in Scripture, son of Zippor.
an even proportion, remainder, or weighing instrument; also to make equal or steady.
A plural form referring to more than one balanc.
A plural form referring to more than one balancing.
Bald; lacking hair on the head, or made bare according to context.
The state, quality, or condition of being baldnes.
Ball; something rounded and rolled together, or a thing tossed like a ball.
Balm is a fragrant resin or healing ointment, often suggesting remedy or soothing.
Bamah; an altar or high place mentioned in Scripture.
Bamoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
bamoth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Quweiqiyeh.

definitions: Bamoth-Baal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
bamoth-baal.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Quweiqiyeh.

definitions: Band; a binding, bond, company, or troop according to context.
Banded; past tense or past participial form of 'band'.
A plural form referring to more than one band.
Bani; a biblical personal name, especially borne by Levites and post-exilic heads of families.
Banished; past tense or past participial form of 'banish'.
expulsion from a place, land, or kingdom.
A bank is the raised side or border of a river, ditch, or similar place.
According to context it may also mean a mound or ridge of earth.
A plural form referring to more than one bank.
A banner is an ensign, standard, or displayed sign of a people or host.
A plural form referring to more than one banner.
A feast or rich entertainment of food and drink.
A plural form referring to more than one banqueting.
the rite of washing with water marking repentance, cleansing, or identification with Christ.
A plural form referring to more than one baptism.
a baptizer; especially John the Baptist.
Baptize; to administer baptism, to dip or wash in the sacramental sense appointed in the New Testament.
Was washed or immersed as a sign of cleansing or admission, according to the ordinance in view.
Baptizest; older second-person singular form of 'baptize', used with 'thou'.
Baptizeth; older third-person singular form of 'baptize'.
Baptizing; present participial form of 'baptize', denoting ongoing action or process.
A bar is a rod, bolt, or piece laid across for fastening or securing.
It may also mean a hindrance, enclosure, or strong fastening according to context.
Bar-jesus; a false prophet and sorcerer mentioned in Acts.
Bar-Jona; son of Jona or Jonah, a patronymic used of Simon Peter.
