Barachel; the Buzite father of Elihu in the book of Job.
Barachias; a man named in Scripture, husband of Jehosheba, father of Zechariah.
Barak; the judge and military leader who, with Deborah, overthrew Sisera and the Canaanite host.
Barbarian; one outside the Greek-speaking civilized world, regarded as foreign in speech and culture.

Barbarous; savage, cruel, or foreign and uncivilized according to the speaker's viewpoint.
Foreigners regarded as outside the speaker's own language or culture; barbarians.
Barbed; past tense or past participial form of 'barb'.
Barber; one who cuts hair or shaves the beard.
Bore; carried; brought forth.
In some places, bare or uncovered, according to context.
Barefoot; without shoes or sandals.
Barest; older second-person singular form of 'bar', used with 'thou'.
Barhumite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Barhum in Scripture.

definitions: Bariah; a descendant of Shemaiah in the genealogies.
Bark; the outer covering of a tree.
Barked; past tense or past participial form of 'bark'.
Barkos; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Barley is a grain used for bread, food, and harvest provision, commonly reckoned among humbler staples than wheat.
A barn is a storehouse for grain and produce.
Barnabas; a Levite of Cyprus and companion of Paul, noted for encouragement and generous service in the early church.
A barnfloor is the floor of a barn or a place where stored grain is handled.
A plural form referring to more than one barn.
A barrel is a vessel or jar for holding meal, liquid, or other provision.
A plural form referring to more than one barrel.
Barren; unfruitful, unproductive, or unable to bear offspring according to context.
The state, quality, or condition of being barrennes.
A plural form referring to more than one bar.
Barsabas; a surname borne by New Testament believers, especially Judas Barsabas and Joseph called Barsabas, surnamed Justus.
Bartholomew; one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.
Bartimaeus; the blind beggar near Jericho whom Jesus healed.
Baruch; a biblical personal name, especially borne by Jeremiah's faithful scribe and companion.
Barzillai; the Gileadite who sustained David in his distress and was honoured by him afterward, and also others bearing the same name.
Base; low, mean, ignoble, or of low degree.

Basest; older second-person singular form of 'base', used with 'thou'.
Bashan; the rich region east of Jordan, famed for cattle, oaks, and strong cities.
In poetry and prophecy it often suggests strength, richness, and height.

definitions: Bashan-Havoth-Jair; the district of Jair's towns in Bashan east of Jordan.
bashan-havoth-jair.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Qamm.

definitions: Bashemath; the name of women in the family narratives of Esau and Ishmael's line.

A plural form referring to more than one basket.
Basmath; the name of women in Genesis, connected with Esau's wives and the Ishmaelite line.
A bason is a basin or broad vessel used for holding liquid.
A plural form referring to more than one bason.
A bastard is one born out of lawful marriage.
A plural form referring to more than one bastard.
A bat is the flying creature so called, counted among unclean creatures in the law.
A bath is a Hebrew measure for liquids; in other contexts it may mean washing or a place for bathing according to usage.

definitions: Bath-Sheba; the wife of Uriah who afterward became the wife of David and mother of Solomon.
Bath-Shua; a woman named in Scripture, and in one place the wife of Judah.
Bathe; to wash the body or soak in water.
Bathed; past tense or past participial form of 'bath'.
A plural form referring to more than one bath.
A plural form referring to more than one bat.
Battered; past tense or past participial form of 'batter'.
Battering; present participial form of 'batter', denoting ongoing action or process.
Battle; a fight or engagement in war.
a protective wall or parapet built along the top of a structure.
A plural form referring to more than one battlement.
A plural form referring to more than one battl.
Bavai; a ruler of half the district of Keilah in the days of Nehemiah.
Bay; of a reddish-brown color, especially of horses.
Bazlith; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants who returned from the exile; the name also appears in the form Bazluth.
Bazluth; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists; it also appears in the form Bazlith.
Bdellium; a precious gum or resin, or possibly a pearl-like substance according to interpretation.
To be; to exist, remain, occur, or stand in a given state according to context.
Beacon; a signal-post, ensign, or conspicuous marker set up to be seen from afar.
Bealiah; a Benjamite warrior who came to David at Ziklag.
Bealoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
bealoth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Beer Yeroham.

definitions: A beam is a great piece of timber used in building.
In Scripture it may also denote anything long and weighty, and in figure something grossly obvious.
A plural form referring to more than one beam.
A plural form referring to more than one bean.
A beard is the hair growing upon a man's chin and cheeks.
A plural form referring to more than one beard.
A plural form referring to more than one bearer.
Bearest; thou bearest; you bear, carry, endure, or bring forth.
The precise sense must be determined from the passage.
Bears; carries; sustains; endures.
It may also mean brings forth, produces, or conveys testimony according to context.
Bearing; present participial form of 'bear', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one bear.
A beast; an animal, especially one considered as a creature of the field or under man's use.
Beasts; animals, especially those viewed as brute creatures.
Beat; to strike repeatedly, smite down, or drive by blows.
Beaten; struck, worked out by blows, or worn smooth by use according to context.
Beatest; older second-person singular form of 'beat', used with 'thou'.
Beateth; older third-person singular form of 'beat'.
Beating; present participial form of 'beat', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one beauty.
Fair, comely, or lovely to behold.
It may refer to outward beauty, but sometimes to moral fitness or pleasant excellence.
Beautify; to make fair, adorn, or render beautiful.
Beauty; fairness, comeliness, or that which is pleasing in form and appearance.
Bebai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Came to be; was made or turned into a certain condition.
Becamest; older second-person singular form of 'become', used with 'thou'.
For the reason that; on account of this.
Becher; a son of Benjamin from whom a family line was reckoned.
Bechorath; an ancestor in Saul's Benjamite line.
Beckoned; past tense or past participial form of 'beckon'.
Beckoning; present participial form of 'beckon', denoting ongoing action or process.
Is fitting, suitable, or proper for.
A bed; a place of lying down, and by extension a channel or layer according to context.
Bedad; the father of Hadad king of Edom.
Bedan; a judge-like deliverer named in Samuel, often identified with Barak or another known figure.
A bedchamber is a private chamber for bed and rest, an inner sleeping room.
Bedeiah; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
A plural form referring to more than one bed.
A bedstead is the frame or structure of a bed.
A bee is the stinging insect that makes honey.
In Scripture bees can also figure swarming attack or sharp trouble.
Beeliada; one of David's sons born at Jerusalem, also written Eliada.
Beelzebub; a Philistine idol-name later used in the New Testament as a title for the prince of devils.
The past participle of be.
Beer; the wilderness stopping-place where the princes dug the well sung of in Numbers.
beer.note: Location: In the wilderness east of the Jordan on Israel's approach to Moab, probably between the Arnon and the plateau route.

definitions: Beer-Elim; a well or encampment in the Moabite region named in Isaiah's oracle.
beer-elim.note: Location: In or near Moab east of the Dead Sea, likely connected with the wilderness route toward Arnon.

definitions: Beer-Lahai-Roi; the well associated with Hagar in the wilderness and later with Isaac's dwelling.
beer-lahai-roi.note: Location: In the southern wilderness between Kadesh and Bered, probably in the northern Negev or Sinai fringe.

definitions: Beer-Sheba; the southern landmark city of the land, frequently used in the phrase from Dan even to Beer-sheba.
beer-sheba.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Beer Sheva.

definitions: Beera; a prince of the Reubenites carried away by the king of Assyria.
Beerah; the well or place-name in the wilderness narrative, and also a personal name in genealogical use.
Beeri; the father of Judith wife of Esau, and also the father of the prophet Hosea.
Beeroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beeroth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Burj.

definitions: Beerothite; a person from Beeroth, the Benjamite town associated with men like Naharai and the murderers of Ishbosheth.
beerothite.note: Location: From Beeroth in Benjamin, usually placed north of Jerusalem near the Gibeon area.

definitions: Beerothites; the people or inhabitants of Beeroth in Benjamin.
beerothites.note: Location: From Beeroth in Benjamin, usually placed north of Jerusalem near the Gibeon area.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one be.
Beesh-Terah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beesh-terah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ashtara.

definitions: Beetle; an insect named among creeping things, though the exact species is debated.
A plural form referring to more than one beev.
Befall; to happen to, come upon, or overtake.
Befallen; happened, come to pass, or come upon.
Befalleth; older third-person singular form of 'befall'.
Befell; the past tense of befall.
In front of; in the presence of; earlier than in time.
Beforehand; beforehand in time, previously, or in anticipation.
Beforetime; in former time, formerly, or in days past.
Beg; to ask earnestly or to seek alms.
The past tense of begin.
Begettest; older second-person singular form of 'begett', used with 'thou'.
Begetteth; older third-person singular form of 'begetts'.
Beggar; one who asks alms or lives by begging.
In a beggar manner or degree.
Begged; past tense or past participial form of 'beg'.
Begging; present participial form of 'beg', denoting ongoing action or process.
Begin; to start, commence, or enter upon a thing.
Beginnest; older second-person singular form of 'begin', used with 'thou'.
The beginning; the first part, origin, or starting point of a thing.
A plural form referring to more than one beginning.
Brought forth; fathered; generated.
To deceive, cheat, or lead astray by subtlety.
Beguiling; present participial form of 'beguile', denoting ongoing action or process.
Begun; the past participle of begin.
Behalf; side, cause, or interest, especially in the phrase on behalf of.
Behave; to conduct oneself or bear oneself in a certain manner.
Behaved; past tense or past participial form of 'behave'.
Behaveth; older third-person singular form of 'behave'.
Behaviour; conduct, manner of life, or outward deportment.
Beheaded; past tense or past participial form of 'behead'.
Beheld; the past tense and past participle of behold.
Behemoth; the great beast described in Job, often taken as a huge and powerful land animal.
Behind; at the back of, after, or remaining behind according to context.
Look; see; attend carefully to what is before you.
Beholdest; thou beholdest; you behold, look upon, or regard with attention.
Beholdeth; older third-person singular form of 'behold'.
Beholding; present participial form of 'behold', denoting ongoing action or process.
Behoved; past tense or past participial form of 'behov'.
Existence, creaturehood, or the state of being.
Bekah; a Hebrew weight and money measure equal to half a shekel.
Bel; a Babylonian false god, akin to Baal.
Bela; the name of more than one person in Scripture, especially a son of Benjamin and also a king of Edom.
Belah; a name borne by early Edomite and Benjamite ancestors; it corresponds to the form Bela in related records.
Belaites; descendants of Bela reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Belch; to pour out or utter forth abundantly, especially words.
worthlessness or wickedness; also a personification of lawless evil.
Belied; past tense or past participial form of 'beli'.
Belief; faith, persuasion, or the thing believed.
Trusted, received as true, or rested upon with confidence.
Often more than bare assent, carrying the sense of inward reliance.
A plural form referring to more than one believer.
Believest; thou believest; you believe, trust, or receive as true.
Often in Scripture it means not mere opinion, but personal reliance.
Believes; places trust or confidence in.
Often more than bare agreement, carrying the sense of relying upon as true and sure.
Believing; present participial form of 'believe', denoting ongoing action or process.
Bell; a small metal instrument that rings when struck or shaken.
Bellow; to roar or cry out loudly, as an ox or in great anguish.
A plural form referring to more than one bellow.
A plural form referring to more than one bell.
The inward part of the body, especially the stomach or womb, according to context.
Often used figuratively for appetite or bodily craving.
Belong; to be the property of, to pertain to, or to be properly connected with.
Belonged; past tense or past participial form of 'belong'.
Belongest; older second-person singular form of 'belong', used with 'thou'.
Pertains properly to; is due to; is the rightful possession or portion of.
Belonging; present participial form of 'belong', denoting ongoing action or process.
Dearly loved; much loved and prized.
Belshazzar; the Babylonian king in whose feast the handwriting appeared on the wall.
Belteshazzar; a man named in Scripture.
Bemoan; to lament, bewail, or make sorrowful complaint over.
Bemoaned; past tense or past participial form of 'bemoan'.
Bemoaning; present participial form of 'bemoan', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ben; a Levite musician or singer named in connection with Davidic worship.
Ben-Ammi; the son born to Lot's younger daughter, regarded as the forefather of the Ammonites.
Ben-Hadad; the royal name of Syrian kings who appear in conflict with Israel.
Ben-Hail; one of the princes sent by Jehoshaphat to teach in the cities of Judah.
Ben-Hanan; an Edomite chief named among the dukes descended from Esau.
Ben-Oni; the name Rachel gave to Benjamin at his birth, meaning son of my sorrow.
Ben-zoheth; older third-person singular form of 'ben zohs'.
Benaiah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by David's valiant captain who later served Solomon.
A plural form referring to more than one bench.
Bend; to bow, curve, incline, or direct toward a point.
Bendeth; older third-person singular form of 'bend'.
Bending; present participial form of 'bend', denoting ongoing action or process.
Bene-Berak; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
bene-berak.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Khayriyya.

definitions: Bene-Jaakan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Below; under; in a lower place or condition than another thing.
A plural form referring to more than one benefactor.
A favor, good turn, or profitable advantage.
kindness, generosity, or goodwill.
Beninu; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Benjamin; the son of Jacob and the tribe descended from him.
Benjamite; a member of the tribe of Benjamin.
Benjamites; the members of the tribe descended from Benjamin.
Beno; a descendant in the line of Merari named in the genealogies.
Bent; curved, inclined, or firmly set toward a purpose.
Beon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beor; the father of Balaam.
Bera; king of Sodom in the days of Abram.
Berachah; a Benjamite warrior who came to David.
Berachiah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the father of Zechariah the prophet.
Beraiah; a Simeonite name in the genealogies.
Berea; the Macedonian city whose Jews were described as more noble for searching the Scriptures.
Bereave; to deprive, especially of children, loved ones, or comfort.
Bereaved; past tense or past participial form of 'bereave'.
Bereaveth; older third-person singular form of 'bereave'.
Berechiah; a biblical personal name, including the father of Zechariah the prophet.
Bered; past tense or past participial form of 'ber'.
Beri; a son of Asher from whom the Beriites were reckoned.
Beriah; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including sons of Ephraim and Asher.
Beriites; descendants of Beriah reckoned among the families of Asher.
Berites; a clan or local people associated with Berothah or Beeroth, mentioned in Old Testament narrative.
Berith; a covenant-related title appearing in the name Baal-berith.
Bernice; the sister of Agrippa before whom Paul made his defence.
Berodach-Baladan; king of Babylon who sent to Hezekiah after his recovery, a form of the name Merodach-Baladan.
Berothah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Berothai; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
berothai.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Bourdai.

definitions: Berothite; a person from Beeroth or Beroth, used of inhabitants of that Benjamite town.
berothite.note: Location: Usually linked with Beeroth in Benjamin north of Jerusalem, near the Gibeon district.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one berry.
Beryl; a precious stone named among the jewels of Scripture.
Besai; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
To entreat earnestly; to beg or plead with urgency.
Beseeching; present participial form of 'beseech', denoting ongoing action or process.
Beset; to surround, press upon, or closely hem in.
Beside; at the side of; besides; in comparison with, according to context.
A preposition marking relation in place, time, manner, cause, or degree according to context.
Besiege; to shut up a city or place with armed forces in order to take it.
Besieged; past tense or past participial form of 'besiege'.
Besodeiah; the father of Meshullam in the rebuilding records of Nehemiah.
Besom; a broom used for sweeping.
Besor; the brook near which David and his men halted, leaving the faint with the baggage.
besor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Nahal HaBesor.

definitions: Most excellent, choice, or chief among several.
Bestead; to put into a certain condition, especially one of distress or difficulty.
Bestir; to stir oneself, rouse oneself, or act with effort.
Bestow; to give, confer, or place upon.
Bestowed; past tense or past participial form of 'bestow'.
Betah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
betah.note: Location: Often identified with between Baalbek and Kadesh.

definitions: Beten; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beten.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Ibtin.

definitions: Beth-Anath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-anath.note: Location: Often identified with Safad el Battikh.

definitions: Beth-Anoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-anoth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Beit Anun.

definitions: Beth-Arabah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-arabah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain el Gharabeh.

definitions: Beth-Aram; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-aram.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Iktanu.

definitions: Beth-Arbel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-arbel.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Irbid.

definitions: Beth-Aven; a place near Bethel, sometimes used as a contemptuous prophetic name for idolatrous Bethel.
beth-aven.note: Location: Near Bethel in the central hill country, commonly associated with the Beitin area.

definitions: Beth-azmaveth; older third-person singular form of 'beth azmavs'.
beth-azmaveth.note: Location: Often identified with Hizma, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Beth-Baal-Meon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beth-Barah; a ford or crossing-place mentioned in Scripture.
Beth-Birei; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-birei.note: Location: Often identified with Jebel el Biri.

definitions: Beth-Car; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-car.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Beit Ur al Tahta.

definitions: Beth-Dagon; a town named after Dagon in the tribal lists of Judah and Asher.
beth-dagon.note: Location: The name is used for more than one site, including lowland Judah and the territory of Asher.

definitions: Beth-Diblathaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-diblathaim.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Libb.

definitions: Beth-el; a notable place in the land of Israel, remembered from the history of Jacob and later worship in the northern kingdom.
The name means house of God and may carry both historical and religious associations in context.
beth-el.note: Location: North of Jerusalem in the hill country, often identified with Beitin though the site remains debated.

definitions: Beth-Elite; an inhabitant of Bethel.
Beth-Emek; a town in the inheritance of Asher.
beth-emek.note: Location: In the northern territory of Asher east of Acco, commonly linked with the Emek area.

definitions: Beth-Ezel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-ezel.note: Location: Often identified with Deir al Asal al Fauqa.

definitions: Beth-Gader; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-gader.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Beth-Gamul; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beth-Haccerem; a place near Jerusalem used as a signal point in Jeremiah's warning of invasion.
beth-haccerem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ramat Rahel.

definitions: Beth-Haran; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-haran.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Iktanu.

definitions: Beth-Hogla; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-hogla.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Hajla.

definitions: Beth-Hoglah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-hoglah.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Hajla.

definitions: Beth-Horon; the pass and adjoining towns controlling an important ascent from the coastal plain into the hill country.
beth-horon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Beit Ur al Tahta.

definitions: Beth-Jeshimoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-jeshimoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Azeimeh.

definitions: Beth-Jesimoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-jesimoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Azeimeh.

definitions: Beth-Lebaoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-lebaoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Jebel el Biri.

definitions: Beth-Lehem; Bethlehem, especially the city of David and the birthplace of Jesus Christ.
Beth-Lehem-Judah; Bethlehem in Judah, distinguished especially as the city of David and birthplace of Christ.
Beth-Lehemite; an inhabitant of Bethlehem.
Beth-Maachah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-maachah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Beth-Marcaboth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-marcaboth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tatrit.

definitions: Beth-Meon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beth-Nimrah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-nimrah.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Bleibel.

definitions: Beth-Palet; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-palet.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Saqati.

definitions: Beth-Pazzez; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beth-Peor; a place in the Moabite region east of Jordan associated with Peor.
beth-peor.note: Location: East of the Jordan in the Moabite highlands opposite Jericho, near Peor and Beth-jeshimoth.

definitions: Beth-Phelet; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-phelet.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Saqati.

definitions: Beth-Rapha; a descendant named in the genealogies of Judah.
Beth-Rehob; a border district or locality in the far north associated with Aramean territory.
beth-rehob.note: Location: In the far north near the entrance to the Beqaa valley between Lebanon and Hermon, on the approach toward Hamath.

definitions: Beth-Shan; the city where the Philistines fastened the bodies of Saul and his sons after Gilboa.
beth-shan.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shean.

definitions: Beth-Shean; the city remembered especially in the account of Saul's death, also written Beth-Shan.
beth-shean.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shean.

definitions: Beth-Shemesh; the city to which the ark returned from the Philistines and where judgment fell for irreverence.
beth-shemesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain Shams.

definitions: Beth-Shemite; an inhabitant of Beth-shemesh.
beth-shemite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shemesh.

definitions: Beth-Shittah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-shittah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Salwim.

definitions: Beth-Tappuah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Beth-Zur; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
beth-zur.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet et Tubeiqa.

definitions: Bethabara; a ford or crossing-place mentioned in Scripture.
Bethany; the village near Jerusalem associated with Mary, Martha, Lazarus, and the Mount of Olives.
bethany.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Eizariya.

definitions: Bether; the hill-country or rugged district named in the Song of Solomon.
bether.note: Location: Probably in the hill country of Judah or Benjamin near Jerusalem, though the exact identification is uncertain.

definitions: Bethesda; a pool or reservoir mentioned in Scripture.
bethesda.note: Location: Usually identified with Pool of Bethesda.

definitions: Bethink; to call to mind, consider, or reflect.
Bethlehem; the town of Judah known as the city of David and the birthplace of the Lord Jesus in the flesh.
It may also be distinguished from Bethlehem in Zebulun according to context.
bethlehem.note: Location: In the hill country south of Jerusalem, commonly identified with Bethlehem in Judah.

definitions: Bethphage; the village near the Mount of Olives from which the colt was brought for Christ's entry into Jerusalem.
bethphage.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tur.

definitions: Bethsaida; a town near the Sea of Galilee, associated with several of the apostles and works of Jesus.
The name is tied to gospel events in the northern ministry.
bethsaida.note: Location: Near the northern end of the sea of Galilee; the exact site is debated, often Al-Araj or et-Tell.

definitions: Bethuel; the father of Rebekah and Laban in the family of Nahor.
Bethul; a southern town in the tribal lists, associated with the Negev settlements.
bethul.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet ar Ras.

definitions: Early; in good time; seasonably.
Betonim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
betonim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Batneh.

definitions: Betray; to deliver up treacherously, or to disclose by one’s actions or words.
Betrayed; past tense or past participial form of 'betray'.
A plural form referring to more than one betrayer.
Betrayest; older second-person singular form of 'betray', used with 'thou'.
Betrayeth; older third-person singular form of 'betray'.
Betroth; to pledge in marriage or espouse.
Betrothed; past tense or past participial form of 'betroth'.
Better; more good, more fitting, or more excellent.
Bettered; past tense or past participial form of 'better'.
In the space or relation separating two or more.
A preposition marking relation in place, time, manner, cause, or degree according to context.
Beulah; a symbolic title meaning married, used in Isaiah for the restored and favoured land.
Bewail; to lament openly or mourn deeply over.
Bewailed; past tense or past participial form of 'bewail'.
Bewaileth; older third-person singular form of 'bewail'.
To beware is to be on guard, take heed, or be watchful against danger or error.
Bewitched; past tense or past participial form of 'bewitch'.
To disclose, reveal, or betray by outward sign or speech.
Gives thee away; reveals what thou art by speech or manner.
A preposition marking relation in place, time, manner, cause, or degree according to context.
Bezai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Bezaleel; the master craftsman filled with the Spirit to make the tabernacle, and also a name among the post-exilic community.
Bezek; a place connected with early conflicts in Judges, especially the defeat of Adoni-bezek.
bezek.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Ibziq.

definitions: Bezer; a son of Zuph in a genealogy of Samuel's line, and also a tribal and place-name elsewhere in Scripture.
Bichri; the father or ancestor associated with Sheba the son of Bichri.
Bid; to command, invite, speak to, or wish according to context.
Bidden; the past participle of bid.
Biddeth; older third-person singular form of 'bid'.
Bidding; present participial form of 'bid', denoting ongoing action or process.
Bidkar; Jehu’s captain, mentioned in the judgment on Joram.
Bier; a frame or couch on which a dead body is carried.
Bigtha; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in the book of Esther.
Bigthan; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who conspired against the king in Esther.
Bigthana; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who conspired against the king, also written Bigthan.
Bigvai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Bildad; the Shuhite friend of Job who spoke in the debates of the book of Job.
Bileam; a town in the northern tribal allotments, also known by a related form in later lists.
bileam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Bilgah; the name of a priestly course and of men associated with that priestly house.
Bilgai; a priest named among the covenant signers after the exile, related in name to the priestly course of Bilgah.
Bilhah; Rachel's handmaid and the mother of Dan and Naphtali.
Bilhan; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including descendants in the lines of Benjamin and Seir.
Bill; a written document, account, or formal writing; in some passages a hooked instrument according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one billow.
Bilshan; a man named among those who returned from exile with Zerubbabel.
Bimhal; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Binds; ties, fastens, or places under bond.
Binding; present participial form of 'bind', denoting ongoing action or process.
Binea; a Benjamite descendant in the line associated with Saul.
Binnui; the name of several post-exilic men, especially Levites and builders in the days of Ezra and Nehemiah.
A fowl; a winged creature.
A plural form referring to more than one bird.
Birsha; king of Gomorrah in the days of Abram.
Birth; the act of being born, or lineage and natural descent according to context.
Birthday; the anniversary of one’s birth.
Birthright; the privilege, rank, or inheritance belonging to the firstborn.
Birzavith; a descendant in the line of Asher.
Bishlam; one of those who wrote against the rebuilding of Jerusalem in the Persian period.
A bishop; an overseer in the church.
The word emphasizes oversight and care rather than worldly rank.
Bishoprick; an office of oversight, charge, or bishop’s place.
Bishops; overseers in the church.
Bit; the piece placed in a horse’s mouth to guide or restrain it.
Bite; to seize or wound with the teeth, or the wound so made according to context.
Biteth; older third-person singular form of 'bits'.
Bithiah; the daughter of Pharaoh who became connected with the house of Judah in the genealogies.
Bithron; a rugged district or ravine-country east of Jordan, mentioned in the account of Abner.
Bithynia; a region of northwestern Asia Minor on the Black Sea, named in Acts and among the destinations of 1 Peter.
A plural form referring to more than one bit.
Bitten; the past participle of bite.
Bitter; sharp to the taste, grievous to the feelings, or severe in spirit according to context.
In a bitter manner or degree.
Bittern; a marsh bird, often taken as a wading bird haunting desolate places.
The state, quality, or condition of being bitternes.
Bizjothjah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Biztha; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Black; dark in color, or made gloomy and mournful according to context.
Blacker; more black or more darkened.
Blackish; somewhat black or dark-colored.
The state, quality, or condition of being blacknes.
Blade; the tender shoot of grain, or the cutting part of an instrument according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one blain.
Blame; fault, reproach, or just ground of accusation.
Blamed; past tense or past participial form of 'blame'.
Without just cause of accusation; free from reproach in conduct.
To speak irreverently against God or sacred things; to rail profanely.
Blasphemer; one who speaks impiously or reproachfully against God, sacred things, or worthy persons.
A plural form referring to more than one blasphemer.
Blasphemest; older second-person singular form of 'blaspheme', used with 'thou'.
Blasphemeth; older third-person singular form of 'blaspheme'.
A plural form referring to more than one blasphemy.
Blaspheming; present participial form of 'blaspheme', denoting ongoing action or process.
Blasphemous; profanely dishonoring God or sacred things.
In a blasphemous manner or degree.
Blast; a blowing wind, a destructive scorching breath, or a sudden stroke according to context.
Blasted; past tense or past participial form of 'blast'.
Blasting; present participial form of 'blast', denoting ongoing action or process.
Blastus; the chamberlain of Herod whose mediation was sought by the people of Tyre and Sidon.
Blaze; a bright flame or burning light.
Bleating; present participial form of 'bleat', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one bleating.
Blemish; a spot, defect, or disqualifying flaw, especially in sacrificial matters.
A plural form referring to more than one blemish.
To speak good upon, invoke favor upon, or pronounce well of.
When used of God, often to bestow good, favor, fruitfulness, or peace.
The state of being blessed; true happiness under divine favor.
Blessest; thou blessest; you bless, speak good upon, or invoke favor upon.
A good bestowed, spoken, or invoked; favor resulting in benefit, peace, or increase.
It may also mean the act of pronouncing blessing.
Blew; the past tense of blow.
Blind; without sight, and also the blind as a class according to context.
Blinded; past tense or past participial form of 'blind'.
Blindeth; older third-person singular form of 'blind'.
Blindfolded; past tense or past participial form of 'blindfold'.
The state, quality, or condition of being blindnes.
The blood of the body as the seat and sign of life.
In Scripture often bound up with life given up, guilt, kinship, cleansing, or sacrificial atonement.
The guilt of bloodshed, especially of blood wrongfully shed.
Bloodthirsty; eager for bloodshed or cruelly violent.
Bloody; stained with blood, characterized by bloodshed, or guilty of it.
Bloomed; past tense or past participial form of 'bloom'.
Blossom; the flower or bloom of a plant.
Blossomed; past tense or past participial form of 'blossom'.
A plural form referring to more than one blossom.
Blot; a stain, reproach, or thing that effaces or mars.
Blotted; past tense or past participial form of 'blot'.
Blotteth; older third-person singular form of 'blot'.
Blotting; present participial form of 'blot', denoting ongoing action or process.
Blow; to breathe, sound, or move by a current of air; also to strike according to context.
Bloweth; older third-person singular form of 'blow'.
Blowing; present participial form of 'blow', denoting ongoing action or process.
Blown; the past participle of blow.

Owed, proper, fitting, or appointed to be rendered.
A plural form referring to more than one du.
A duke; a chief, prince, or head of a clan or tribe.
In the Old Testament the word often answers to an elder tribal ruler rather than to the later European rank.
Dukes; chiefs, princes, or heads of clans or tribes.
Dulcimer; a musical instrument named in Daniel, though its exact form is uncertain.
Dull; slow, blunt, heavy, or lacking quickness and perception.
Dumah; the name of a son of Ishmael, and also of an Edomite line or district associated with his name.
Dumb; unable to speak, or silent according to context.
Dung; excrement, refuse, or filth.
Dungeon; a pit, prison, or deep place of confinement.
Dunghill; a heap of dung or refuse.
A plural form referring to more than one dunghill.
Dura; a district mentioned in Scripture.
dura.note: Location: Often identified with Tulul Dura, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Durable; lasting, enduring, or able to continue.
Dureth; older third-person singular form of 'durs'.
An older past form meaning 'dared.'
Dust; fine earth, powder, or the substance to which man returns in death.
A plural form referring to more than one duty.
Duty; that which is owed, required, or fitting to be done.
Dwarf; a person of unusually small stature, or a stunted creature according to context.
Dwell; to abide, live, or remain in a region, house, or condition.
Dwelled; past tense or past participial form of 'dwell'.
A plural form referring to more than one dweller.
Dwellest; thou dwellest; you abide, live, or remain in a place.
Abiding place, habitation, or remaining in a place.
Dwellingplace; a place of habitation or abode.
A plural form referring to more than one dwellingplac.
A plural form referring to more than one dwelling.
Dyed; past tense or past participial form of 'dy'.
Dying; present participial form of 'dy', denoting ongoing action or process.
Every one separately taken.
Eagle; the great bird of prey so called, often an emblem of swiftness, strength, or desolation.
A plural form referring to more than one eagl.
The ear; the organ of hearing, and by extension attention or obedience.
Eared; past tense or past participial form of 'ear'.
Earing; present participial form of 'ear', denoting ongoing action or process.
Early; at an early time, soon in the day, or beforehand.
A pledge or first part given in assurance that the rest will follow.
A token securing a future full possession.
With sincere force, zeal, and serious intent.
Earneth; older third-person singular form of 'earns'.
Earring; present participial form of 'ear', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one earring.
Ears; the organs of hearing, and by extension attention or understanding according to context.
The earth, land, or ground.
According to context it may mean the whole world, a country, or the soil underfoot.
Earthen; made of earth or clay, and therefore frail or lowly in nature.
In a earth manner or degree.
Earthquake; a shaking of the earth, often associated in Scripture with divine visitation or judgment.
A plural form referring to more than one earthquak.
Earthy; of the earth, earthly in nature, or crude and material.
Ease; rest, freedom from trouble, or comfortable security.
Eased; past tense or past participial form of 'ease'.
Easier; more easy, less difficult.
In a easi manner or degree.
The east; the quarter where the sun rises.
Easter; in Acts 12:4 a rendering referring to the Passover season.
Toward the east.
Easy; not hard, gentle, or light to bear.
Consumed as food; devoured.
Eater; one that eats, or that which devours.
A plural form referring to more than one eater.
Eatest; thou eatest; you eat or partake of food.
Eats; takes food; partakes.
Eating; present participial form of 'eat', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ebal; Mount Ebal near Shechem, and also a personal name in genealogical lists.
Ebed; past tense or past participial form of 'eb'.
Ebed-Melech; the Ethiopian servant of the king who rescued Jeremiah from the dungeon.
Eben-Ezer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
eben-ezer.note: Location: Often identified with Izbet Sartah.

definitions: Eber; the name of men in Scripture, especially the ancestor from whom the Hebrews are traditionally named.
Ebiasaph; a Korhite ancestor in the Levitical line; the name also appears in the form Abiasaph.
Ebony; a dark precious wood.
Ebronah; an encampment of Israel near the head of the Gulf of Aqaba during the wilderness journey.
ebronah.note: Location: Near the northern end of the Gulf of Aqaba in the Arabah on Israel's southern wilderness route.

definitions: Ed; past tense or past participial form of 'e'.
Edar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
edar.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet es Siyar el Ghanam.

definitions: Eden; a name borne in Scripture by a person or house, distinct from the garden of Eden and the region so called.
Eder; the name of men in genealogical records, distinct from the place-name.
Edge; the sharp side of a blade, or the border or brink of a thing.
A plural form referring to more than one edg.
To build up, especially in spiritual strength and understanding.
Building up spiritually; strengthening in faith and godliness.
Edom; the land and nation descended from Esau, and at times Esau himself according to context.
The name often carries the idea of the neighboring people south of Judah.
edom.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Buseira.

definitions: Edomite; a member of the people of Edom, descended from Esau.
Edomites; the people descended from Esau, dwelling in Edom south of the Dead Sea and often standing in tense or hostile relation to Israel.
Edrei; a royal city of Bashan where Og was defeated.
Effect; result, operation, or working power according to context.
Effected; past tense or past participial form of 'effect'.
Powerful in producing the intended effect; working efficiently.
In a effectual manner or degree.
Effeminate; soft, unmanly, or morally corrupt in a way condemned by Scripture.
Egg; the egg of a bird or similar creature.
A plural form referring to more than one egg.
Eglah; one of David's wives and the mother of Ithream.
Eglaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
eglaim.note: Location: Often identified with Rujm el Jilimeh.

definitions: Eglon; king of Moab who oppressed Israel and was slain by Ehud.
Egypt; the land and kingdom so called, often standing for its people or power.
egypt.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ain Shams.

definitions: Egyptian; a person of Egypt or something belonging to that land and its people.
Egyptians; the people of Egypt, often appearing as Israel's oppressors, neighbours, or allies depending on the passage.
Ehi; a Benjamite named in the tribal genealogies; the same figure is elsewhere given in the form Aharah.
Ehud; the judge who delivered Israel by slaying Eglon king of Moab.
The number eight.
The number eighteen.
Next after the seventeenth.
The one next after the seventh; occupying the eighth place.
Eightieth; older third-person singular form of 'eighty'.
The number eighty.
Either; one of two, or each of two according to context.
Eker; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ekron; one of the chief Philistine cities, associated with the idol Baal-zebub.
ekron.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Miqne.

definitions: Ekronites; inhabitants of Ekron, one of the Philistine cities.
El-Beth-El; an altar or high place mentioned in Scripture.
el-beth-el.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with in Beitin.

definitions: El-Elohe-Israel; a memorial or compound sacred name used in Scripture.
El-Paran; a place at the edge of the wilderness of Paran, named in the campaign of Chedorlaomer and his allies.
el-paran.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Aqaba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Eladah; a descendant of Ephraim in the tribal genealogies.
Elah; the king of Israel slain by Zimri, and also the name of other biblical men.
Elam; the name of a man or family line in Scripture, distinct from the region of Elam.
A plural form referring to more than one elamit.
elamites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Shush.

definitions: Elasah; the name of men in Scripture, including one sent by king Zedekiah to Babylon.
Elath; the port or settlement on the Gulf of Aqaba used for trade and access to the Red Sea.
elath.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Aqaba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Eldaah; a son of Midian in the genealogies of Abraham's descendants.
Eldad; one of the elders upon whom the Spirit rested in the wilderness.
An elder; an older man, or one holding dignity, rule, and recognized standing among a people or in the church.
According to context the term may refer to age, office, or representative authority.
Elders; older men or those holding rule and dignity in a people.
Oldest; first in age among children or others compared.
Elead; a descendant of Ephraim slain by the men of Gath.
Elealeh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Eleasah; the name of men in Scripture, including a descendant of Saul and one sent by Jeremiah with a letter to Babylon.
Eleazar; especially the son of Aaron, though the name is borne by others according to context.
Chosen out; selected.
Often chosen by God to privilege, office, or saving mercy according to context.
Elected; past tense or past participial form of 'elect'.
the act of choosing, or the state of being chosen.
A plural form referring to more than one element.
Eleph; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
eleph.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Salah.

definitions: The number eleven.
Next after the tenth.
Elhanan; the name of warriors in David's time, including the Bethlehemite noted in the giant traditions.
Eli; a man named in Scripture, father of Hophni.
Eliab; David's eldest brother, and also the name of other notable men of tribal and Levitical lines.
Eliada; one of David's sons, and also the name of a valiant captain under Jehoshaphat.
Eliadah; borne by several biblical men, including a son of David and one of David's mighty men.
Eliah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Eliahba; one of David's mighty men.
Eliakim; son of Hilkiah in Hezekiah's court, and also the king of Judah later called Jehoiakim, with other men of the same name in Scripture.
Eliam; a mighty man of David, also associated with Bath-sheba's family.
Elias; the Greek form of Elijah, the prophet of the LORD.
Eliasaph; the name of tribal princes in the wilderness, including leaders of Gad and the Gershonites.
Eliashib; the high priest in the days of Nehemiah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Eliathah; a Levite or musician named in the chronicler's records.
Elidad; the son of Chislon and prince of Benjamin appointed in the division of the land.
Eliel; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and mighty men in David's time.
Elienai; the name of men in the genealogies, especially in the line of David and Benjamin.
Eliezer; Abraham's servant of Damascus and also the name of a son of Moses and other biblical men.
Elihoenai; a post-exilic or genealogical name, especially in the lines of David and Benjamin.
Elihoreph; one of Solomon's scribes.
Elihu; the young speaker in the book of Job, and also several other men of that name in genealogical records.
Elijah; the prophet of that name.
Elika; one of David's mighty men.
Elim; the oasis in the wilderness with springs and palm trees where Israel encamped after crossing the Red Sea.
elim.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Gharandal.

definitions: Elimelech; the husband of Naomi and father of Mahlon and Chilion in the book of Ruth.
Elioenai; borne by several biblical men, especially in Davidic and post-exilic genealogies.
Eliphal; one of David's mighty men.
Eliphalet; the name of Davidic sons and other men in Scripture, elsewhere also written Eliphelet.
Eliphaz; the name of men in Scripture, especially Esau's son and Eliphaz the Temanite in the book of Job.
Elipheleh; a Levite or singer named in David's arrangements for the ark.
Eliphelet; borne by several biblical men, including sons of David and one of David's mighty men.
Elisabeth; older third-person singular form of 'elisabs'.
Eliseus; the Greek form of Elisha the prophet.
Elisha; son of Shaphat and successor of Elijah in the prophetic office.
Elishah; a son of Javan from whom maritime peoples or coasts were associated in early tradition.
Elishama; a prince of Ephraim in the wilderness, and also the name of other men in royal or tribal lines.
Elishaphat; one of the captains who supported Jehoiada in setting Joash on the throne.
Elisheba; the wife of Aaron and mother of his sons.
Elishua; one of David's sons born to him at Jerusalem.
Eliud; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Matthew.
Elizaphan; the name of men in Scripture, especially the son of Uzziel who was chief over the Kohathites.
Elizur; the son of Shedeur and prince of the tribe of Reuben in the wilderness.
Elkanah; the father of Samuel, and also the name of other biblical men.
Elkoshite; a person from Elkosh, used especially of Nahum the prophet.
elkoshite.note: Location: Elkosh is uncertain; proposals range from Judah to Mesopotamia, so the precise homeland is not known.

definitions: Ellasar; the kingdom named in the time of the eastern confederate kings in Genesis.
ellasar.note: Location: In Mesopotamia, often linked with southern Babylonian or Assyrian territory, though the exact identification is uncertain.

definitions: Elmodam; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
A plural form referring to more than one elm.
Elnaam; the father of two of David's mighty men.
Elnathan; an officer of king Jehoiakim, and also the name of later post-exilic figures.
Eloi; an Aramaic form meaning my God, used by the Lord on the cross.
Elon; the name of men in Scripture, especially the judge of Israel from Zebulun and a Hittite father-in-law of Esau.
Elon-Beth-Hanan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
elon-beth-hanan.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Wadi Illin.

definitions: Elonites; descendants of Elon reckoned among the families of Zebulun.
Eloquent; fluent, persuasive, or powerful in speech.
Eloth; the Red Sea port in the far south; the name also appears in the form Elath.
eloth.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Aqaba, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Elpaal; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Elpalet; a post-exilic descendant in the line of David, related in form to the name Eliphelet.
Else; besides, otherwise, or in addition.
Eltekeh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
eltekeh.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Shalaf.

definitions: Eltekon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
eltekon.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet ed Deir.

definitions: Eltolad; a southern town allotted to Judah and later associated with Simeon.
eltolad.note: Location: In the southern Negev near Beersheba.

definitions: Elul; the sixth month of the Hebrew civil year.
Eluzai; one of the warriors who came to David at Ziklag.
Elymas; the sorcerer and false prophet on Cyprus, also called Bar-jesus.
Elzabad; the name of men in Scripture, including Benjamite and Gadite warriors who joined David.
Elzaphan; the son of Uzziel set over the Kohathites, also written Elizaphan.
Embalm; to prepare a dead body with spices or preservatives.
Embalmed; past tense or past participial form of 'embalm'.
Emboldened; past tense or past participial form of 'embolden'.
Emboldeneth; older third-person singular form of 'emboldens'.
Embrace; to take in the arms, receive gladly, or accept according to context.
Embraced; past tense or past participial form of 'embrace'.
Embracing; present participial form of 'embrace', denoting ongoing action or process.
Embroider; to ornament cloth with needlework.
Embroiderer; one skilled in ornamental needlework.
Emerald; a precious green stone.
A plural form referring to more than one emerald.
Emerods; painful swellings or tumors in the Philistine plague narratives, likely severe boils or hemorrhoidal swellings.
Emims; an ancient giant-like people formerly dwelling in Moab.
Eminent; high, exalted, notable, or standing above others.
Emmanuel; a prophetic name meaning God with us, applied in the New Testament to Christ.
A plural form referring to more than one emmau.
emmaus.note: Location: Often identified with Qalunya, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Emmor; a man named in Scripture, father of Shechem.
Empire; rule or dominion over many lands or peoples.
Employ; to use, occupy, or engage in service.
Employed; past tense or past participial form of 'employ'.
work, occupation, or use.
Emptied; past tense or past participial form of 'empty'.
A plural form referring to more than one emptier.
The state, quality, or condition of being emptines.
Empty; void, unfilled, or lacking substance or effect according to context.
jealous rivalry or eager imitation.
A plural form referring to more than one emulation.
En-Eglaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
En-Gannim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
en-gannim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Umm Jina.

definitions: En-Gedi; the oasis stronghold by the Dead Sea where David hid from Saul.
en-gedi.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ein Gedi.

definitions: En-Haddah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
En-Hakkore; the spring or place opened for Samson after his great thirst.
en-hakkore.note: Location: At Lehi in the Shephelah of Judah, in the area of Samson's Philistine victory, though the exact spring is uncertain.

definitions: En-Hazor; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
En-Mishpat; another name of Kadesh in the wilderness narrative.
en-mishpat.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: En-Rimmon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
en-rimmon.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Halif.

definitions: En-Rogel; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
en-rogel.note: Location: Commonly identified with Bir Ayub.

definitions: En-Shemesh; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
en-shemesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ein Haud.

definitions: En-Tappuah; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
en-tappuah.note: Location: In the hill country on the border of Ephraim and Manasseh, probably near the Tappuah district, though the exact spring is uncertain.

definitions: Enabled; past tense or past participial form of 'enabl'.
Enam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
enam.note: Location: Often identified with Nahal Yarmut.

definitions: Enan; the father of Ahira of Naphtali in the wilderness order of the tribes.
To encamp is to pitch tents or take up a camp.
Encamped; past tense or past participial form of 'encamp'.
Encampeth; older third-person singular form of 'encamp'.
Encamping; present participial form of 'encamp', denoting ongoing action or process.
Enchanter; one who practices enchantments or magical arts.
A plural form referring to more than one enchanter.
sorcery, incantation, or spell-like influence.
A plural form referring to more than one enchantment.
Encountered; past tense or past participial form of 'encounter'.
to give heart, confidence, or courage to.
Encouraged; past tense or past participial form of 'encourage'.
The end; the conclusion, limit, result, or purpose of a thing according to context.
to injure or cause loss to.
Endanger; to put in peril or hazard.
Endangered; past tense or past participial form of 'endanger'.
Earnest effort; diligent attempt; striving toward a thing.
Endeavouring; present participial form of 'endeavour', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one endeavour.
Ended; past tense or past participial form of 'end'.
Endeth; older third-person singular form of 'ends'.
Ending; present participial form of 'end', denoting ongoing action or process.
Endless; without end, boundless, or continuing without limit.
Endor; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
endor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet es Safsafa.

definitions: Endow; to furnish with a gift, portion, or endowment.
A plural form referring to more than one end.
Endued; past tense or past participial form of 'endu'.
Endured; past tense or past participial form of 'endur'.
Endures; continues; remains under trial without failing.
Often speaks of lasting steadfastly or abiding through time.
Enduring; present participial form of 'endur', denoting ongoing action or process.
Enemies; those who are hostile or set against another.
An enemy; one who is hostile or opposed.
Enflaming; present participial form of 'enflam', denoting ongoing action or process.
Engaged; past tense or past participial form of 'engag'.
A plural form referring to more than one engin.
Engrafted; past tense or past participial form of 'engraft'.
Engrave; to cut, inscribe, or carve into a surface.
Engraven; carved, graven, or inscribed.
Engraver; one who cuts or carves inscriptions and devices.
A plural form referring to more than one engraving.
Enjoin; to command, charge, or direct with authority.
Enjoined; past tense or past participial form of 'enjoin'.
Enjoy; to possess, partake of, or take delight in.
Enjoyed; past tense or past participial form of 'enjoy'.
To make greater, widen, or set at liberty into a larger room.
increase, expansion, or release from restraint.
Enlargeth; older third-person singular form of 'enlarge'.
Enlarging; present participial form of 'enlarge', denoting ongoing action or process.
Enlighten; to give light to, illuminate, or make to understand.
Enlightened; past tense or past participial form of 'enlighten'.
Enlightening; present participial form of 'enlighten', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hostility, deep opposition, or settled hatred.
Enoch; the patriarch who walked with God and was translated that he should not see death, and also the name of other early figures.
Enos; the son of Seth in the early genealogy of mankind.
Enosh; the form of the name Enos used for the son of Seth, in whose days men began to call upon the name of the LORD.
Enough; sufficient in quantity or degree.
Enquire; to ask, seek information, or inquire of God or others.
Enquired; past tense or past participial form of 'enquire'.
Enquirest; older second-person singular form of 'enquire', used with 'thou'.
Enquiry; inquiry, question, or search for information.
Enrich; to make rich, increase in value, or furnish abundantly.
Enriched; past tense or past participial form of 'enrich'.
Enrichest; older second-person singular form of 'enrich', used with 'thou'.
An example or pattern set before others for imitation or warning.
Ensign; a standard, banner, or signal lifted up for gathering or display.
A plural form referring to more than one ensign.
Ensnared; past tense or past participial form of 'ensnar'.
Ensue; to follow after or come as a result.
To involve, ensnare, or twist into hindering difficulty.
Entangleth; older third-person singular form of 'entangle'.
Went in; came into; took possession or began according to context.
Entereth; older third-person singular form of enter, meaning goes in or comes into.
Entering; the act of going in or the place by which one enters, according to context.
Enterprise; a purposeful undertaking or bold design.
Entertain; to receive, host, or hold in the mind according to context.
Entertained; past tense or past participial form of 'entertain'.
To lure, draw on, or lead by persuasion toward a desired end.
Often toward evil.
Enticing; present participial form of 'entice', denoting ongoing action or process.
Entire; whole, complete, or undivided.
the act or place of entering.
A plural form referring to more than one entranc.
To beseech, plead with, or earnestly ask.
In some contexts, to deal with or treat in a certain manner.
Entreateth; older third-person singular form of 'entreat'.
A plural form referring to more than one entry.
Entry; the act of entering, or a place of entrance.
Envied; past tense or past participial form of 'envy'.
A plural form referring to more than one envy.
Enviest; older second-person singular form of 'envy', used with 'thou'.
Envieth; older third-person singular form of 'envy'.
Envious; full of envy, grudging another's good.
Environ; to surround or compass about.
Painful resentment at another's good; grudging another's advantage.
A plural form referring to more than one envying.
Epaenetus; a beloved Christian greeted by Paul as an early convert unto Christ.
Epaphras; a fellow servant of Paul, especially associated with the church at Colosse.
Epaphroditus; the messenger and fellow labourer sent between Philippi and Paul.
Ephah; a Hebrew dry measure for grain and other goods.
Ephai; a Midianite son of Abraham by Keturah, and also a Judahite name.
Epher; borne by several biblical men, including descendants of Midian, Caleb, and Benjamin.
Ephes-Dammim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ephes-dammim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Damun.

definitions: Ephesian; an inhabitant of Ephesus or something belonging to that city.
Ephesians; the people of Ephesus, and especially the church addressed in Paul's epistle.
Ephesus; the chief city of Asia in the New Testament and an important center of trade, idolatry, and apostolic ministry.
It is especially associated with Paul, the Ephesian church, and the worship of Diana.
ephesus.note: Location: On the western coast of Asia Minor, near modern Selcuk in Turkey.

definitions: Ephlal; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ephod; a sacred garment associated with the priesthood, and in some passages an object used in worship.
Ephphatha; an Aramaic word meaning Be opened.
Ephraim; the son of Joseph, the tribe descended from him, or the northern kingdom represented by that tribe.
Ephraimite; a member of the tribe of Ephraim.
Ephraimites; the members of the tribe descended from Ephraim, often prominent in the northern tribes.
Ephrain; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Ephratah; a personal or clan name associated with Caleb's line, and also a place-name linked with Bethlehem by related form.
Ephrath; a woman in the genealogies of Judah, associated with the line from which Bethlehem Ephratah is named.
Ephrathite; one belonging to Ephrath or Bethlehem, and in some passages one of a family so called.
Ephrathites; people of Ephrath or Bethlehem, and in some contexts members of an Ephrathite line in Judah.
Ephron; the Hittite from whom Abraham bought the field and cave of Machpelah.
A plural form referring to more than one epicurean.
Epistle; a letter, especially one written with doctrinal or pastoral authority.
A plural form referring to more than one epistl.
equal in measure, value, or rank; also to match, compare, or one who is an equal.
sameness in measure, value, or standing.
In a equal manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one equal.
fairness, justice, or what is equitable.
Er; the firstborn son of Judah, slain by the LORD for wickedness, and also a name in the genealogy of Jesus.
Eran; a son of Shuthelah from whom the Eranites were reckoned.
Eranites; descendants of Eran reckoned among the families of Ephraim.
Erastus; the name of New Testament men, including Paul's fellow worker and the chamberlain of Corinth.
Ere; before.
Erech; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Erected; past tense or past participial form of 'erect'.
Eri; a son of Gad from whom the Erites were reckoned.
Erites; descendants of Eri reckoned among the families of Gad.
To wander from the right way; to go astray in judgment or conduct.
Errand; a message, commission, or business sent on.
Erreth; older third-person singular form of 'err'.
Mistake, wandering from truth, or false belief and conduct.
Esaias; the Greek form of Isaiah the prophet.
Esar-Haddon; king of Assyria, remembered in Scripture for conquest and resettlement.
Esau; the elder son of Isaac and Rebekah, brother of Jacob, and ancestor of Edom.
To get free from danger, judgment, or confinement.
Escapeth; older third-person singular form of 'escape'.
Escaping; present participial form of 'escape', denoting ongoing action or process.
Eschew; to shun, avoid, or turn away from.
Eschewed; past tense or past participial form of 'eschew'.
Escheweth; older third-person singular form of 'eschew'.
Esek; the well dug by Isaac's servants and so named because of contention.
esek.note: Location: In the Gerar-to-Beersheba region of the southern Philistine borderlands where Isaac's herdsmen contended for water.

definitions: a Benjamite, son of Saul, also known as Ish-bosheth.
Eshban; a descendant in the line of Seir the Horite.
Eshcol; an Amorite ally of Abram, and also the name associated with the valley of the cluster.

Flocks; companies of sheep or goats.
Flood; a great overflow of waters, especially the deluge in Noah’s day, or a rushing body of water.
A plural form referring to more than one flood.
A floor is the level surface of a house or structure.
In Scripture it may also mean a threshing floor, the prepared place for threshing grain.
A plural form referring to more than one floor.
Flour is meal finely ground for bread and offerings.
Flourish; to grow vigorously, prosper, or display oneself in splendor.
Flourished; past tense or past participial form of 'flourish'.
Flourisheth; older third-person singular form of 'flourish'.
Flourishing; present participial form of 'flourish', denoting ongoing action or process.
Flowed; past tense or past participial form of 'flow'.
Flower; the bloom of a plant, often a figure of beauty and transience.
A plural form referring to more than one flower.
Flows; runs in a stream; issues forth freely.
Flowing; present participial form of 'flow', denoting ongoing action or process.
Flute; a wind instrument of music.
Fluttereth; older third-person singular form of 'flutters'.
Flux; a flowing issue, especially a diseased discharge.
In a f manner or degree.
Flying; present participial form of 'fly', denoting ongoing action or process.
a young ass, horse, or similar animal.
A plural form referring to more than one foal.
Foam; froth or frothy matter on the surface of water or other liquid.
Foameth; older third-person singular form of 'foam'.
Foaming; present participial form of 'foam', denoting ongoing action or process.
Fodder is food laid up for cattle and beasts.
A plural form referring to more than one fo.
Fold; an enclosure for sheep, or a fold and wrapping according to context.
Folden; folded.
Foldeth; older third-person singular form of 'folds'.
Folding; present participial form of 'fold', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one fold.
Folk; people, kindred, or nation.
A plural form referring to more than one folk.
Went after, attended, pursued, or obeyed.
Followedst; an old second-person singular form of follow, used with thou.
A plural form referring to more than one follower.
Follows; comes after; goes in the way of another.
Often also means imitates, obeys, or pursues.
A following; those who go after or attend one; also the act of coming after, according to context.
In a fol manner or degree.
Food; that which is eaten for nourishment.
A fool; one lacking wisdom, sense, or godly fear.
Foolish; lacking wisdom, sense, or spiritual understanding.
In a foolish manner or degree.
Folly, want of wisdom, or that which seems foolish to natural judgment.
A plural form referring to more than one fool.
The foot; the bodily member, and by extension a lower part, step, or infantry according to context.
Footmen are men who go on foot, whether as runners, servants, or soldiers according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one footstep.
Footstool; a stool for the feet, and figuratively a place beneath one’s dominion.
For; because of, in place of, in behalf of, or toward.
Since; because; inasmuch as.
Forbad; an older past tense of forbid.
Forbare; an older past tense of forbear.
To refrain, hold back, or cease from a thing.
Also to bear patiently with another.
Patient restraint; holding back what one has power to do.
Forbeareth; older third-person singular form of 'forbear'.
Forbid; to command against, restrain, or refuse permission.
Forbidden; prohibited or not allowed.
Forbiddeth; older third-person singular form of 'forbid'.
Forbidding; present participial form of 'forbid', denoting ongoing action or process.
Forborn; the past participle of forbear.
Force; strength, violence, power, or armed body according to context.
Forced; past tense or past participial form of 'force'.
A plural form referring to more than one forc.
Forcible; powerful, compelling, or effected by force.
Forcing; present participial form of 'force', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ford; a shallow crossing-place in a river or stream.
A plural form referring to more than one ford.
Forecast; an old second-person singular form of foreca, used with thou.
A plural form referring to more than one forefather.
Forefront; the front rank or foremost part.
The forehead is the front part of the head above the eyes.
It may also signify openness, boldness, or marked identity according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one forehead.
Foreigner; one belonging to another land or people.
A plural form referring to more than one foreigner.
Foreknew; the past tense of foreknow.
Foreknow; to know beforehand.
Foreknowledge; knowledge beforehand, especially God’s prior knowledge and purpose.
Foremost; an old second-person singular form of foremo, used with thou.
Foreordained; past tense or past participial form of 'foreordain'.
Forepart; the front part.
Forerunner; one who goes before to prepare the way.
Foresaw; the past tense of foresee.
Foreseeing; present participial form of 'foresee', denoting ongoing action or process.
Foreseeth; older third-person singular form of 'foreses'.
the forepart or bow of a ship.
Foreskin; the covering removed in circumcision.
A plural form referring to more than one foreskin.
A wooded region; a tract thick with trees.
Foretell; to declare beforehand what is to come.
Foretold; the past tense and past participle of foretell.
Forewarn; to warn beforehand.
Forewarned; past tense or past participial form of 'forewarn'.
Forfeited; past tense or past participial form of 'forfeit'.
Forgat; an older past tense of forget.
Forgavest; older second-person singular form of 'forgav', used with 'thou'.
Forged; past tense or past participial form of 'forg'.
A plural form referring to more than one forger.
Forget; to fail to remember, or to neglect according to context.
Forgetful; apt to forget, neglectful in remembrance.
The state, quality, or condition of being forgetfulnes.
Forgettest; older second-person singular form of 'forget', used with 'thou'.
Forgetteth; older third-person singular form of 'forget'.
Forgetting; present participial form of 'forget', denoting ongoing action or process.
To remit, pardon, or release from debt or offense.
A plural form referring to more than one forgiveness.
Forgives; remits the offense; releases from the debt or guilt of a wrong.
Forgiving; present participial form of 'forgive', denoting ongoing action or process.
Lost remembrance of; failed to keep in mind; neglected.
A plural form referring to more than one fork.
Form; shape, pattern, outward appearance, or prescribed order according to context.
Formed; past tense or past participial form of 'form'.
Former; previous, first, or earlier.
Formeth; older third-person singular form of 'forms'.
A plural form referring to more than one form.
Sexual uncleanness; unlawful sexual commerce.
Fornicator; one guilty of unlawful sexual intercourse.
A plural form referring to more than one fornicator.
Forsaken; left, abandoned, or deserted.
Forsakes; leaves, abandons, or gives up.
Forsaking; present participial form of 'forsak', denoting ongoing action or process.
Forsomuch; forasmuch, inasmuch, or since.
Forsook; the past tense of forsake.
Forsookest; older second-person singular form of 'forsook', used with 'thou'.
Forswear; to renounce by oath, deny on oath, or swear falsely.
Fort; a fortified place or stronghold.
Forward or outward from a place; out into view or action.
Frequently used to mark going out, being brought out, or proceeding onward.
Forthwith; immediately, without delay.
Fortieth; older third-person singular form of 'forty'.
Fortified; past tense or past participial form of 'fortify'.
Fortify; to strengthen with defenses or make strong.
A plural form referring to more than one fortres.
A plural form referring to more than one fortress.
A plural form referring to more than one fort.
Fortunatus; a Christian associated with Stephanas and Achaicus in Paul's correspondence.
The number forty.
Forum; a public place of meeting or market in a Roman town.
Eager, ready, or zealous.
At times over-bold or presumptuous, depending on context.
The state, quality, or condition of being forwardnes.
The past tense of fight; strove or warred.
Foul; filthy, defiling, or offensive.
Fouled; past tense or past participial form of 'foul'.
Fouledst; an old second-person singular form of foul, used with thou.
Came upon, discovered, or was found to be in a certain state.
That which is laid as the base of a building.
Figuratively, the ground, beginning, or settled basis upon which something stands.
Founded; past tense or past participial form of 'found'.
Founder; one who casts metal or lays a foundation according to context.
Foundest; older second-person singular form of 'found', used with 'thou'.
Fountain; a spring, source of water, or figuratively a source of life and blessing.
A plural form referring to more than one fountain.
The number four.
Fourfold; four times as much, or in four parts.
Fourfooted; past tense or past participial form of 'fourfoot'.
Fourscore; eighty.
Foursquare; square on every side, equally proportioned.
The number fourteen.
The one next after the thirteenth; occupying the fourteenth place.
The one next after the third; occupying the fourth place.
Fowl; birds collectively.
Fowler; one who catches birds.
A plural form referring to more than one fowler.
A plural form referring to more than one fowl.
Fox; the crafty wild animal so called.
A plural form referring to more than one fox.
A plural form referring to more than one fragment.
Frail; weak, fragile, or morally or physically feeble.
To form, shape, or fashion.
Also the formed structure or inward constitution of a thing.
Framed; past tense or past participial form of 'frame'.
Frameth; older third-person singular form of 'frame'.
Frankincense; a precious aromatic resin used in worship and anointing.
In a frank manner or degree.
Fraud; deceit, false dealing, or wrongful trickery.
Fray; to frighten, drive away, or wear by rubbing according to context.
Freckled; past tense or past participial form of 'freckl'.
Free; not bound, enslaved, hindered, or under obligation in the way in view.
It may also mean liberal or open according to context.
Freed; past tense or past participial form of 'free'.
liberty or the state of being free.
In a free manner or degree.
Freeman; one who is free, not in bondage.
Freewill; voluntary willingness, especially in offerings freely given.
Freewoman; a woman not in bondage.
Frequent; happening often, common, or repeated.
Fresh; new, recent, or not stale.
Fresher; more fresh or more recent.
Fret; to be vexed, consume away, or wear by rubbing according to context.
Fretted; past tense or past participial form of 'fret'.
Fretteth; older third-person singular form of 'fret'.
Fretting; present participial form of 'fret', denoting ongoing action or process.
Fried; past tense or past participial form of 'fri'.
Friend; one loved, allied, or kindly disposed toward another.
In a friend manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one friend.
the relation, affection, or goodwill of friends.
Fringe; a border, tassel, or hanging edge of a garment.
A plural form referring to more than one fring.
Fro; away in the opposite direction, chiefly in the phrase to and fro.
A plural form referring to more than one frog.
From; out of, away from, or proceeding out of.
Front; the forepart, face, or foremost side.
A plural form referring to more than one frontier.
A plural form referring to more than one frontlet.
Frost; frozen dew or cold rime.
Perverse; willfully contrary; hard to govern or persuade.
In a froward manner or degree.
Perverseness; stubborn contrary temper.
Frozen; made solid by cold.
Fruit; produce, offspring, or result according to context.
Bearing fruit; productive; yielding increase.
Also rich in good works or spiritual increase.
A plural form referring to more than one fruit.
Frustrate; to make void, disappoint, or bring to nothing.
Frustrateth; older third-person singular form of 'frustrate'.
Fryingpan; a pan used for frying or preparing food over fire.
Fuel; material used to feed a fire.
Fugitive; fleeing, wandering, or unstable.
As a noun, one who flees or lives as a wanderer.
A plural form referring to more than one fugitiv.
Fulfil; to complete, accomplish, or fill up fully.
Completed, accomplished, or brought to full effect.
Fulfilling; present participial form of 'fulfill', denoting ongoing action or process.
Full; filled, complete, abundant, or entire.
Fuller; one who cleanses and thickens cloth.
A plural form referring to more than one fuller.
In a ful manner or degree.
Fullness; the state of being full or complete.
Often abundance brought to completion or the whole measure appointed.
Furbish; to polish, especially a weapon.
Furbished; past tense or past participial form of 'furbish'.
Furious; full of rage, violent, or stormy in force.
In a furious manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one furlong.
A furnace is a heated place or structure for smelting, burning, or intense fire.
It often signifies severe trial or consuming judgment by figure.
A plural form referring to more than one furnac.
Furnish; to supply, equip, or provide.
Furnished; past tense or past participial form of 'furnish'.
equipment, furnishings, or household goods.
A furrow is the trench or line made in the earth by the plough.
A plural form referring to more than one furrow.
Further; to promote, advance, or help onward; as an adverb, farther.
advancement, aid, or promotion.
Furthered; past tense or past participial form of 'further'.
Furthermore; moreover, besides, or in addition.
Fury; fierce anger or wrath.
Gaal; the son of Ebed who opposed Abimelech at Shechem.
Gaash; the hill-country area associated with the burial place of Joshua.
gaash.note: Location: In the hill country of Ephraim near Timnath-serah, commonly placed northwest of Jerusalem, though the exact ridge is uncertain.

definitions: Gaba; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gaba.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gabbai; a Benjamite dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Gabbatha; the paved place where Pilate sat in judgment over Jesus.
gabbatha.note: Location: In Jerusalem at the praetorium or governor's judgment place; whether at Herod's palace or the Antonia area remains debated.

definitions: Gabriel; the angelic messenger sent to Daniel, Zacharias, and Mary.
Gad; the son of Jacob, the tribe descended from him, or the prophet so named according to context.
Gadarenes; the inhabitants of the district associated with Gadara in the Gospel account of the demoniac.
