Gaddest; older second-person singular form of 'gad', used with 'thou'.
Gaddi; the father of Susi and a name in tribal records of Manasseh.
Gaddiel; the son of Sodi and spy of the tribe of Zebulun.
Gadi; the father of Menahem king of Israel.
Gadite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gad in Scripture.
Gadites; the members of the tribe descended from Gad, especially settled east of Jordan.
Gaham; a son born to Nahor by Reumah.
Gahar; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Gain; profit, advantage, or increase obtained.
Gained; past tense or past participial form of 'gain'.
A plural form referring to more than one gain.
To speak against, contradict, or oppose by words.
A plural form referring to more than one gainsayer.
Gaius; the name of several New Testament believers, including companions of Paul and the host commended in Romans.
Galal; the name of Levites and singers in the records of temple service.
Galatia; the central Asia Minor region and Roman province whose churches received Paul's Epistle to the Galatians.
Galatians; the people of Galatia, and especially the churches addressed in Paul's epistle.
Galbanum; an aromatic gum used in the holy incense.
Galeed; past tense or past participial form of 'gale'.
galeed.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Galilaean; a person of Galilee or something belonging to that northern region.
Galilaeans; the people of Galilee, sometimes viewed distinctively by speech or region.
Galilee; the northern region of the land of Israel, associated especially with the earthly ministry of Jesus.
In some passages the name is used by relation to the sea of Galilee.
galilee.note: Location: In the north of the land of Israel, west of the upper Jordan and the sea of Galilee.

definitions: Gall; bitterness, poisonous bitterness, or bile according to context.
Gallant; brave, noble, or splendid in appearance.
A plural form referring to more than one gallery.
Gallery; a covered walk, upper passage, or projecting structure.
Galley; a large ship with oars.
Gallim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gallim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Kakul.

definitions: Gallio; the deputy of Achaia before whom Paul was brought at Corinth.
A plural form referring to more than one gallow.
Gamaliel; the respected Pharisee and doctor of the law who counselled moderation regarding the apostles.
A plural form referring to more than one gammadim.
gammadims.note: Location: Often identified with Kamid el Loz, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gamul; the head of one of the priestly courses in David's arrangement.
Gap; a breach, opening, or break in a wall or hedge.
Gaped; past tense or past participial form of 'gap'.
A plural form referring to more than one gap.
A garden is a cultivated place for herbs, fruits, flowers, or pleasant growth.
In Scripture it may also suggest delight, refreshment, or enclosed care.
Gardener; one who tends a garden.
A plural form referring to more than one garden.
Gareb; one of David's mighty men, and elsewhere the name of a hill or place near Jerusalem.
A plural form referring to more than one garland.
Garlick; the pungent bulb used for food.
A piece of clothing; an article of raiment worn upon the body.
Garmite; one belonging to a Judahite family or clan associated with Garm.
A granary or storehouse for grain.
Garnish; to adorn, furnish, or beautify.
Garnished; past tense or past participial form of 'garnish'.
Garrison; a body of soldiers stationed in a fortified place, or the post they occupy.
A plural form referring to more than one garrison.
Gashmu; the Arabian opponent of Nehemiah; the name also appears in the form Geshem.
Gat; an older past tense of get.
Gatam; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
A gate; the entrance of a city, building, or enclosure.
Gates; entrances or openings of cities, courts, or enclosures.
Gath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Gath-Hepher; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gath-hepher.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gat Hefer.

definitions: Gath-Rimmon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gath-rimmon.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Collected together; assembled; brought into one place.
In some contexts also taken in, harvested, or brought to one's people in death.
Gatherer; one who gathers, especially fruit, grapes, or tribute according to context.
Gatherest; older second-person singular form of 'gather', used with 'thou'.
Gathers; collects into one; brings together.
It may also mean harvests, takes in, or draws by degrees.
Gathering; present participial form of 'gather', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one gathering.
The past tense of give.
Died; breathed out the last breath of life.
Gavest; thou gavest; you gave or bestowed.
Showy; fine in outward appearance; splendidly dressed.
Gaza; one of the chief Philistine cities, prominent in the histories of Samson and later conflicts.
gaza.note: Location: On the southwestern coastal plain near the route between Canaan and Egypt.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one gazathit.
gazathites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Harube.

definitions: Gaze; to look steadily or with wonder.
Gazer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gazer.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gezer.

definitions: Gazez; the name of men in the genealogies connected with Caleb's line.
Gazing; present participial form of 'gaze', denoting ongoing action or process.
Gazingstock; one made a spectacle to others.
A plural form referring to more than one gazit.
gazites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Harube.

definitions: Gazzam; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Geba; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
geba.note: Location: Commonly identified with El Jib.

definitions: Gebal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Geber; one of Solomon's officers set over a district of Israel.
Gebim; a place named in Isaiah's picture of the Assyrian advance toward Jerusalem.
gebim.note: Location: Probably a small site north of Jerusalem on the invasion route from the Benjamin plateau, in the same general approach as Nob and Anathoth.

definitions: Gedaliah; the governor set over Judah after Jerusalem fell to Babylon, and also the name of other biblical men.
Gedeon; the Greek form of Gideon, the judge who overthrew Midian.
Geder; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
geder.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Gederah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gederah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gederathite; a person from Gederah or Gederath in the lowland towns of Judah.
gederathite.note: Location: From the Shephelah of Judah, in the lowland towns south or southwest of Jerusalem.

definitions: Gederite; a person from Geder, one of the towns of Judah.
gederite.note: Location: From the southern or lowland district of Judah, though the exact site of Geder is uncertain.

definitions: Gederoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gederoth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gederothaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gederothaim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gedor; a Judahite or Benjamite name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Gehazi; the servant of Elisha who was smitten with leprosy after acting deceitfully in the matter of Naaman.
Geliloth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
geliloth.note: Location: Often identified with Araq ed Deir.

definitions: Gemalli; the father of Ammiel, the spy from the tribe of Dan.
Gemariah; the name of men in Scripture, especially one who heard Jeremiah's roll and one sent to Babylon by Zedekiah.
Gender; to produce, bring forth, or generate.
Gendered; past tense or past participial form of 'gender'.
Gendereth; older third-person singular form of 'gender'.
A plural form referring to more than one genealogy.
Genealogy; a record or tracing of descent and family line.
common, broad, or universal; also a chief commander.
In a general manner or degree.
The people of one age or descent; an age or family line springing from common stock.
Sometimes the race or class of men marked by a certain character.
Gennesaret; the fertile plain or district by the lake of Galilee.
The name may also be connected by relation with the adjoining waters in context.
gennesaret.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with El Ghuweir.

definitions: Gentile; one of the nations outside Israel, or something pertaining to those nations.
Gentiles; the nations outside Israel, especially as distinguished from the Jews.
Gentle; mild, kind, or gracious in manner.
The state, quality, or condition of being gentlenes.
In a gent manner or degree.
Genubath; the son of Hadad the Edomite, reared in Pharaoh's house.
Gera; a Benjamite ancestor from whom lines in Benjamin were reckoned.
A plural form referring to more than one gerah.
Gerar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gerar.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Gergesenes; the inhabitants of the district named in one Gospel form for the country of the demoniacs.
Gerizim; the mount near Shechem from which blessing was pronounced.
gerizim.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Gerizim.

definitions: Gershom; the son of Moses, and also the name of a Levitical family descended from him and of other biblical men.
Gershon; a man named in Scripture, son of Levi, father of Libni.
Gershonite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gershon in Scripture.
Gershonites; the Levitical family descended from Gershon, charged with parts of the tabernacle service.
Geshan; a descendant in the line of Judah named in the genealogical lists.
Geshem; an Arabian opponent of Nehemiah who joined in resisting the rebuilding of Jerusalem.
Geshur; a small Aramean border kingdom linked with Talmai and with Absalom through his mother.
geshur.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Fiq.

definitions: Geshuri; one belonging to Geshur, the small Syrian kingdom associated with Absalom's maternal house.
A plural form referring to more than one geshurit.
geshurites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Fiq.

definitions: Gether; a son of Aram in the early post-Flood genealogies.
Gethsemane; a garden or enclosed cultivated place mentioned in Scripture.
Gets; obtains; gains; begets, according to context.
Getting; present participial form of 'gett', denoting ongoing action or process.
Geuel; the son of Machi and spy for the tribe of Gad, also called Nahbi in the parallel account.
Gezer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gezer.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gezer.

definitions: Gezrites; a people of the south country mentioned in David's wilderness campaigns.
Spirit; breath of life.
In the phrase 'gave up the ghost,' to yield up life.
Giah; a locality or district named in the account of Asahel's pursuit and death.
giah.note: Location: East of Gibeon in Benjamin, on the route of Abner's retreat, though the exact locality is uncertain.

definitions: Giant; a man of extraordinary size or strength.
A plural form referring to more than one giant.
Gibbar; the name attached to a returning family in the post-exilic lists, likely tied to the Gibeonites or to Gibeon.
Gibbethon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gibbethon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Melat.

definitions: Gibea; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gibea.note: Location: Sometimes identified with south of Hebron.

definitions: Gibeah; a Benjaminite town associated with Saul and with the outrage that led to civil war in Judges.
gibeah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gibeath; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
gibeath.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Tel el Ful, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gibeathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gibeath in Scripture.
gibeathite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gibeon; the Hivite city whose inhabitants made a league with Joshua, and also the ancestor from whom the Gibeonites were reckoned in some genealogical contexts.
Gibeonite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gibeon in Scripture.
Gibeonites; the inhabitants of Gibeon, remembered especially for making league with Israel in Joshua's day.
A plural form referring to more than one giblit.
Giddalti; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Giddel; the head of a family of Nethinims or servants of Solomon.
Gideon; the judge of Israel who overthrew the Midianites; in other passages he is called Jerubbaal.
Gideoni; the father of Abidan, prince of Benjamin.
Gidom; a locality named in the aftermath of the war against Benjamin in Judges.
gidom.note: Location: In Benjamin near Gibeah and the routes mentioned in the war against Benjamin, though the exact locality is unknown.

definitions: Gier; an older name for a bird of prey, often identified with a vulture or kite.
Gift; something given freely, whether an offering, bribe, or gracious bestowal according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one gift.
Gihon; the name of a river in Eden and also of the spring or water source associated with Jerusalem.
gihon.note: Location: In Genesis it is one of Eden's rivers, often linked broadly with Cush; in Jerusalem it refers to the spring on the city's eastern side.

definitions: Gilalai; a priest or musician present in the dedication and worship records of Jerusalem.
Gilboa; the mountain range or heights associated especially with the death of Saul and Jonathan.
The name carries a solemn historical association in the books of Samuel.
gilboa.note: Location: A ridge south of the Jezreel Valley, commonly identified with Mount Gilboa in northern Israel.

definitions: Gilead; the hill country east of Jordan, known for balm, pasture, and many important historical events.
gilead.note: Location: East of the Jordan River, in the hill country between Bashan to the north and Moab to the south.

definitions: Gileadite; an inhabitant of Gilead east of the Jordan.
gileadite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell edh Dhahab esh Sherqiyeh.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one gileadit.
gileadites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell edh Dhahab esh Sherqiyeh.

definitions: Gilgal; the early encampment of Israel after crossing Jordan, associated with covenant remembrance and later royal and prophetic events.
gilgal.note: Location: Often identified with Araq ed Deir, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Giloh; the town in Judah from which Ahithophel came.
giloh.note: Location: In the hill country of Judah south of Jerusalem, usually identified in the Hebron region.

definitions: Gilonite; a person from Gilon in the hill country of Judah, such as Ahithophel.
gilonite.note: Location: From Gilon in the hill country of Judah south of Jerusalem, likely in the Hebron region.

definitions: Gimzo; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Gin; a snare, trap, or engine for catching.
Ginath; the father of Tibni, claimant to the throne of Israel.
Ginnetho; the name of a priestly house in the post-exilic records, also written Ginnethon.
Ginnethon; the name of a priestly house, especially one represented among the covenant signers after the exile.
A plural form referring to more than one gin.
To bind about, equip, or prepare by fastening on what is needed.
Girdedst; an old second-person singular form of gird, used with thou.
Girding; present participial form of 'gird', denoting ongoing action or process.
A girdle is a belt, sash, or band worn about the loins.
It may signify readiness, strength, office, or binding close for action.
A plural form referring to more than one girdl.
Girgashite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Girgash in Scripture.
Girgashites; one of the peoples of Canaan named among the older inhabitants of the land.
Girgasite; a member of the Girgashite people, one of the ancient Canaanite nations of the land.
Girl; a young female child or maiden.
A plural form referring to more than one girl.
Gispa; an overseer of the Nethinims in the days of Nehemiah.
Gittah-Hepher; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gittah-hepher.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gat Hefer.

definitions: Gittaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gittaim.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Abu Hamid.

definitions: Gittite; an inhabitant of Gath.
gittite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one gittit.
gittites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Granted, bestowed, delivered, or appointed.
Giver; one who gives.
Givest; thou givest; you give, bestow, or grant.
Gives; bestows; grants.
Giving; present participial form of 'giv', denoting ongoing action or process.
Gizonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Gizon in Scripture.
Glad; pleased, joyful, or cheerful.
In a glad manner or degree.
Joy, cheerfulness, and inward delight.
A plural form referring to more than one glas.
A plural form referring to more than one glass.
Glean; to gather what remains after the main reaping or harvest.
Gleaned; past tense or past participial form of 'glean'.
Gleaning; present participial form of 'glean', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one gleaning.
Glede; a bird of prey, commonly identified with a kite or similar raptor.
Shining brightly; sparkling.
Glitter; to shine with a bright flashing light.
Glittering; present participial form of 'glitter', denoting ongoing action or process.
The state, quality, or condition of being gloomines.
Gloriest; older second-person singular form of 'glory', used with 'thou'.
Glorieth; older third-person singular form of 'glory'.
Glorifieth; older third-person singular form of 'glorify'.
To honor, magnify, and make glorious in esteem or display.
Glorifying; present participial form of 'glorify', denoting ongoing action or process.
Glorious; full of splendor, honor, or excellence.
In a glorious manner or degree.
Honor, splendor, praise, or manifested excellence, especially that which belongs to God.
Glorying; present participial form of 'glory', denoting ongoing action or process.
Glutton; one given to excessive eating.
Gluttonous; given to excessive eating or indulgence.
Gnash; to grind or strike together, especially the teeth.
Gnashed; past tense or past participial form of 'gnash'.
Gnasheth; older third-person singular form of 'gnash'.
Gnashing; present participial form of 'gnash', denoting ongoing action or process.
Gnat; the small stinging or flying insect so called.
Gnaw; to bite or wear away by repeated biting.
Gnawed; past tense or past participial form of 'gnaw'.
Goad; a pointed staff used to drive oxen or other beasts.
A plural form referring to more than one goad.
Goat; the animal so called, used for food, sacrifice, and figurative teaching.
Goath; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
goath.note: Location: Near Jerusalem in Jeremiah's boundary description of the restored city, though the exact hill is uncertain.

definitions: Goats; the animals so called.
A plural form referring to more than one goatskin.
Gob; a battle site in the wars between Israel and the Philistines in David's reign.
gob.note: Location: In the Philistine border region, sometimes linked with the area of Gezer in the western lowlands.

definitions: Goblet; a cup or bowl for drinking.
God; the one true and living God, the Maker and Lord of all things.
A god; a false deity or object of worship, when the context speaks of the gods of the nations.
God; one who bears divine title or authority in a subordinate or figurative sense, in a few passages according to context.
May it never be; certainly not.
God-ward; toward God, in relation to God, or with reference to Him.
A plural form referring to more than one goddes.
Godhead; the divine nature or deity.
Reverent piety; a life shaped by the fear of God and likeness to His holiness.
In a god manner or degree.
Gods; false deities, supposed divine beings, or rulers so called according to context.
Goest; thou goest; you go, depart, or proceed.
Goes; proceeds; departs.
Often used of a person's course, movement, or the advancing of a matter.
Gog; a personal name in genealogy and also the name of the hostile ruler seen in prophecy.
Going; the act of departing, proceeding, or moving onward.
A plural form referring to more than one going.
Golan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
golan.note: Location: Often identified with Saham al Jawlan.

definitions: Gold; the precious metal so called.
Golden; made of gold, colored like gold, or of special worth.
Goldsmith; one who works in gold.
A plural form referring to more than one goldsmith.
Golgotha; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
golgotha.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Church of the Holy Sepulchre, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Goliath; the Philistine giant of Gath slain by David.
Gomer; the name of a son of Japheth and also of Hosea's wife in the prophetic sign-act.
Gomorrah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gomorrah.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Gomorrha; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gomorrha.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Gone; departed, passed away, or proceeded onward.
Good in quality, usefulness, uprightness, or moral worth.
Goodlier; more goodly, more fair, or more stately.
Goodliest; older second-person singular form of 'goodly', used with 'thou'.
The state, quality, or condition of being goodlines.
Fair, handsome, stately, or of pleasing appearance.
Goodman; the master of a house, householder, or husband.
Moral excellence, kindness, and beneficent quality.
A plural form referring to more than one good.
Gopher; the wood named in connection with Noah’s ark; the exact tree is uncertain.
Gore; to pierce or wound with a horn.
Gored; past tense or past participial form of 'gore'.
Gorgeous; splendid, showy, or richly adorned.
In a gorgeous manner or degree.
Goshen; the district in Egypt where Jacob's family settled and multiplied before the Exodus.
goshen.note: Location: Generally placed in the eastern Nile Delta of Egypt, though the exact bounds are uncertain.

definitions: The good tidings, especially the glad message of Christ and salvation in Him.
Got; the past tense and often the past participle of get.
Gotten; the past participle of get.
Gourd; a vine or plant with broad leaves, especially the one prepared over Jonah.
A plural form referring to more than one gourd.
Govern; to rule, direct, or control.
rule, administration, or governing authority.
A plural form referring to more than one government.
A governor; a ruler, overseer, or one set over others in authority.
The term may denote a provincial ruler, household overseer, or one entrusted with civil administration.
Gozan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
gozan.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Halaf.

definitions: Favor freely shown, especially the unmerited favor of God.
Also gracious influence and beauty of manner, according to context.
Full of grace, favor, kindness, and readiness to show mercy.
In a gracious manner or degree.
Graff; an older form of graft, meaning to insert or join a branch.
Graffed; past tense or past participial form of 'graff'.
Grain is corn or seed gathered for food and increase.
Grandmother; the mother of one’s father or mother.
Grant; to give, bestow, or allow.
As a noun, a gift or concession.
Granted; past tense or past participial form of 'grant'.
A grape is the fruit of the vine, used for eating and for making wine.
Grapegatherer; one who gathers grapes at vintage.
A plural form referring to more than one grapegatherer.
A plural form referring to more than one grapegleaning.
A plural form referring to more than one grap.
A plural form referring to more than one gras.
Grasshopper; the leaping insect so called.
A plural form referring to more than one grasshopper.
Grate; a lattice or framework, especially of brass, or to rasp according to context.
Weighty, serious, or dignified.
Not only the burial place.
A plural form referring to more than one gravecloth.
Graved; past tense or past participial form of 'grave'.
Gravel; small stones or pebbles.
Graven; carved, engraved, or made by carving.
A plural form referring to more than one grav.
Graveth; older third-person singular form of 'grave'.
Graving; present participial form of 'grave', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one graving.
Seriousness, dignity, and weightiness of behavior.
Gray; hoary or gray in color, especially of hair with age.
Grayheaded; past tense or past participial form of 'grayhead'.
Grease; fat or oily substance.
Large, mighty, weighty, eminent, or important.
Greater; larger, more important, or higher in degree.
Greatest; chiefest in size, rank, weight, or importance among those compared.
Greatly; much, exceedingly, or in a great degree.
Great extent, dignity, power, or excellence.
A plural form referring to more than one greav.
Grecia; the Greek world or realm of the Greeks.
grecia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Athens.

definitions: Grecians; Greek-speaking people, especially Hellenistic Jews as distinguished from Hebrews in Acts.
Greece; the Greek world or land of the Greeks, especially in the later biblical setting.
In a greedi manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being greedines.
Greedy; eager to get, ravenous, or covetous.
Greek; one belonging to the Greek people or language, and in the New Testament often the Gentile in contrast with the Jew according to context.
Greeks; the Greek people, or more broadly Gentiles of the Hellenistic world depending on context.
Green; fresh in color or growth, not dry.
Greenish; somewhat green in hue.
The state, quality, or condition of being greennes.
Greet; to salute or welcome.
Greeteth; older third-person singular form of 'greet'.
Greeting; present participial form of 'greet', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one greeting.
Grew; the past tense of grow.
Greyhound; a creature named in Proverbs among things of stately going; the exact animal is debated.
Grief; sorrow, pain, or vexation.
A plural form referring to more than one grief.
a cause of complaint, injury, or suffering.
Grieve; to cause sorrow, pain, or distress; also to be sorrowful.
Grieved; past tense or past participial form of 'grieve'.
Grieveth; older third-person singular form of 'grieve'.
Grieving; present participial form of 'grieve', denoting ongoing action or process.
Heavy, painful, burdensome, or severe.
In a grievous manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being grievousnes.
Grind; to crush into powder, or to oppress harshly according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one grinder.
Grinding; present participial form of 'grind', denoting ongoing action or process.
Grisled; past tense or past participial form of 'grisl'.
Groan; to utter a deep mournful sound from pain, burden, or longing.
Groaned; past tense or past participial form of 'groan'.
Groaneth; older third-person singular form of 'groan'.
Groaning; present participial form of 'groan', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one groaning.
Grope; to feel about blindly or uncertainly.
Gropeth; older third-person singular form of 'grope'.
A plural form referring to more than one gros.
Ground; earth, soil, or the basis of a thing according to context.
Grounded; past tense or past participial form of 'ground'.
Grove; in many passages the idolatrous sacred tree, pole, or Asherah set up for false worship.
A plural form referring to more than one grov.
Grows; increases; develops; waxes greater.
Grown; having grown, come to full size, or increased.
Growth; increase, growing, or product that has grown.
To murmur, begrudge, or feel ill will at another's good.
In a grudging manner or degree.
Guard; watch, protection, or a body of men appointed for keeping.
Gudgodah; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
gudgodah.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Khadakhid, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Guest; older second-person singular form of 'gu', used with 'thou'.
Guestchamber; a room prepared for guests or lodging.
A plural form referring to more than one guest.
Guide; one who leads or directs another, or to lead according to context.
Guided; past tense or past participial form of 'guide'.
A plural form referring to more than one guid.
Guiding; present participial form of 'guide', denoting ongoing action or process.
Subtle deceit, craft, or treacherous cunning.
Guilt; liability to blame or punishment, especially for sin or bloodshed.
The state, quality, or condition of being guiltines.
Guiltless; innocent, free from guilt, or not chargeable with wrong.
Guilty; chargeable with offense or liable to condemnation.
Gulf; a great chasm or deep separation.
Guni; the name of men in Scripture, including ancestors in the tribes of Naphtali and Gad.
Gunites; descendants of Guni reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Gur; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
gur.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Khirbet en Najjar, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gur-Baal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Gush; to burst forth and flow suddenly.
Gushed; past tense or past participial form of 'gush'.
Gutter; a channel for water, drain, or watercourse.
A plural form referring to more than one gutter.
Ha; an exclamation of surprise, mockery, or sudden notice.
Haahashtari; a Judahite name in the genealogies of Ashur's house.
Habaiah; the head of a priestly family whose genealogy could not be found after the exile.
Habakkuk; the prophet who pleaded with God concerning judgment and lived by faith.
Habaziniah; the father of Jaazaniah of the house of the Rechabites.
A coat or breastpiece of armor, commonly of mail.
Habitable; fit to be inhabited or lived in.
A habitation; a dwelling place, settled abode, or place of residence.
It may refer to a single dwelling, a settled district, or the place where a people or creature lives.
Habor; a river of Mesopotamia to which captives from Israel were carried.
habor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khabur River.

definitions: Hachaliah; the father of Nehemiah.
Hachilah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hachilah.note: Location: Often identified with Zahrat al Kula.

definitions: Hachmoni; the ancestor or family-name associated with one of David's chief mighty men.
Hachmonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Hachmon in Scripture.
The past tense of have.
Hadad; the name of several Edomite rulers and other persons, and also the name of a Syrian deity in related usage.
Hadadezer; a man named in Scripture, son of Rehob.
Hadadrimmon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hadar; a royal or chief name in Edomite lines; in some passages the form Hadad appears instead.
Hadarezer; a man named in Scripture, son of Rehob.
Hadashah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hadashah.note: Location: Often identified with between Lachish and Gath.

definitions: Hadassah; the Hebrew name of Esther, the Jewish queen of Persia.
Hadattah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hadattah.note: Location: Often identified with Har Hezron.

definitions: Hadid; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hadid.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Haditha.

definitions: Hadlai; an Ephraimite leader who opposed harsh treatment of Judah's captives.
Hadoram; borne by several biblical men, including a son of Joktan and a messenger sent from Tou king of Hamath to David.
Hadrach; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hadrach.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Afis.

definitions: Hadst; an old second-person singular form of have, used with thou.
Haft; the handle of a weapon or tool.
Hagab; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Hagaba; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Hagabah; the name of a family of Nethinims or temple servants; it also appears in the form Hagaba.
Hagar; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Abram, mother of Ishmael.
A plural form referring to more than one hagaren.
Hagarites; a desert people east of Jordan, often named in conflict with Israelite tribes.
Hagerite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Hager in Scripture.
Haggai; the prophet who stirred the returned exiles to resume the rebuilding of the temple.
Haggeri; a family designation borne by one of David's mighty men.
Haggi; a son of Gad from whom the Haggites were reckoned.
Haggiah; a Levite named in the genealogies.
Haggites; descendants of Haggi reckoned among the families of Gad.
Haggith; one of David's wives and the mother of Adonijah.
Hai; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hai.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tell.

definitions: Hail; frozen drops of rain, or a greeting according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one hailston.
Hair is the natural growth upon the head or body.
In Scripture it can also signify beauty, weakness, consecration, or minuteness according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one hair.
Hairy; covered with hair or having much hair.
Hakkatan; the father of Johanan, one of the returning exiles named by Ezra.
Hakkoz; the head of a priestly family or course in Israel.
Hakupha; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Halah; one of the Assyrian districts to which the ten tribes were carried away in exile.
halah.note: Location: In northern Mesopotamia under Assyrian rule, probably in the Habur region east of the upper Euphrates.

definitions: Halak; the mountain marking a southern extent in Joshua's conquests.
halak.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jebel Halaq, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hale; to draw or drag with force.
Half; one of two equal parts.
Halhul; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hali; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hali.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Alil.

definitions: Haling; present participial form of 'hale', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hall; a large room, court, or public chamber.
Hallohesh; ruler of half the district of Jerusalem in the rebuilding days of Nehemiah.
To make holy, set apart, or treat as sacred.
Halohesh; the ruler named in Nehemiah; the name also appears in the form Hallohesh.
To limp; to walk lamely.
In some places, to hesitate or waver between two courses.
Halting; present participial form of 'halt', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ham; a man named in Scripture, son of Noah, father of Canaan.
Haman; the Agagite enemy of the Jews in the book of Esther, overthrown by the providence of God.
Hamath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hamath-Zobah; the northern Aramean district associated with Hamath and Zobah.
hamath-zobah.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Hama, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hamathite; one belonging to Hamath or to the Hamathite branch named among Canaanite peoples.
Hammath; a fenced city of Naphtali, likely remembered for its hot springs.
hammath.note: Location: On the western side of the Sea of Galilee, commonly linked with the hot-springs area south of Tiberias.

definitions: Hammedatha; the father of Haman the Agagite.
Hammelech; a royal designation used in names such as Jerahmeel the king's son.
Hammer; a tool for striking, or figuratively an instrument of breaking power.
A plural form referring to more than one hammer.
Hammoleketh; older third-person singular form of 'hammoleks'.
Hammon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hammon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Hamat Tiberias.

definitions: Hammoth-Dor; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hammoth-dor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Hamat Tiberias.

definitions: Hamon-Gog; the place associated in prophecy with the burial of Gog's multitude.
Hamonah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hamor; a man named in Scripture, father of Shechem.
Hamuel; a Simeonite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Hamul; a son of Pharez from whom the Hamulites were reckoned.
Hamulites; descendants of Hamul reckoned among the families of Judah.
Hamutal; wife of Josiah and mother of Jehoahaz and Zedekiah.
Hanameel; the son of Shallum whose field Jeremiah purchased at Anathoth.
Hanan; borne by several biblical men, especially post-exilic officials, Levites, and heads of families.
Hananeel; the name of a man in Scripture, and also of the tower on the north side of Jerusalem mentioned in prophetic and rebuilding contexts.
Hanani; the seer who reproved Asa and also the brother of Nehemiah, with other biblical men of the same name.
Hananiah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially the companion of Daniel called Shadrach and the false prophet opposed by Jeremiah.
The hand; also power, possession, agency, or control, according to context.
Handbreadth; a small measure equal to the breadth of the hand.
Handed; past tense or past participial form of 'hand'.
Handful; as much as the hand can hold, or a small quantity.
A plural form referring to more than one handful.
A plural form referring to more than one handkerchief.
Handle; to take in the hand, touch, or deal with.
Handled; past tense or past participial form of 'handle'.
A plural form referring to more than one handl.
Handleth; older third-person singular form of 'handle'.
Handling; present participial form of 'handle', denoting ongoing action or process.
A handmaid; a female servant, especially one speaking of herself in humble dependence.
The term often carries a tone of lowliness and submission.
A handmaiden is a handmaid, a female servant speaking or regarded in humble dependence.
A plural form referring to more than one handmaiden.
Handmaids; female servants, especially in a relation of humble dependence.
Hands; the bodily members, and by extension power, possession, labor, or agency.
A plural form referring to more than one handstav.
Handwriting; present participial form of 'handwrit', denoting ongoing action or process.
Handywork; work made by the hand, workmanship.
A plural form referring to more than one han.
hanes.note: Location: Often identified with Ihnasiyyah al Madinah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hang; to suspend, be suspended, or put to death by hanging according to context.
Hanged; past tense or past participial form of 'hang'.
Hangeth; older third-person singular form of 'hang'.
Hanging; present participial form of 'hang', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one hanging.
Haniel; the son of Ephod and prince of the tribe of Manasseh.
Hannah; the mother of Samuel, known for fervent prayer and praise before the LORD.
Hannathon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hannathon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Hanaton.

definitions: Hanniel; a name in the tribal genealogies and territorial records, especially in Asher and Manasseh.
Hanoch; borne by several biblical men, including a son of Reuben and a son of Midian.
Hanochites; descendants of Hanoch reckoned as a family in Israel, especially in Reuben.
Hanun; the Ammonite king who shamefully treated David's servants, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hap; chance, event, or occurrence.
Hapharaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hapharaim.note: Location: Often identified with Et Taiyibeh.

definitions: Perhaps; by chance; it may be.
Happen; to occur or come to pass.
Happened; past tense or past participial form of 'happen'.
Happeneth; older third-person singular form of 'happen'.
Happier; more happy or more blessed.
Happy; blessed, fortunate, or joyful.
Hara; a mountain range or chain of heights mentioned in Scripture.
hara.note: Location: Likely in the Assyrian-Mesopotamian region connected with Israel's exile, though the exact range is uncertain.

definitions: Haradah; an encampment of Israel during the wilderness journey.
haradah.note: Location: On the wilderness route south of Canaan, probably in the northern Sinai or southern Negev, though the exact camp site is uncertain.

definitions: Haran; the brother of Abram and father of Lot, and also a Levitical name elsewhere in Scripture.
Hararite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Harar in Scripture.
Harbona; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who mentioned Haman's gallows.
Harbonah; a variant spelling of Harbona, the chamberlain of Ahasuerus.
Hard; firm, difficult, severe, or stubborn according to context.
To make hard, stubborn, or insensitive.
Hardeneth; older third-person singular form of 'harden'.
Harder; more hard, more severe, or more difficult.
Hardhearted; past tense or past participial form of 'hardheart'.
In a hard manner or degree.
Harshness, severity, or stubborn insensibility.
Often the hardening of heart against truth.
Hare; the animal so called, named among unclean creatures.
Hareph; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Hareth; older third-person singular form of 'hare'.
hareth.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Kharas, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Harhaiah; the father of Uzziel the goldsmith in Nehemiah's rebuilding record.
Harhas; father of Shallum, the husband of Huldah the prophetess, also written Hasrah.
Harhur; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Harim; the name of priestly or family groups after the exile, and also of individual men in genealogical and reform contexts.
Hariph; the name of a returning family and also of those represented among the covenant signers after the exile.
Harlot; a prostitute, or figuratively one guilty of spiritual unfaithfulness.
A plural form referring to more than one harlot.
Harm is injury or hurt; to harm is to injure or do wrong to.
Harmless; innocent, inoffensive, or doing no hurt.
Harnepher; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
The state, quality, or condition of being harnes.
Harnessed; past tense or past participial form of 'harness'.
Harod; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
harod.note: Location: Commonly identified with Well of Harod.

definitions: Harodite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Harod in Scripture.
harodite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Harazan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Haroeh; an ancestor in the Judahite genealogies, elsewhere represented by a related form such as Reaiah.
Harorite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Haror in Scripture.
harorite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Harazan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Harosheth; older third-person singular form of 'haroshs'.
Harp; the stringed instrument so called.
Harped; past tense or past participial form of 'harp'.
A plural form referring to more than one harper.
Harping; present participial form of 'harp', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one harp.
Harrow; an implement for breaking and smoothing soil, or to tear harshly according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one harrow.
Harsha; the head of a family whose genealogy could not be shown after the exile.
Hart; a stag or male deer.
A plural form referring to more than one hart.
Harum; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Harumaph; the father of Jedaiah in the rebuilding record of Nehemiah.
Haruphite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Haruph in Scripture.
Haruz; the father of Meshullemeth, wife of Manasseh king of Judah.
The reaping and gathering of ripe grain or other produce.
By figure, a season of ingathering, reward, or judgment.
Harvestman; one who reaps the harvest.
Hasadiah; a descendant of Zerubbabel in the Davidic line.
Hasenuah; a Benjamite family-name or ancestor in the post-exilic records.
Hashabiah; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and rulers in temple or administrative service.
Hashabnah; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Hashabniah; the father of Hattush, and also a Levite name in post-exilic records.
Hashbadana; one of those who stood with Ezra at the reading of the law.
Hashem; father or family designation connected with David's mighty men, especially in the phrase 'the sons of Hashem.'
Hashmonah; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
hashmonah.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route toward Edom, probably in the northern Sinai or Arabah fringe, though the exact camp site is uncertain.

definitions: Hashub; the name of several post-exilic men, especially builders and signers connected with Jerusalem.
Hashubah; a descendant of Zerubbabel in the Davidic line.
Hashum; the head of a family named among the returned exiles and covenant signers.
Hashupha; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Hasrah; father of Shallum the keeper of the wardrobe and father-in-law of Huldah; the name also appears in the form Harhas.
Hassenaah; the ancestor or family-name of the builders of the fish gate in Nehemiah.
Hasshub; the name of several post-exilic builders and family heads; the name also appears in the form Hashub.
An older second-person singular form meaning 'have.'
Hasted; past tense or past participial form of 'hast'.
Hasten; to make haste, speed, or urge on quickly.
Hastened; past tense or past participial form of 'hasten'.
Hasteneth; older third-person singular form of 'hasten'.
Makes haste; moves or acts quickly.
In a hasti manner or degree.
Hasting; present participial form of 'hast', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hasty; quick, rash, or done with too much speed.
Hasupha; the name of a family of Nethinims or temple servants; it also appears in the form Hashupha.
Hatach; the chamberlain appointed to attend Esther.
Hatch; to bring forth from the egg, or to devise according to context.
Hatcheth; older third-person singular form of 'hatch'.
Hated; past tense or past participial form of 'hat'.
Hateful; hateful to others, odious, or filled with hatred according to context.
In a hateful manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one hater.
Hatest; thou hatest; you hate or regard with settled aversion.
Hates; regards with aversion or settled opposition.
The older third-person singular present form of have.
Hathath; a descendant in the line of Othniel.
Hating; present participial form of 'hat', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hatipha; the head of a family of servants of Solomon.
Hatita; the head of a family of porters among the returned exiles.
Hatred; past tense or past participial form of 'hatr'.
A plural form referring to more than one hat.
Hattil; the head of a family whose genealogy was uncertain after the exile.
Hattush; borne by several biblical men, including a descendant of David and one who returned with Ezra.
In a haughti manner or degree.
Proud loftiness; arrogance of spirit.
Haughty; proud, lofty, and disdainful.
Haunt; a place of frequent resort, or to frequent according to context.
Hauran; a fertile district east of the Jordan, known for pastureland and tableland country.
To possess, hold, receive, or be under, according to context.
Haven; a harbor or place of safety for ships.
A plural form referring to more than one haven.
Havilah; the name of a son in early genealogies, distinct from the land of Havilah.
Possessing, holding, or being in a certain condition; also the act of possession according to context.
Havock; devastation, ruin, or violent destruction.
Havoth-Jair; the villages or tent-towns associated with Jair east of Jordan.
havoth-jair.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Qamm.

definitions: Hawk; the bird of prey so called.
Hay; dried grass used for fodder, and figuratively what soon withers.
Hazael; a man named in Scripture, father of Benhadad.
Hazaiah; a man of Judah dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Hazar-Addar; a southern border place in the territorial description of Judah.
hazar-addar.note: Location: In the far south near Kadesh-barnea and the wilderness of Zin on Judah's southern border.

definitions: Hazar-Enan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazar-enan.note: Location: Often identified with Al Qaryatayn.

definitions: Hazar-Gaddah; a southern town named in the inheritance of Judah.
hazar-gaddah.note: Location: In the southern Negev of Judah, likely in the Beersheba district.

definitions: Hazar-Hatticon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazar-hatticon.note: Location: Often identified with Al Qaryatayn.

definitions: Hazar-Shual; a southern town allotted to Judah and later counted to Simeon.
hazar-shual.note: Location: In the southern Negev near Beersheba.

definitions: Hazar-Susah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazar-susah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet esh Shamsaniyat.

definitions: Hazar-Susim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazar-susim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet esh Shamsaniyat.

definitions: Hazarded; past tense or past participial form of 'hazard'.
Hazarmaveth; older third-person singular form of 'hazarmavs'.
Hazazon-Tamar; another name associated with En-Gedi near the Dead Sea.
hazazon-tamar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ein Gedi.

definitions: Hazel; the tree or wood so called.
Hazelelponi; a woman named in the genealogies of Judah.
Hazerim; villages or enclosed settlements, and in some passages a named locality.
Hazeroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazeroth.note: Location: Often identified with Ain el Khadra.

definitions: Hazezon-Tamar; the place associated with En-Gedi near the Dead Sea; the name also appears in the form Hazazon-Tamar.
hazezon-tamar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ein Gedi.

definitions: Haziel; a son of Shimei in the Levitical genealogies.
Hazo; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Hazor; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hazor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Hazor.

definitions: The masculine singular personal pronoun.
The head; the bodily member, and by extension chief place, ruler, or top part according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one headband.
Headlong; with the head foremost, violently, rashly, or precipitately.
Heads; the bodily members, and by extension chiefs, tops, or leading parts.
A headstone is the chief stone, especially the top or principal stone in a building.
Heady; rash, headstrong, or reckless.
to cure, restore, or make whole.
Made whole; cured or restored.
Healer; one who heals or restores.
Healeth; older third-person singular form of 'heal'.
Healing; present participial form of 'heal', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one healing.
Health; soundness, healing, or welfare.
A heap is a pile or gathered mass of things; to heap is to pile up.
In Scripture it may describe ruins, gathered stones, or abundance laid together.
Heaped; past tense or past participial form of 'heap'.
Heapeth; older third-person singular form of 'heaps'.
A plural form referring to more than one heap.
Heard; listened to; received by hearing.
Often also took notice of or granted audience to.
Heardest; thou heardest; you heard or gave ear to.
A hearer is one who hears, especially one who listens to teaching or command.
A plural form referring to more than one hearer.
Hearest; thou hearest; you hear.
Frequently implies that you listen with attention or heed what is spoken.
Hears; receives by the ear; listens to.
Frequently implies heeding, attending, or obeying what is heard.
Hearing; present participial form of 'hear', denoting ongoing action or process.
To listen attentively; to give ear with obedience or regard.
Hearkenedst; an old second-person singular form of hearken, used with thou.
Hearkening; present participial form of 'hearken', denoting ongoing action or process.
The heart of the body.
Very often the inward man: the seat of thought, desire, purpose, and affection.
Hearted; past tense or past participial form of 'heart'.
A hearth is the paved or prepared place of a fire within a house.
In a hearti manner or degree.
Hearts; inward beings, minds, affections, or wills according to context.
Hearty; sincere, vigorous, or from the heart.
Heat; warmth, burning force, or fervor.
Heated; past tense or past participial form of 'heat'.
Heath; a bare or desert shrub-place, and by extension a desolate place.
The nations, especially those outside the covenant people and ignorant of the true God.
Heave; to lift up, raise, or move with effort.
Heaved; past tense or past participial form of 'heave'.
Heaven; the visible sky or the dwelling place of God and the blessed, according to context.
In a heaven manner or degree.
Heavier; more heavy, weightier, or more grievous.
In a heavi manner or degree.
Sorrow, grief, or burden of spirit.
Sometimes literal weight, but often inward sadness.
Heavy; weighty, burdensome, sorrowful, or dull according to context.
Heber; especially Heber the Kenite in the days of Deborah and Jael, and also the name of an ancestor in older genealogies and other biblical men.
Heberites; descendants of Heber reckoned among the families of Asher.
Hebrew; one of the covenant people descended from the patriarchs, especially viewed over against foreign nations.
A plural form referring to more than one hebrewes.
Hebrews; the people of Israel considered as Hebrews, especially in distinction from other nations or from Hellenists.
Hebron; one of the oldest cities in the land, associated with Abraham, the patriarchal tombs, and David's early reign.
hebron.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Hebronites; descendants of Hebron reckoned among the Levitical families.
Hedge; a fence or enclosure of thorns or plants, or a barrier set about.
Hedged; past tense or past participial form of 'hedge'.
A plural form referring to more than one hedg.
Careful attention; watchful regard.
As a verb, to give careful attention to.
Heel; the hinder part of the foot.
A plural form referring to more than one heel.
Hegai; the keeper of the women in the court of Ahasuerus who had charge of Esther.
Hege; the chamberlain who had charge of the women in Esther; the name also appears in the form Hegai.
Heifer; a young cow.
Height; elevation, high place, or exalted measure.
A plural form referring to more than one height.
Heinous; very wicked, grievous, or hateful.
One who receives an inheritance or stands to receive it by right.
Helah; one of the wives of Ashur in the genealogies of Judah.
Helam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Helbah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
helbah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet al Mahalib.

definitions: Helbon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Held; the past tense and past participle of hold.
Heldai; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and a returned exile mentioned in Zechariah.
Heleb; one of David's mighty men; the name also appears in the forms Heled and Heldai.
Heled; past tense or past participial form of 'hel'.
Helek; a son of Gilead from whom the Helekites were reckoned.
Helekites; descendants of Helek reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Helem; the name of a man in the Asherite genealogies and also a returned exile in the visions of Zechariah.
Heleph; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
heleph.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Irbadeh.

definitions: Helez; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and a Judahite ancestor.
Heli; the father of Joseph in Luke's genealogy of Jesus.
Helkai; a priestly head in the days of Joiakim.
Helkath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
helkath.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Qashish.

definitions: Helkath-Hazzurim; a field or tract of ground mentioned in Scripture.
helkath-hazzurim.note: Location: By the pool of Gibeon in Benjamin, where Joab's men fought Abner's men.

definitions: Hell; the unseen world of the dead in some passages, and in others the place of final punishment.
Helm; the steering gear or rudder of a ship.
A helmet is defensive armor worn upon the head.
In figurative use it may signify protection for the mind or hope of salvation.
A plural form referring to more than one helmet.
Helon; the father of Eliab, prince of the tribe of Zebulun.
Help; aid, assistance, or support.
Helped; past tense or past participial form of 'help'.
Helper; one who helps or aids.
A plural form referring to more than one helper.
Helpeth; older third-person singular form of 'helps'.
Helping; present participial form of 'help', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one help.
Helve; the handle of an axe or similar tool.
Hem; the border or edge of a garment.
Hemam; a Horite chief in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Homam.
Heman; the name of men in Scripture, especially the singer and wise man associated with temple music.
Hemath; a family-name or ancestor associated with the house of Rechab.
Hemdan; a Horite descendant in the line of Dishon, named in the Edomite genealogies.
Hemlock; a bitter and poisonous plant, or something compared to it for bitterness.
A plural form referring to more than one hem.
Hen; the female bird, especially of domestic fowl.
Hena; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Henadad; the name of post-exilic Levites and their family line in the rebuilding period.
From this place; away; from this cause or time.
From this time forward; hereafter.
Henceforward; from this time onward.
Henoch; the Greek form of Enoch or Hanoch in genealogical passages.
Hepher; borne by several biblical men, including ancestors in the tribes of Manasseh and Judah.
Hepherites; descendants of Hepher reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Hephzi-Bah; the wife of Hezekiah, and also a symbolic name for Zion meaning My delight is in her.
The feminine objective or possessive form: her.
A herald; a public announcer or one who proclaims on behalf of authority.
Herb; a green plant, especially one used for food or healing.
A plural form referring to more than one herb.
Herd; a company of cattle or beasts kept together.
Herdman; one who tends cattle or herds.
Herdmen; those who tend cattle or herds.
A plural form referring to more than one herd.
In this place; at this point.
Hereafter; after this, from now on, or in time to come.
Hereby; by this means, by this thing, or in this matter.
Herein; in this, within this, or in this matter.
Hereof; of this.
Heres; the name of a mountain slope, boundary point, or district in several Old Testament passages.
heres.note: Location: Used of more than one place; in context it may refer to a mount or border area in the hill country or toward the Jordan side.

definitions: Heresh; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
A plural form referring to more than one heresy.
Heresy; a sect, party, or false doctrine that departs from sound truth.
Heretick; a factious or divisive person holding and spreading error.
Heretofore; before now, formerly, or until this time.
Hereunto; to this, unto this end, or for this cause.
Herewith; with this, or by means of this.
An inheritance, possession, or allotted portion received as one's own.
A plural form referring to more than one heritag.
Hermas; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Hermes; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Hermogenes; one of those in Asia who turned away from Paul.
Hermon; the lofty northern mountain marking the far north of the land.
hermon.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one hermonit.
hermonites.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Herod; a name borne by several rulers of the Herodian house in the New Testament.
The exact person must be determined from the passage, such as Herod the Great, Herod Antipas, or Herod Agrippa.
Herodians; a party associated with the house, interests, or policy of Herod.
Herodias; the wife of Herod Philip and later of Herod Antipas, whose resentment led to John the Baptist's death.
Herodion; a kinsman of Paul greeted in the Epistle to the Romans.
Heron; the bird so called, named among unclean fowl.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
Hesed; past tense or past participial form of 'hes'.
Heshbon; the royal city of Sihon king of the Amorites, later held by Reuben and often named in prophecies concerning Moab.
heshbon.note: Location: On the plateau east of the Jordan north of the Arnon, usually identified with Hesban in modern Jordan.

definitions: Heshmon; a town in the southern inheritance of Judah.
heshmon.note: Location: In the southern Negev of Judah, probably in the Beersheba district.

definitions: Heth; older third-person singular form of 'he'.
Hethlon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hew; to chop, cut, or shape by cutting.
Hewed; past tense or past participial form of 'hew'.
Hewer; one who cuts wood or stone.
A plural form referring to more than one hewer.
Heweth; older third-person singular form of 'hew'.
Hewn; cut out or shaped by cutting.
Hezeki; an ancestor in the post-exilic line of Benjamin.
Hezekiah; the king of Judah so called.
Hezion; an early Syrian ruler in the line of Rezon and Ben-hadad.
Hezir; the name of priestly heads and courses in Israel.
Hezrai; one of David's mighty men, the Carmelite; the name also appears in the form Hezro.
Hezro; one of David's mighty men; the name also appears in the form Hezrai.
Hezron; especially a grandson of Judah and the ancestor of an important family line in Israel, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hezronites; descendants of Hezron reckoned as a family in Israel, especially in Judah.
Hiddai; one of David's mighty men from the brooks of Gaash; the name also appears in the form Hurai.
Hiddekel; the ancient name of the Tigris River.
hiddekel.note: Location: Usually identified with Tigris River.

definitions: To conceal, cover, or keep from sight.
Also to withdraw oneself from view or notice.
Hidest; thou hidest; you hide or conceal.
Hides; conceals; keeps from view or notice.
Hiding; present participial form of 'hide', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hiel; the Bethelite who rebuilt Jericho at the cost of his sons.
A plural form referring to more than one hierapoli.
Higgaion; a musical or meditative direction, often linked with solemn sound or meditation.
High; elevated in place, rank, dignity, or degree.
Higher; more high, more exalted, or more lofty.
Highest; most high, lofty, or exalted among those compared.
In a high manner or degree.
Highminded; past tense or past participial form of 'highmind'.
The state, quality, or condition of being highnes.
Highway; a raised road, main road, or public way.
A plural form referring to more than one highway.
Hilen; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hilen.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Alin.

definitions: Hilkiah; especially the high priest in Josiah's day who found the book of the law, and also the name of other biblical men.
A hill; a raised ground smaller than a mountain.
Hills in Scripture may mark habitations, worship sites, watch-points, or cultivated ground.
Hillel; the father of Abdon the judge of Israel.
Hills; raised grounds smaller than mountains.
The masculine singular objective personal pronoun.
The reflexive or emphatic form of he: his own self.
Hin; a Hebrew liquid measure.
Hind; a female deer.
To obstruct, delay, or keep back from progress.
Hindereth; older third-person singular form of 'hinder'.
Hindermost; an old second-person singular form of hindermo, used with thou.
Hindmost; an old second-person singular form of hindmo, used with thou.
A plural form referring to more than one hind.
A plural form referring to more than one hing.
Hinnom; the valley of Hinnom near Jerusalem, later associated with abomination and judgment.
Hip; the joint or side of the body at the upper thigh.
Hirah; the Adullamite friend and companion of Judah.
Hiram; the name of men in Scripture, especially the king of Tyre and also the craftsman who worked for Solomon.
Hire; wages, reward, or payment for service.
Hired; past tense or past participial form of 'hire'.
A hireling; one who serves for wages.
The word often carries the sense of a hired servant whose care is measured by pay and not by faithful ownership.
A plural form referring to more than one hir.
Hirest; older second-person singular form of 'hire', used with 'thou'.
The masculine possessive form: belonging to him.
A plural form referring to more than one his.
Hissing; present participial form of 'hiss', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hit; to strike or smite.
To this place; here.
Up to this time; until now; thus far.
Hittite; a member of the Hittite people.
Hittites; a people of Canaan, also represented in notable individuals such as Uriah the Hittite.
Hivite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hiv in Scripture.
Hivites; one of the peoples of Canaan named among the older inhabitants of the land.
Hizkiah; a man named among the returned exiles or covenant community.
Hizkijah; the name of men in post-exilic and Levitical records.
An exclamation expressing feeling or calling attention.
Hoar; gray or white with age or frost.
Hoarfrost; white frost formed from frozen vapor.
Hoary; gray or white with age, especially of hair, or ancient by extension.
Hobab; a Midianite relative of Moses associated with guiding Israel in the wilderness.
Hobah; the place north of Damascus to which Abram pursued the kings.
hobah.note: Location: North of Damascus in Syria, marking the far point of Abram's pursuit toward the upper Anti-Lebanon routes.

definitions: Hod; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Hodaiah; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and heads of families after the exile.
Hodaviah; the name of several post-exilic men and family heads in the records of return and service.
Hodesh; a woman named in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Hodevah; a variant form linked with Hodaviah, especially in the post-exilic family and Levitical records.
Hodiah; the name of men in post-exilic records, including Levites and heads of families.
Hodijah; a Levite or covenant signer named after the exile.
Hoglah; one of the daughters of Zelophehad.
Hoham; king of Hebron who joined the confederacy against Gibeon.
Hoised; past tense or past participial form of 'hois'.
Holden; an older form of held or the past participle of hold.
Holdest; thou holdest; you hold, keep fast, consider, or maintain.
The exact force must be determined from the passage.
Holds; keeps, grasps, contains, or maintains.
The exact force must be determined from the passage.
Holding; present participial form of 'hold', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one hold.
Hole; an opening, hollow, or cavity.
A plural form referring to more than one hol.
Holier; more holy.
Holiest; older second-person singular form of 'holy', used with 'thou'.
In a holi manner or degree.
The state of being holy, set apart, and pure before God.
Hollow; empty within, sunken, or insincere according to context.
Holon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Holpen; an older past participle of help.
Set apart unto God; pure, sacred, and morally clean.
Holyday; a feast day or sacred observance.
Homam; a Horite chief in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Hemam.
Home; one’s house, dwelling, or native place.
Homeborn; born in the house or land, not foreign.
Homer; a large Hebrew dry measure.
A plural form referring to more than one homer.
Honorable, decent, or worthy of respect.
Often carrying the sense of morally seemly as well as truthful.
In a honest manner or degree.
Honesty; honorable conduct, decency, or uprightness.
Honey; the sweet substance made by bees, or similar sweetness according to context.
Honeycomb; the comb containing honey.
Worthy of honor; held in esteem; dignified.
Honoured; past tense or past participial form of 'honour'.
Honourest; older second-person singular form of 'honour', used with 'thou'.
Honours; gives esteem, respect, or due value to.
A plural form referring to more than one honour.
A plural form referring to more than one hood.
Hoof; the hard foot of certain beasts.
A plural form referring to more than one hoof.
A hook is a curved instrument for catching, fastening, hanging, or drawing.
A plural form referring to more than one hook.
Confident expectation of good; a looking forward with trust.
Hoped; past tense or past participial form of 'hope'.
Hopes; expects with desire and confidence.
Hophni; the wicked son of Eli the priest, slain in the battle in which the ark was taken.
Hoping; present participial form of 'hope', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hor; the mountain on which Aaron died, though the name may refer to more than one height in Scripture.
hor.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Har Zin, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hor-Hagidgad; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
hor-hagidgad.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Khadakhid, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Horam; the name of kings and rulers in Scripture, including the king of Gezer and a king of the Zidonians.
Horeb; the mountain of God associated with Moses and Elijah.
It is closely related to Sinai and may designate the same mountain or the mountain range according to context.
horeb.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Jebel Musa.

definitions: Horem; a town in the inheritance of Naphtali.
horem.note: Location: In Upper Galilee within the territory of Naphtali.

definitions: Hori; the name of men in the genealogies, especially among the descendants of Seir.
Horims; the Horite people who dwelt in Mount Seir before Edom possessed it.
Horite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hor in Scripture.
Horites; the earlier inhabitants of Mount Seir before Edom became dominant there.
Hormah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hormah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Masos.

definitions: Horn; the horn of an animal, and figuratively strength, power, or exaltation.
Hornet; the stinging insect so called.
A plural form referring to more than one hornet.
A plural form referring to more than one horn.
Horonaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
horonaim.note: Location: Often identified with Beit Ur al Tahta.

definitions: Horonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Horon in Scripture.
Horrible; dreadful, shocking, or causing horror.
In a horrib manner or degree.
Horror; great fear, dread, or trembling astonishment.
Horse; the animal so called, often associated with war, strength, and swiftness.
Horseback; on the back of a horse.
A plural form referring to more than one horsehoof.
Horseleach; the bloodsucking leech used proverbially in Proverbs.
Horseman; one riding a horse, especially in war.
Horsemen; men riding horses, especially as mounted warriors or messengers.
Horses; the animals so called, often used for riding, chariots, or war.
Hosah; a Levite porter appointed to gatekeeping service.
Hosanna; a cry meaning Save now, later used as a shout of praise and acclamation.
Hosea; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also the earlier name of Joshua the son of Nun before Moses called him Jehoshua.
Hosen; stockings or leggings covering the legs.
Hoshaiah; borne by several biblical men, including a leader in the days of Jeremiah.
Hoshama; one of the sons of Jeconiah in the royal line.
Hoshea; the last king of Israel, and also the earlier name of Joshua and of other biblical men.
generous reception and care of guests or strangers.
A host; an army, a great company, or a heavenly company according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one hostag.
Hosts; armies, great companies, or multitudes.
Hot; full of heat, fervent, or vehement.
Hotham; an Asherite ancestor named in the tribal genealogies, elsewhere connected with the form Helem.
Hothan; the father of Shama and Jeiel in the tribe of Asher.
Hothir; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
In a hot manner or degree.
Hottest; older second-person singular form of 'hot', used with 'thou'.
Hough; to hamstring, especially horses.
Houghed; past tense or past participial form of 'hough'.
An hour; a measured part of the day, or a particular time or season.
A plural form referring to more than one hour.
A house, household, family line, or dwelling place, according to context.
All belonging to one house or family, including servants and dependents.
The master or head of a household.
A plural form referring to more than one household.
Houses; dwellings, households, or family lines according to context.
Housetop; the flat roof of a house.
A plural form referring to more than one housetop.
In what manner; to what degree; for what reason.
Nevertheless; yet; notwithstanding.
Howl; to cry aloud in pain, grief, or desolation.
Howled; past tense or past participial form of 'howl'.
Howling; present participial form of 'howl', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one howling.
Howsoever; in whatever way, however.
Huge; very great in size.
Hukkok; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hukkok.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Jemeijimeh.

definitions: Hukok; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
hukok.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Regev.

definitions: Hul; a son of Aram in the early post-Flood genealogies.
Huldah; the prophetess consulted in the days of Josiah concerning the book of the law.
Lowly in mind; not proud or self-exalting.
As a verb, to bring low or cause to be lowly.
Humbledst; an old second-person singular form of humbl, used with thou.
The state, quality, or condition of being humblenes.
In a humb manner or degree.
the act or state of humbling oneself; abasement.
Lowliness of mind; a modest and submissive temper.
Humtah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
humtah.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Tel Rumeida.

definitions: The number one hundred.
Hundredfold; a hundred times as much.
A plural form referring to more than one hundred.
Hundredth; the one-hundredth part, or the ordinal number one hundredth.
Lack of food; hunger.
Also to hunger, that is, to long or crave.
Hungerbitten; wasted or pinched by hunger.
Hungered; past tense or past participial form of 'hunger'.
Hungred; past tense or past participial form of 'hungr'.
Hungry; feeling hunger, or figuratively longing eagerly.
Hunt; to pursue animals or persons in order to catch them.
Hunted; past tense or past participial form of 'hunt'.
Hunter; one who hunts.
A plural form referring to more than one hunter.
Huntest; older second-person singular form of 'hunt', used with 'thou'.
Hunteth; older third-person singular form of 'hunt'.
Hunting; present participial form of 'hunt', denoting ongoing action or process.
Hupham; a Benjamite descendant whose name also stands behind the Huphamite clan.
Huphamites; descendants of Hupham reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Huppah; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Huppim; a Benjamite descendant whose name also stands behind the Huppimite clan.
Hur; the man who upheld Moses' hands with Aaron during the battle with Amalek, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hurai; one of David's mighty men from the brooks of Gaash; the name also appears in the form Hiddai.
Huram; the form of the name Hiram used especially of the king of Tyre or the skilled workman employed by Solomon.
Huri; the father of Shaphat, prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Hurl; to cast or throw with force.
Hurleth; older third-person singular form of 'hurl'.
Hurling; present participial form of 'hurl', denoting ongoing action or process.
To hurt is to injure, harm, or wound; hurt is injury or harm.
Hurtful; harmful, damaging, or injurious.
Hurting; present participial form of 'hurt', denoting ongoing action or process.
A husband; a married man in relation to his wife.
A husbandman; a cultivator of the ground, especially a farmer or vine-dresser.
The term regularly belongs to agricultural life and is often used figuratively in parables.
Husbandmen; cultivators of the ground, especially farmers or vine-dressers.
Husbandry; cultivation of land, farming, or by figure God’s tillage.
A plural form referring to more than one husband.
Hushah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hushai; David's friend and counsellor, whose counsel helped frustrate Ahithophel during Absalom's rebellion.
Husham; the Edomite king who reigned after Bela.
Hushathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hushath in Scripture.
hushathite.note: Location: Often identified with Husan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hushim; the name of persons in Scripture, including a son of Dan and a wife in the house of Shaharaim.
A husk is the outer covering of grain or fruit.
A plural form referring to more than one husk.
Huz; a son of Nahor, and also a name associated with the land of Job.
Huzzab; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Hymenaeus; a false teacher opposed by Paul in the Pastoral Epistles.
A hymn; a song of praise, especially directed unto God.
Hymns; songs of praise, especially directed unto God.
A plural form referring to more than one hypocrisy.
Dissimulation in religion or morals; a false outward show concealing the inward reality.
Hypocrite; one who pretends to piety, goodness, or sincerity while concealing another heart.
A plural form referring to more than one hypocrit.
marked by pretense or hypocrisy.
Hyssop; the small plant used in sprinkling and ceremonial cleansing.
The first-person singular personal pronoun.
I-Chabod; the name given at the fall of Eli's house, meaning the glory is departed.
Ibhar; one of the sons of David born in Jerusalem.
Ibleam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ibleam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Ibneiah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ibnijah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ibri; a Merarite name in the Levitical genealogies.
Ibzan; the judge of Israel from Bethlehem.
Ice; frozen water.
Iconium; a city of Asia Minor visited in apostolic mission work.
It is associated with preaching, division, and persecution in the book of Acts.
iconium.note: Location: In the central plateau of Asia Minor, commonly identified with modern Konya in Turkey.

definitions: Idalah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
idalah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Huwarah.

definitions: Idbash; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Iddo; a prophet or seer, and also the name of heads of families and priestly lines.
Idle; inactive, vain, unprofitable, or empty according to context.
