The state, quality, or condition of being idlenes.
Idol; an image or false god worshipped in place of the true God.
Idolater; one who worships idols or gives religious honour to false gods.
A plural form referring to more than one idolater.
A plural form referring to more than one idolatry.
Idolatrous; given to idolatry or marked by false worship.
Idolatry; the worship of idols or false gods, and by extension the giving of devotion to what belongs to God alone.
Idols; images or false gods worshipped in place of the true God.
Idumaea; the Greek form of Edom, the southern territory associated with Esau's descendants.
idumaea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Idumea; the later Greek and Roman form of Edom, the southern land tied to Esau's line.
idumea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Buseira.

definitions: If; on condition that; whether.
Igal; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and one of the spies.
Igdaliah; the father of Hanan in the days of Jeremiah.
Igeal; a name borne by one of David's mighty men.
Ignominy; public shame, disgrace, or reproach.
Lack of knowledge; unawareness; uninstructed condition.
Lacking knowledge; uninstructed in the matter at hand.
Not necessarily stupid, but uninformed.
In a ignorant manner or degree.
Iim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
iim.note: Location: Often identified with Deir al Ghawi.

definitions: Ije-Abarim; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
ije-abarim.note: Location: Often identified with Medeineh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Ijon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ijon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Dibbine.

definitions: Ikkesh; the father of Ira, one of David's mighty men.
Ilai; one of David's mighty men, the Ahohite, in a parallel place elsewhere called Zalmon.
Ill; evil, sick, harmful, or badly according to context.
Illuminated; past tense or past participial form of 'illuminat'.
Illyricum; the region northwest of Macedonia marking the far reach of Paul's preaching journey.
illyricum.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Salona.

definitions: A likeness, representation, or formed resemblance.
At times an idol or graven representation.
Imagery; carved or pictured images, especially idolatrous representations, or figurative image-work by context.
a device, inward thought, or forming of ideas in the mind.
A plural form referring to more than one imagination.
Imagine; to devise in thought, purpose, or inward meditation, whether good or evil.
Imagined; past tense or past participial form of 'imagine'.
Imagineth; older third-person singular form of 'imagine'.
Imla; the form of the name used for Imlah, father of the prophet Micaiah.
Imlah; father of Micaiah the prophet who spoke before Ahab and Jehoshaphat.
Immanuel; a prophetic name meaning God with us, pointing to the promised presence of God with His people and applied to Christ.
Immediately; without delay; at once.
Immer; the name of a priestly family and its ancestral head.
not subject to death; also one who is deathless.
Freedom from death; endless life and incorruption.
unchangeableness.
Immutable; unchangeable, fixed, or not subject to alteration.
Imna; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the lines of Asher and Levite service.
Imnah; an Asherite ancestor and also the name of a Levite officer in the days of Hezekiah and other biblical men.
Impart; to give, communicate, or share with another.
Imparted; past tense or past participial form of 'impart'.
a hindrance, obstacle, or obstruction.
Impenitent; not repentant, unremorseful, or hardened in sin.
Imperious; commanding, overbearing, or acting with lordly authority.
Implacable; not to be appeased, relentless, or unforgiving.
Implead; to bring into judgment, accuse, or sue before a court.
persistent urgent request or pressing solicitation.
Impose; to lay upon, place as a burden, or enjoin.
Imposed; past tense or past participial form of 'impose'.
Impossible; not able to be done, attained, or brought about.
Impotent; weak, powerless, or unable to act effectively.
Impoverish; to make poor or reduce in substance.
Impoverished; past tense or past participial form of 'impoverish'.
Imprisoned; past tense or past participial form of 'imprison'.
confinement in prison or captivity.
A plural form referring to more than one imprisonment.
Impudent; shamelessly bold, insolent, or disrespectful.
To reckon, attribute, or set to one's account.
Imputing; present participial form of 'impute', denoting ongoing action or process.
Imrah; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Imri; the name of men in post-exilic and tribal records.
In; within, into, among, or in relation to.
In so far as; seeing that; because.
Incense; a fragrant substance burned in sacred service.
It may also figure prayer or acceptable worship rising before God.
Incensed; past tense or past participial form of 'incense'.
Incline; to bend, turn, stoop, or dispose the heart toward something.
Inclined; past tense or past participial form of 'incline'.
Inclineth; older third-person singular form of 'incline'.
Inclose; to enclose, shut in, or surround.
Inclosed; past tense or past participial form of 'inclose'.
A plural form referring to more than one inclosing.
Incontinency; lack of self-restraint, especially in sensual desire.
Incontinent; lacking self-restraint, especially in passions or appetite.
Incorruptible; not subject to decay, corruption, or moral bribery.
freedom from decay or corruption.
Growth, multiplication, or addition.
As a verb, to make or become greater.
Increasest; older second-person singular form of 'increase', used with 'thou'.
Increasing; present participial form of 'increase', denoting ongoing action or process.
Incredible; hard to believe, astonishing, or beyond common expectation.
Incurable; not able to be healed or remedied.
Indebted; past tense or past participial form of 'indebt'.
In truth; truly; really.

india.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Indus River.

definitions: Strong displeasure or anger stirred by what is wrong.
Inditing; present participial form of 'indit', denoting ongoing action or process.
Industrious; diligent, busy, and steady in labor.
Inexcusable; without excuse or just defense.
Infallible; not liable to fail, err, or deceive.
Infamous; of evil repute, disgraceful, or notorious for shame.
Infamy; disgrace, dishonour, or evil repute.
A very young child; a babe.
Of or relating to infancy or an early stage of growth.
A plural form referring to more than one infant.
Inferior; lower in rank, place, or quality.
As a noun, one lower in station.
Infidel; an unbeliever, especially one without faith in the true God or in the Christian confession.
As an adjective, unbelieving.
Infinite; without limit or bound; immeasurable or unending.
Weakness, frailty, or feebleness, whether bodily or moral.
Inflame; to set on fire, kindle, or stir up strongly.
kindling, swelling, or intense heat in a body or matter.
Inflicted; past tense or past participial form of 'inflict'.
A plural form referring to more than one influenc.
Infolding; present participial form of 'infold', denoting ongoing action or process.
Inform; to give knowledge, report, or make known.
Informed; past tense or past participial form of 'inform'.
Ingathering; present participial form of 'ingather', denoting ongoing action or process.
To dwell in, occupy, or live within.
One who dwells in a place; a resident.
A plural form referring to more than one inhabiter.
Inhabitest; older second-person singular form of 'inhabit', used with 'thou'.
Inhabiteth; older third-person singular form of 'inhabit'.
Inhabiting; present participial form of 'inhabit', denoting ongoing action or process.
That which is received as a possession, lot, or heritage.
Often an allotted possession from God or by family right.
A plural form referring to more than one inheritanc.
Inherited; past tense or past participial form of 'inherit'.
Inheriteth; older third-person singular form of 'inherits'.
Inheritor; one who inherits or receives an inheritance.
Moral crookedness, unrighteousness, or guilt.
Often wickedness considered in its perverse and blameworthy character.
Injured; past tense or past participial form of 'injur'.
Harmful, unjust, or abusive.
Injustice; wrong, unfair dealing, or unrighteous judgment.
Ink; the liquid used for writing.
Inkhorn; a small vessel or case for holding ink.
Inn; a lodging place for travellers, a place of temporary shelter or entertainment on a journey.
Inner; inward, within, or more hidden from the outer part.
Innermost; an old second-person singular form of innermo, used with thou.
Innocency; innocence, cleanness from guilt, or uprightness of conduct.
Free from guilt, sin, or blame.
Harmless or without evil intent.
A plural form referring to more than one innocent.
Too many to be counted; countless.
Inordinate; excessive, immoderate, or disorderly beyond proper bounds.
search, inquiry, or careful investigation.
words written or engraved on something.
A preposition marking relation in place, time, manner, cause, or degree according to context.
Insomuch; to such a degree, so that, or inasmuch as the matter extends so far.
divine influence communicating truth; also the breathing in of air.
Urgent, earnest, or pressing in action or request.
Sometimes ready at hand or immediately present, but often carrying the sense of persistence and earnestness.
Earnestly; urgently; with pressing persistence.
Sometimes also without delay, but not limited to that.
Instead; in place of, in exchange for, or as a substitute.
Instruct; to teach, direct, or furnish with understanding.
Instructed; past tense or past participial form of 'instruct'.
Instructer; an older spelling of instructor, one who teaches or gives direction.
A plural form referring to more than one instructer.
Instructing; present participial form of 'instruct', denoting ongoing action or process.
Teaching, training, and discipline imparted for guidance.
Instructor; one who teaches, directs, or gives knowledge.
a tool, implement, document, or means by which something is done.
A plural form referring to more than one instrument.
a revolt or uprising against authority.
uprightness, honesty, or wholeness of character.
understanding, knowledge, or news received.
Intend; to purpose, mean, or have in design.
Intended; past tense or past participial form of 'intend'.
Intendest; older second-person singular form of 'intend', used with 'thou'.
Intending; present participial form of 'intend', denoting ongoing action or process.
Closely attentive, fixed, or earnest in mind.
Purpose, design, or intention.
A plural form referring to more than one intent.
Pleading on behalf of another; mediation by prayer or request.
A plural form referring to more than one intercession.
Intercessor; one who intercedes or pleads on behalf of another.
Intermeddle; to meddle in a matter, mix oneself in, or become involved.
Intermeddleth; older third-person singular form of 'intermeddle'.
a pause or temporary cessation.
Interpret; to explain, expound, or make known the meaning of words, dreams, or signs.
The declaring or unfolding of the meaning of a thing.
Often the explanation of a dream, vision, saying, or tongue.
Interpreted; past tense or past participial form of 'interpret'.
Interpreter; one who explains, translates, or makes another's speech understood.
Interpreting; present participial form of 'interpret', denoting ongoing action or process.
Into; from without to within; toward entrance or change.
Intreat; to entreat, ask earnestly, deal with, or treat according to context.
Intreated; past tense or past participial form of 'intreat'.
A plural form referring to more than one intreaty.
Intreaty; earnest request, entreaty, or supplication.
Intruding; present participial form of 'intrud', denoting ongoing action or process.
Invade; to enter by force, assail, or break in upon.
Invaded; past tense or past participial form of 'invade'.
a hostile entrance into a land or place.
To devise, contrive, or frame in thought.
Invented; past tense or past participial form of 'invent'.
A plural form referring to more than one invention.
A plural form referring to more than one inventor.
Not seen or not visible to the eye.
Invited; past tense or past participial form of 'invit'.
Within; on the inside; turned toward the inner part.
Also pertaining to the inner man, hidden thought, or secret affection.
In a inward manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one inward.
Iphedeiah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ir; a son of Bela in the Benjamite genealogies.
Ir-Nahash; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ir-nahash.note: Location: Often identified with Dayr Nakhkhas.

definitions: Ir-Shemesh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ir-shemesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shemesh.

definitions: Ira; one of David's priests and mighty men, and also the name of other biblical men.
Irad; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Iram; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Iri; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Irijah; the captain who arrested Jeremiah at the gate of Benjamin.
Iron; the metal so called.
A plural form referring to more than one iron.
Irpeel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Iru; a son of Caleb in the genealogies of Judah.
The third-person singular present form of be.
Isaac; the son of Abraham and father of Jacob.
Isaiah; son of Amoz, the major prophet of Judah through whom many great prophecies of judgment and Messiah were given.
Iscah; the daughter of Haran in the family of Abram.
Iscariot; a surname or identifying designation, used most notably of Judas Iscariot and commonly linked with Kerioth.
Ish-bosheth; older third-person singular form of 'ish boshs'.
Ish-Tob; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ish-tob.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tayibeh.

definitions: Ishbah; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Ishbak; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Ishbi-Benob; a giant of the Philistines who sought to slay David.
Ishi; borne by several biblical men, especially in the tribal genealogies of Israel.
Ishiah; borne by several biblical men, especially among the Levites and the line of Issachar.
Ishijah; the name of men in Scripture, including Levites and descendants in the line of Issachar.
Ishma; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Ishmael; the son of Abraham by Hagar, and also the name of other biblical men.
Ishmaelite; a descendant of Ishmael, or a member of the Ishmaelite people.
Ishmaelites; the descendants of Ishmael, often appearing as desert traders and nomadic peoples.
Ishmaiah; borne by several biblical men, including a Gibeonite leader who came to David.
Ishmeelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Ishmeel in Scripture.
Ishmeelites; a spelling variant of Ishmaelites, the descendants of Ishmael.
Ishmerai; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ishod; a descendant in the line of Manasseh.
Ishpan; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ishuah; a son of Asher named among the tribal ancestors.
Ishuai; a form of the name borne by a son of Asher and by families descending from him.
Ishui; a name in the house of Saul and in the tribe of Asher, used of different persons in Scripture.
Island; a tract of land surrounded by water, and in some passages a distant coastland or maritime region.
A plural form referring to more than one island.
An isle; an island, or sometimes a coastland lying across the sea.
In prophetic or poetic passages it may point broadly to distant maritime lands.
Isles; islands or distant coastlands.
In Scripture the word often reaches beyond small islands to far maritime lands and peoples.
Ismachiah; a Levite appointed under Hezekiah in matters of offerings and dedicated things.
Ismaiah; borne by several biblical men, including one over the tribe of Zebulun and one of David's mighty men.
Ispah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Israel; the covenant people descended from Jacob, the kingdom bearing that name, or the patriarch himself according to context.
israel.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Israelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Israel in Scripture.
Israelites; the covenant people descended from Jacob, the nation of Israel.
Israelitish; belonging to the Israelites or to Israel.
Issachar; chiefly the son of Jacob and the tribe descended from him, though the name also belongs to others in Scripture.
Isshiah; a Levite or family head named in the genealogies; the name also appears in the form Jesiah.
Issue; a coming forth, offspring, result, or discharge according to context.
As a verb, to go out or proceed.
A discharge or continual flow of blood.
Issued; past tense or past participial form of 'issue'.
A plural form referring to more than one issu.
Isuah; the form of the name used for Ishuah, a son of Asher named among the tribal ancestors.
Isui; an Asherite name in the tribal records, corresponding to the forms Ishuai or Jesui.
The neuter singular personal pronoun.
Italian; belonging to Italy or an Italian person.
In a ita manner or degree.
italy.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Rome.

definitions: Itch; an irritating sensation that causes desire to scratch.
As a verb, to feel such irritation or figuratively to have an eager craving.
Itching; present participial form of 'itch', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ithai; a name used of notable men, including David's loyal Gittite warrior; it corresponds to the form Ittai.
Ithamar; a man named in Scripture, son of Aaron.
Ithiel; a name in Proverbs associated with Agur's sayings, and also a post-exilic name.
Ithmah; one of David's mighty men, called the Moabite.
Ithnan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ithnan.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Mesudat Har Hemet.

definitions: Ithra; the father of Amasa, called an Ishmaelite or Israelite in parallel accounts.
Ithran; the name of men in the genealogies, especially among Seir and Asher.
Ithream; a son of David by Eglah.
Ithrite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Ithr in Scripture.
ithrite.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Attir.

definitions: Ithrites; a clan or family group named among David's mighty men.
The neuter possessive form: belonging to it.
The reflexive or emphatic form of it: its own self.
Ittah-Kazin; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ittah-kazin.note: Location: Often identified with Kefr Kenna.

definitions: Ittai; especially the Gittite who remained faithful to David in his flight, and also other men of the name.
Ituraea; a district northeast of Galilee, ruled with neighboring lands in the time mentioned by Luke.
ituraea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Ivah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ivah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Kafr Aya.

definitions: The hard white material of an elephant's tusk or similar tooth.
Made of ivory or resembling it in whiteness.
Izehar; the form of the name Izhar used for a son of Kohath in the Levitical line.
Izeharites; a spelling variant of Izharites, descendants of Izhar among the Levites.
Izhar; a son of Kohath and ancestor in an important Levitical branch.
Izharites; descendants of Izhar reckoned among the Levitical families.
Izrahiah; a descendant of Issachar in the tribal genealogies.
Izrahite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Izrah in Scripture.
Izri; one of the sons of Jeduthun, over the course of temple singers appointed for sacred music.
Jaakan; a wilderness station remembered in the phrase Beeroth of the children of Jaakan during Israel's journey.
Jaakobah; a Simeonite name in the genealogies.
Jaala; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Jaalah; the name of a family of Nethinims or temple servants; it also appears in the form Jaala.
Jaalam; a son of Esau by Aholibamah.
Jaanai; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Jaare-Oregim; a personal name occurring in the Davidic battle records.
Jaasau; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jaasiel; the name of men in Scripture, including one associated with David's officers.
Jaazaniah; borne by several biblical men, including leaders seen in Ezekiel's visions and a Rechabite.
Jaazer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jaazer.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Sara.

definitions: Jaaziah; a Merarite name in the Levitical records.
Jaaziel; the name of men in Scripture, including a musician or officer in Davidic and Levitical lists; the name also appears in the form Aziel.
Jabal; the early ancestor of those who dwell in tents and keep cattle.
Jabbok; the river where Jacob wrestled before meeting Esau.
jabbok.note: Location: Usually identified with Zarqa River.

definitions: Jabesh; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place Jabesh-gilead.
Jabesh-Gilead; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jabesh-gilead.note: Location: Often identified with Tell el Maqlub.

definitions: Jabez; the Judahite whose prayer for blessing and enlargement is specially remembered.
Jabin; the royal name of Canaanite kings, especially the king of Hazor in the days of Deborah and Barak.
Jabneel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jabneh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jachan; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Jachin; the right-hand pillar of Solomon's temple, and also the name of persons and a family in Israel.
Jachinites; descendants of Jachin reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Jacinth; a precious stone or deep bluish-purple colour named in apocalyptic description.
Jacob; the patriarch afterward called Israel, and by extension the people descended from him.
Jada; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jadau; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jaddua; the name of men in Scripture, especially a high priest in post-exilic times.
Jadon; one who helped in the rebuilding of Jerusalem in the days of Nehemiah.
Jael; the wife of Heber the Kenite who slew Sisera.
Jagur; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jagur.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Ira.

definitions: Jah; a shortened form of the divine name of the LORD, used especially in praise.
Jahath; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites named in genealogies and temple service.
Jahaz; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jahaz.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahazah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jahazah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahaziah; the name of men in Scripture, including one who spoke in Ezra's assembly.
Jahaziel; the Levite upon whom the Spirit came in Jehoshaphat's day, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jahdai; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jahdiel; a name in tribal and pastoral records, including one over David's flocks.
Jahdo; the father of Obed, associated with the half tribe of Manasseh.
Jahleel; a son of Zebulun from whom the Jahleelites were reckoned.
Jahleelites; descendants of Jahleel reckoned among the families of Zebulun.
Jahmai; a descendant of Tola in the line of Issachar.
Jahzah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jahzah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahzeel; a son of Naphtali whose descendants formed a clan in Israel, also written Jahziel.
Jahzeelites; descendants of Jahzeel reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Jahzerah; a priestly name in the line of Aaron.
Jahziel; the form of the name used for Jahzeel, a son of Naphtali and ancestor of a clan.
Jailor; keeper of a prison, a gaoler.
Jair; the judge of Israel and an ancestor linked with Jair's settlements in Gilead, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jairite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Jair in Scripture.
Jairus; the ruler of the synagogue whose daughter Jesus raised from the dead.
Jakan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Akan.
Jakeh; the father of Agur in Proverbs 30.
Jakim; a name in the genealogies, especially in the line of Simeon.
Jalon; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jambres; one of the names Paul gives for the men who withstood Moses.
James; the name of several New Testament men, especially James the son of Zebedee, James the son of Alphaeus, and James the Lord's brother.
Jamin; the name of men in Scripture, especially sons of Simeon and descendants in priestly or tribal lists.
Jaminites; descendants of Jamin reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Jamlech; a Simeonite prince in the tribal records.
Jangling; present participial form of 'jangl', denoting ongoing action or process.
Janna; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jannes; one of the names Paul gives for the men who withstood Moses.
Janoah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
janoah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Yanun.

definitions: Janohah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
janohah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Yanun.

definitions: Janum; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
janum.note: Location: Often identified with Bani Nai’im.

definitions: Japheth; older third-person singular form of 'japhs'.
Japhia; the name of men in Scripture, including a Canaanite king defeated in Joshua's day.
Japhlet; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Japhleti; one belonging to the house or clan descended from Japhlet, named among the family divisions in Israel.
Japho; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jarah; a descendant in the Benjamite line of Saul; the name also appears in the form Jehoadah.
Jareb; a prophetic title for the king of Assyria, probably carrying the sense of an adversary or avenger.
Jared; past tense or past participial form of 'jar'.
Jaresiah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jarha; the Egyptian servant who became son-in-law to Sheshan.
Jarib; borne by several biblical men, especially heads of families and priests in the post-exilic community.
Jarmuth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jarmuth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yarmuth.

definitions: Jaroah; a Gadite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Jashen; a family designation connected with David's mighty men, likely represented elsewhere by the form Hashem.
Jasher; a personal name distinct from the title of the book of Jasher.
Jashobeam; one of David's chief mighty men and captain over the first course.
Jashub; a son of Issachar and ancestor of a clan, elsewhere represented by the form Job.
Jashubi-Lehem; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jashubites; descendants of Jashub reckoned among the families of Issachar.
Jasiel; one of David's mighty men.
Jason; the Thessalonian host who received Paul and suffered on his account.
Jasper; a precious stone used in descriptions of beauty and heavenly glory.
Jathniel; the son of Meshelemiah in the records of gatekeepers.
Jattir; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jattir.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Attir.

definitions: Javan; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with the Greek or Ionian peoples.
Javelin; a light spear or dart used in war.
Jaw; the bone or part of the mouth in which the teeth are set.
Jawbone; the bone of the jaw, especially used of the ass's jawbone in Samson's history.
A plural form referring to more than one jaw.
Jazer; an important city east of the Jordan in the territory later held by Gad, known for its surrounding pastureland and vineyards.
jazer.note: Location: East of the Jordan north of Heshbon, commonly identified with Khirbet Jazzir in modern Jordan.

definitions: Jaziz; the Hagerite set over David's flocks.
Zealous to guard what belongs to oneself; intolerant of rivalry.
Suspicious or resentful because of another's favor or possession.
A plural form referring to more than one jealousy.
Jealousy; zeal for exclusive right, suspicion of rivalry, or passionate resentment according to context.
Jearim; a height or ridge associated with the border descriptions near Kirjath-jearim.
jearim.note: Location: Near Kirjath-jearim on the Judah-Benjamin border, in the western hill country.

definitions: Jeaterai; a Levite ancestor in the line leading to Asaph, elsewhere represented by the variant Ethni.
Jeberechiah; the father of Zechariah's companion in Isaiah's sign.
A plural form referring to more than one jebu.
Jebusi; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jebusite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Jebus in Scripture.
Jebusites; the older inhabitants of Jerusalem before David took the stronghold.
Jecamiah; the name of men in the genealogies, including descendants of Jeconiah and Caleb.
Jecholiah; the mother of king Uzziah, also called Jecoliah.
Jechonias; the Greek form of Jeconiah or Jehoiachin.
Jecoliah; the mother of king Uzziah, also written Jecholiah.
Jeconiah; another name of Jehoiachin king of Judah.
Jedaiah; borne by several biblical men, especially priests and builders after the exile.
Jediael; the name of men in Scripture, including a Benjamite ancestor and one of David's mighty men.
Jedidah; the mother of Josiah king of Judah.
Jedidiah; a man named in Scripture, son of David, husband of Pharaoh, father of Taphath.
Jeduthun; especially the Levitical singer and musician appointed by David, and also the family or musical line associated with his name.
Jeezer; the form of the name used for Abiezer, the Manassite clan especially associated with Gideon.
Jeezerites; descendants of Jeezer reckoned among the families of Manasseh; Jeezer is elsewhere called Abiezer.
Jegar-Sahadutha; a heap or marked pile of stones mentioned in Scripture.
jegar-sahadutha.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Jehaleleel; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jehalelel; a Merarite or Levitical name in the temple records.
Jehdeiah; a Merarite name in the Levitical genealogies.
Jehezekel; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Jehiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites or porters connected with the ark and temple service.
Jehiel; the name of many men in Scripture, especially Levites, princes, and overseers in Davidic and post-exilic records.
Jehieli; a name borne by men in Levitical and royal-administrative records; it corresponds closely to the form Jehiel.
Jehizkiah; an Ephraimite leader who opposed the cruel treatment of Judah's captives.
Jehoadah; a descendant in the line of Saul; the same figure appears under the form Jarah.
Jehoaddan; the mother of Amaziah king of Judah.
Jehoahaz; borne by kings of Israel and Judah who reigned in troubled times, and by other biblical men.
Jehoash; the form of the name Joash used especially of kings of Israel and Judah.
Jehohanan; the name of men in post-exilic and genealogical records.
Jehoiachin; king of Judah, son of Jehoiakim, carried away captive to Babylon.
Jehoiada; the priest who preserved Joash and restored proper worship in Judah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jehoiakim; king of Judah, son of Josiah, who reigned under Egyptian and then Babylonian pressure.
Jehoiarib; the head of the first priestly course, and also a family name among the priests.
Jehonadab; the son of Rechab who joined Jehu and became the forefather of the Rechabites, also written Jonadab.
Jehonathan; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and officers in the service of temple and king.
Jehoram; the name of kings in Israel and Judah, sometimes shortened in the text to Joram.
Jehoshabeath; the daughter of king Jehoram who hid the young Joash from Athaliah, also written Jehosheba.
Jehoshaphat; especially the king of Judah, son of Asa, noted for seeking the LORD and for alliances that brought trouble.
Jehosheba; the daughter of king Jehoram who rescued and hid Joash, also written Jehoshabeath.
Jehoshua; a man named in Scripture, son of Nun.
Jehovah; an English form used for the covenant name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
Jehovah-Jireh; a hill or elevated place mentioned in Scripture.
jehovah-jireh.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Mount Moriah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Jehovah-Nissi; a memorial or compound sacred name used in Scripture.
Jehovah-Shalom; a memorial or compound sacred name used in Scripture.
Jehozabad; borne by several biblical men, including Levites and the servant who conspired against Joash.
Jehozadak; the priest carried away in the captivity, father of Jeshua the high priest.
Jehu; especially the king of Israel who executed judgment on the house of Ahab, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jehubbah; a son of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Jehucal; son of Shelemiah, one of the princes sent to Jeremiah in the last days of Judah.
Jehud; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jehudi; the man who read Jeremiah's roll before king Jehoiakim.
Jehudijah; a woman named in the genealogies of Judah.
Jehush; borne by several biblical men, including sons in the houses of David and Benjamin.
Jeiel; the name of many men in Scripture, especially Levites, gatekeepers, musicians, and heads of families.
Jekabzeel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jekabzeel.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Hora.

definitions: Jekameam; a Levitical name in the line of Hebron.
Jekamiah; the name of men in the royal and Judahite genealogies.
Jekuthiel; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jemima; one of the daughters of Job born after his restoration.
Jemuel; a son of Simeon, elsewhere represented by the form Nemuel.
Jeoparded; past tense or past participial form of 'jeopard'.
Jeopardy; danger, hazard, or exposure to peril.
Jephthae; the Greek form of Jephthah the judge.
Jephthah; a judge of Israel who delivered the people from the Ammonites.
Jephunneh; best known as the father of Caleb.
Jerah; a son of Joktan in the early genealogies after the Flood.
Jerahmeel; the name of men in Scripture, especially a Judahite ancestor and a royal son in Jeremiah.
Jerahmeelites; descendants of Jerahmeel, a Judahite house in the south country.
Jered; past tense or past participial form of 'jer'.
Jeremai; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jeremiah; the prophet of Judah who warned of coming judgment and ministered in the final days before the Babylonian fall.
Jeremias; the Greek form of Jeremiah.
Jeremoth; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites, singers, and tribal leaders.
Jeremy; an English form of Jeremiah used in the Apocryphal style citation of the prophet.
Jeriah; a chief of the Hebronites in the days of David.
Jeribai; one of David's mighty men.
Jericho; the city whose walls fell before Israel in Joshua, later also prominent in Gospel narratives.
jericho.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell es Sultan.

definitions: Jeriel; a man of the tribe of Issachar in the genealogies.
Jerijah; a chief of the Hebronites in David's later administration.
Jerimoth; borne by several biblical men, especially among the sons of David, Levites, and princes.
Jerioth; a woman or family name associated with Caleb's house in the genealogies.
Jeroboam; the name of the kings of Israel so called, especially Jeroboam the son of Nebat.
Jeroham; borne by several biblical men, including Levites and the father of one branch of Samuel's line.
Jerubbaal; another name of Gideon, given after the overthrow of Baal's altar.
Jerubbesheth; older third-person singular form of 'jerubbeshs'.
Jeruel; the wilderness or district toward which Jehoshaphat's enemies advanced before their overthrow.
Jerusalem; the chief city of Judah and the place of the temple, sometimes standing for its people or worship.
jerusalem.note: Location: In the hill country of Judah, commonly identified with the historic city of Jerusalem.

definitions: Jerusha; the wife of king Uzziah and mother of Jotham, also written Jerushah.
Jerushah; the wife of king Uzziah and mother of Jotham, also written Jerusha.
Jesaiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites and descendants in Davidic lines.
Jeshaiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially among the descendants of Merari, Elam, and the royal line.
Jeshanah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jeshanah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Burj el Isaneh.

definitions: Jesharelah; a son of Asaph appointed in the courses of temple musicians, also written Asarelah.
Jeshebeab; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Jesher; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jeshimon; the desolate waste or wilderness tract near the Dead Sea.
jeshimon.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Zahrat al Kula.

definitions: Jeshishai; one of the sons of Anak in the narrative of the spies.
Jeshohaiah; a Simeonite leader in the genealogies.
Jeshua; especially the high priest who stood with Zerubbabel after the return from exile, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jeshurun; a poetical title for Israel, used as an affectionate or honourable name for the covenant people.
Jesiah; the name of men in Levitical and genealogical records; the name also appears in the form Isshiah.
Jesimiel; one appointed over the sons of the king in David's administration.
Jesse; the Bethlehemite father of David, from whose line the Messiah was promised.
Jesting; present participial form of 'jest', denoting ongoing action or process.
Jesui; an Asherite family name or clan designation, represented in related forms such as Isui and Ishuai.
Jesuites; descendants of Jesui reckoned among the families of Asher.
Jesurun; the form of Jeshurun used as a poetical title for Israel.
Jesus; the personal name of the Lord Jesus Christ, and in some passages the Greek form of Joshua.
Jether; borne by several biblical men, including kin of Gideon and members of David's family line.
Jetheth; older third-person singular form of 'jeths'.
Jethlah; a town on the border of Dan.
jethlah.note: Location: In the Shephelah or lower hill country west of Benjamin, near the Danite border.

definitions: Jethro; a man named in Scripture, father of Zipporah.
Jetur; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of the Ituraean line.
Jeuel; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and heads of returning families.
Jeush; borne by several biblical men, especially sons in the houses of Esau, Benjamin, and Rehoboam.
Jeuz; a son in the line of Esau and another in the house of Caleb.
Jew; a member of the Jewish people, especially of the covenant nation descended from Judah or identified with the people of Israel after the exile.
Jewel; a precious ornament or gem of value and beauty.
A plural form referring to more than one jewel.
A plural form referring to more than one jewes.
Jewish; belonging to the Jews, their people, religion, or customs.
jewish.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Jewry; the Jewish people or their region and community as described in older English.
jewry.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Jews; the people of Judah and later the covenant nation known by that name, especially in the post-exilic and New Testament period.
Jezaniah; the name of men in Scripture, including a captain in the troubled days after Jerusalem's fall, represented in related forms such as Jaazaniah.
Jezebel; a woman named in Scripture, daughter of Ethbaal, wife of Ahab, mother of Ahaziah.
Jezer; a son of Naphtali from whom the Jezerites were reckoned.
Jezerites; descendants of Jezer reckoned among the families of Gilead and Manasseh.
Jeziah; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jeziel; the son of Azmaveth, associated with Saul's kindred and the Benjamites.
Jezliah; the name of a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jezoar; one named among the sons of Asaph or temple singers in post-exilic records.
Jezrahiah; an overseer in the thanksgiving order at the dedication in Nehemiah's day.
Jezreel; a city and valley name tied to royal history, Naboth's vineyard, and prophetic judgment.
Jezreelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Jezreel in Scripture.
jezreelite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yizre’el.

definitions: Jezreelitess; Ahinoam, one of David's wives, so called because she was from Jezreel.
jezreelitess.note: Location: From Jezreel; in context this usually refers to the Judean Jezreel in the southern hill country.

definitions: Jibsam; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jidlaph; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Jimna; the name of Asherite ancestors and related clan records; it also appears in the forms Jimnah and Imnah.
Jimnah; the name of Asherite ancestors and related family lines; it also appears in the forms Jimna and Imnah.
Jimnites; descendants of Jimna reckoned among the families of Asher.
Jiphtah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jiphthah-El; a valley named in the tribal boundary descriptions.
jiphthah-el.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Wadi el Melek, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Joab; the captain of David's host.
Joah; borne by several biblical men, including recorders, Levites, and fathers in royal service.
Joahaz; a son of Josiah listed in the genealogies of the kings of Judah.
Joanna; the wife of Chuza who ministered to Jesus and was a witness of the resurrection tidings.
Joash; especially the king of Judah preserved from Athaliah and later active in temple repair, and also the name of other biblical men.
Joatham; a man named in Scripture, son of Azariah, father of Ahaz.
Job; the righteous sufferer of the book of Job, famed for endurance under trial.
Jobab; borne by several biblical men, including kings and descendants in early genealogies.
Jochebed; past tense or past participial form of 'jocheb'.
Joed; past tense or past participial form of 'jo'.
Joel; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also several other men of Israel bearing the same name.
Joelah; one of the warriors who came to David at Ziklag.
Joezer; one of the Benjamite archers who came to David.
Jogbehah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jogbehah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Jubeiha.

definitions: Jogli; the father of Bukki, prince of the tribe of Dan.
Joha; the name of men in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Johanan; the name of many men in Scripture, especially priests, captains, and leaders after the exile.
John; the name of John the Baptist, John the apostle, or another person of that name according to context.
Joiada; the name of men in Scripture, including a chief of the Aaronites in David's day.
Joiakim; the post-exilic high priest, son of Jeshua, in the days of Nehemiah.
Joiarib; the name of priestly heads and family lines in Israel.
Join; to unite, cleave to, attach oneself, or bring together.
Joined; past tense or past participial form of 'join'.
Joining; present participial form of 'join', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one joining.
Joint; the place where parts meet, especially bones or members of the body.
A plural form referring to more than one joint heir.
A plural form referring to more than one joint.
Jokdeam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jokdeam.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet er Raqqa.

definitions: Jokim; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jokmeam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jokmeam.note: Location: Often identified with Tel al Mazar.

definitions: Jokneam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jokneam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yokneam.

definitions: Jokshan; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Joktan; a son of Eber and ancestor of a wide southern line in Genesis 10.
Joktheel; a Judahite town later renamed by Amaziah after his victory over Edom.
joktheel.note: Location: In the Shephelah or southern hill-country region of Judah, often linked with the Lachish area.

definitions: Jona; the form used in the Gospels for the name Jonah or Jonas, especially in the patronymic of Simon Bar-jona.
Jonadab; the name of notable men in Scripture, especially the subtle son of Shimeah and Jehonadab son of Rechab.
Jonah; son of Amittai, the prophet sent to Nineveh.
Jonan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jonas; the Greek form of Jonah, and also the name used for the father of Peter in the Gospels.
Jonathan; especially the son of Saul, though the name is borne by others according to context.
Joppa; the coastal port associated with Jonah, the cedar shipments for the temple, and Peter's vision in Acts.
joppa.note: Location: On the Mediterranean coast, commonly identified with Jaffa at modern Tel Aviv-Yafo.

definitions: Jorah; the name attached to a returning family in the exile lists; the same family appears under the form Hariph.
Jorai; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Joram; a shortened form of Jehoram, used especially of kings in Israel and Judah.
Jordan; the chief river of the land, flowing through the great valley from the north toward the Dead Sea.
The name often marks a boundary or crossing-place in Israelite history.
jordan.note: Location: The river valley running from the north of the land down to the Dead Sea.

definitions: Jorim; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jorkoam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
jorkoam.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet er Raqqa.

definitions: Josabad; a Levite name in the records of temple service and restoration.
Josaphat; the Greek form of Jehoshaphat, especially the king of Judah.
Jose; a form of Joseph borne by men in the New Testament family records.
Josedech; the Greek form of Jehozadak.
Joseph; especially the son of Jacob, though the name is borne by others according to context.
Joses; borne by several biblical men, including one of the brethren named with Jesus and also Barnabas under another form.
Joshah; a Simeonite prince in the genealogies.
Joshaphat; a form of Jehoshaphat used for men in genealogical or administrative records.
Joshaviah; a son of Elnaam and one of David's mighty men.
Joshbekashah; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Joshua; the son of Nun, successor of Moses, and also others of that name according to context.
Josiah; king of Judah who led a notable reform and renewed the covenant after the book of the law was found.
Josias; the Greek form of Josiah king of Judah.
Josibiah; a Simeonite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Josiphiah; the father of Shelomith in Ezra's list.
Jot; the smallest letter or least stroke, hence the least part.
Jotbah; the home of Meshullemeth, mother of king Amon of Judah.
jotbah.note: Location: In Judah, likely in the southern hill country.

definitions: Jotbath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jotbathah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Jotham; especially the king of Judah son of Uzziah, and also the name of other biblical men.
A journey is travel from one place to another; to journey is to travel.
Journeyed; past tense or past participial form of 'journey'.
Traveling; going on a journey or course from place to place.
A plural form referring to more than one journeying.
A plural form referring to more than one journey.
Joy; gladness, delight, or rejoicing.
Joyed; past tense or past participial form of 'joy'.
Full of joy; glad and rejoicing.
In a joyful manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being joyfulnes.
Joying; present participial form of 'joy', denoting ongoing action or process.
Full of joy; glad.
Jozabad; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and captains in the days of Joash and Josiah.
Jozachar; one of the servants who conspired against king Joash, elsewhere represented by the related name Zabad.
Jozadak; the form of the name Jehozadak used for the father of Jeshua the high priest.
Jubal; the early ancestor of those who handle the harp and organ.
Jubile; the Jubilee year of release and restoration in Israel.
Jucal; the form of the name Jehucal used for the son of Shelemiah, one of the princes in Jeremiah's time.
Juda; the Greek form of Judah, used of the patriarch, the tribe descended from him, and others bearing that name in Scripture.
Judaea; the southern region of the land associated with Judah and Jerusalem.
In New Testament usage it may denote the wider province under Roman rule or the Jewish land more generally according to context.
judaea.note: Location: The southern district of the land around Jerusalem and Bethlehem, corresponding broadly to ancient Judah.

definitions: Judah; the son of Jacob, the tribe descended from him, or the southern kingdom bearing that name.
In some passages the name may extend to the land, people, or royal line identified with that tribe.
Judas; the Greek form of Judah, used of several men in Scripture, especially Judas Iscariot, Judas Barsabas, Judas of Damascus, and Judas the brother of James.
Jude; the New Testament form of Judas, especially used of the servant of Jesus Christ and brother of James who wrote the Epistle of Jude.
Judea; the southern Jewish province or region centered on Jerusalem in the New Testament period.
judea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: To judge is to discern, decide, examine, or pronounce sentence.
As a noun, a judge is one appointed to decide causes, and in some contexts one who governs or delivers Israel.
Those who judge; rulers or officers appointed to decide causes.
Also the book title or governing deliverers of Israel, according to context.
Judgest; thou judgest; you judge, decide, or pronounce upon.
Judging; present participial form of 'judge', denoting ongoing action or process.
Sentence, decision, or act of judging.
Also justice, right order, wise discernment, or the punishment awarded by authority, according to context.
Judith; an Hittite wife of Esau, named among the women who grieved Isaac and Rebekah.
Juice; liquid pressed out from fruit or plant substance.
Julia; a Christian woman greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Julius; the centurion of Augustus' band who escorted Paul toward Rome.
Jumping; present participial form of 'jump', denoting ongoing action or process.
Junia; a Christian greeted by Paul among his kinsfolk and fellow prisoners.
Juniper; a shrub or tree so named in Scripture, noted especially as a plant of the wilderness.
Jupiter; the Roman chief god, identified by the people of Lystra with Barnabas in Acts.
authority to judge, govern, or exercise power.

definitions: To guard, preserve, observe, or retain.

Levy; an imposed tax, tribute, or body of men raised for service.
