Lewd; wicked, base, or morally corrupt; in some contexts simply vile or lawless.
In a lewd manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being lewdnes.
Liar; one who speaks falsehood or denies truth deceitfully.
A plural form referring to more than one liar.
Bountiful; generous; free in giving.
generosity, openhandedness, or largeness of mind.
A plural form referring to more than one libertin.
Freedom from restraint or bondage.
Often lawful freedom under right order, not lawless license.
Libnah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
libnah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Bornat.

definitions: Libni; the name of Levites in Scripture, especially in the line of Gershon.
Libnites; descendants of Libni reckoned among the Levitical families.
Libya; the North African region west of Egypt, especially in connection with Cyrene.
libya.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one libyan.
libyans.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: Lice; the small parasitic insects of the plague in Egypt.
permission, liberty, or formal leave.
Lick; to pass the tongue over, or in some contexts to strike down or consume.
As a noun, a passing touch with the tongue.
Licked; past tense or past participial form of 'lick'.
Licketh; older third-person singular form of 'lick'.
Lid; a cover for a vessel, or an eyelid by context.
To lie stretched out at full length; to lie flat.
To continue pressing upon or hanging over, especially of a storm.
To project outward; to jut or extend beyond the rest.
Lied; past tense or past participial form of 'li'.
Lien; in older usage, the line, side, or body form of a person; elsewhere a retained claim depending on context.
A plural form referring to more than one lier.
A plural form referring to more than one ly.
Liest; older second-person singular form of 'li', used with 'thou'.
Lies; rests; is laid down.
It may also mean remains in a certain state, is situated, or lies concealed.
A plural form referring to more than one lieutenant.
Life; living existence; the course or condition of one who lives.
Lifetime; the duration of a person's life.
Raised up, taken up, or exalted.
Lifter; one who lifts or raises up.
Liftest; thou liftest; you lift, raise up, or exalt.
Lifts; raises up; exalts.
Lifting; present participial form of 'lift', denoting ongoing action or process.
Light; brightness, illumination, or that which makes things visible; also spiritual light according to context.
Lighted; past tense or past participial form of 'light'.
Lighten; to make light, relieve, illuminate, or flash according to context.
Lightened; past tense or past participial form of 'lighten'.
Lighteneth; older third-person singular form of 'lighten'.
Lighter; a small vessel used to convey goods, especially to or from a ship.
Also comparative of light, more light in weight or brightness.
Lightest; older second-person singular form of 'light', used with 'thou'.
Lighteth; older third-person singular form of 'lights'.
Lighting; present participial form of 'light', denoting ongoing action or process.
In a light manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being lightnes.
Lightning; present participial form of 'lightn', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one lightning.
A plural form referring to more than one light.
Lign; wood, especially in the phrase lign aloes, meaning fragrant aloes wood.
An aromatic wood, prized for fragrance.
a precious stone named in the high priest’s breastplate.
Like; similar, resembling, or such as.
Liked; past tense or past participial form of 'like'.
Of one mind; sharing the same inward disposition.
Liken; to compare, make like, or represent as similar.
Likened; past tense or past participial form of 'liken'.
Resemblance, similarity, or form answering to another thing.
Sometimes an image or representation.
Liketh; older third-person singular form of 'like'.
In like manner; in the same way.
Likhi; a Manassite name in the genealogies.
Liking; present participial form of 'like', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one lily.
In a li manner or degree.
Lime; a calcareous substance used in mortar or plaster, and in some contexts a sticky substance for catching birds.
Limit; boundary, appointed measure, or utmost extent.
As a verb, to bound or restrict.
Limited; past tense or past participial form of 'limit'.
Limiteth; older third-person singular form of 'limit'.
A cord, measuring line, or stretched thread.
Also a boundary, portion, or line of descent according to context.
descent, ancestry, or family line.
Linen; cloth made from flax, often noted for fine or sacred garments.
Lingered; past tense or past participial form of 'linger'.
Lingereth; older third-person singular form of 'lingers'.
Lintel; the upper beam or crosspiece of a doorway.
A plural form referring to more than one lintel.
Linus; a Christian who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul.
A lion; the beast so called, often used figuratively for strength, danger, or royalty.
The state, quality, or condition of being liones.
A plural form referring to more than one lioness.
Lionlike; like a lion in courage, fierceness, or appearance.
A plural form referring to more than one lion.
The lip is the edge of the mouth.
In Scripture lips often stand for speech, confession, praise, or deceit according to context.
Lips; the parts of the mouth, and by extension speech or profession.
Liquor; liquid substance, fluid, or drink depending on context.
A plural form referring to more than one liquor.
Listed; past tense or past participial form of 'list'.
Listen; to give ear, attend, or hearken.
Listeth; older third-person singular form of 'lists'.
A plural form referring to more than one litter.
Little; small in size, amount, age, or importance.
Lived; past tense or past participial form of 'liv'.
Living; full of life and active power.
Often simply 'living' in older usage.
Liver; the inward organ of the body so called, and in sacrificial language a part taken with the offerings.
A plural form referring to more than one liv.
Livest; older second-person singular form of 'liv', used with 'thou'.
Lives; remains alive; continues in life.
In some places, lives in a certain manner or under a certain rule.
Alive; having life.
Sometimes livelihood or means of support, according to context.
Lizard; a small creeping reptile named among the creatures of Scripture.
Look; behold; a word calling attention.
Lo-Ammi; a symbolic name or title used in Scripture.
Lo-Debar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
lo-debar.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Dover.

definitions: Lo-Ruhamah; a symbolic name or title used in Scripture.
Loaden; laden or burdened.
Older participial form of load.
Loadeth; older third-person singular form of 'loads'.
A loaf is a formed mass of bread baked for food.
Loan; something lent, or the act of lending.
To loathe is to feel disgust toward, abhor, or regard with strong aversion.
Loatheth; older third-person singular form of 'loathe'.
Loathsome; hateful, disgusting, or fit to be abhorred.
A plural form referring to more than one loav.
Lock; a fastening, or a tress of hair depending on context.
As a verb, to fasten or shut with a lock.
Locked; past tense or past participial form of 'lock'.
A plural form referring to more than one lock.
Locust; an old second-person singular form of locu, used with thou.
A plural form referring to more than one locust.
Lod; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Lodge; to stay for a night, dwell temporarily, or be laid up in a place.
As a noun, a temporary shelter or abode.
Lodged; past tense or past participial form of 'lodge'.
Lodgest; older second-person singular form of 'lodge', used with 'thou'.
Lodgeth; older third-person singular form of 'lodge'.
Lodging; present participial form of 'lodge', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one lodging.
Loft; an upper room, raised chamber, or height.
In a lofti manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being loftines.
Lofty; high, exalted, proud, or elevated in place or spirit.
Log; a block or cut portion of wood, and in some contexts a small measure.
The lower part of the body about the hips and reins.
Often used figuratively for strength, readiness, or posterity.
Lois; the grandmother of Timothy, remembered for sincere faith.
Long; extended in measure or duration.
Longed; past tense or past participial form of 'long'.
Longedst; an old second-person singular form of long, used with thou.
Longer; more long, or for a greater time.
Longeth; older third-person singular form of 'long'.
Longing; present participial form of 'long', denoting ongoing action or process.
Patient endurance under provocation; slowness to anger.
Longwinged; past tense or past participial form of 'longwing'.
Looked; beheld, regarded, or turned the eyes toward.
Lookest; thou lookest; you look or behold.
At times it also means you regard or take notice of.
Looks; beholds; directs the eyes toward.
At times it also means to regard, attend to, or expect.
Looking; present participial form of 'look', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one lookingglass.
A plural form referring to more than one look.
A plural form referring to more than one loop.
Loose; free, unbound, unfastened, or not fixed.
As a verb, to release or untie.
Loosed; past tense or past participial form of 'loose'.
Looseth; older third-person singular form of 'loose'.
Loosing; present participial form of 'loose', denoting ongoing action or process.
Lop; to cut off branches or cut down projecting parts.
One having authority, rule, or ownership.
In Scripture often a title of dignity for men, and supremely the divine title of the Lord himself.
In a lord manner or degree.
dominion, authority, or the rank of a lord.
Lose; to forfeit, fail to keep, be deprived of, or destroy.
Loseth; older third-person singular form of 'loss'.
A plural form referring to more than one los.
Lost; an old second-person singular form of lo, used with thou.
Lot; the son of Haran and nephew of Abraham, and also a lot cast or portion assigned according to context.
Lotan; a Horite chief in the genealogies of Seir.
Lothe; older form of loathe, meaning to dislike greatly or abhor.
Lothed; past tense or past participial form of 'lothe'.
Lotheth; older third-person singular form of 'lothe'.
Lothing; present participial form of 'lothe', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one lot.
Loud; sounding strongly or with great voice.
Louder; more loud in sound or utterance.
Love; affection, goodwill, or devoted regard.
Loved; had affection for or showed goodwill toward.
Lovedst; an old second-person singular form of lov, used with thou.
In a love manner or degree.
Lover; one who loves, and in some contexts one joined in unlawful or passionate attachment.
A plural form referring to more than one lover.
A plural form referring to more than one lov.
Lovest; thou lovest; you love, delight in, or bear affection toward.
Loves; bears affection toward; delights in.
In Scripture often includes settled goodwill, covenant kindness, or holy delight rather than mere feeling.
Loving; present participial form of 'love', denoting ongoing action or process.
Steadfast and tender covenant love expressed in mercy and favor.
A plural form referring to more than one lovingkindness.
Low; not high, humble, faint, or reduced according to context.
Lower; less high, beneath, or of an inferior place.
As a verb, to bring down or let fall.
Lowest; least high, least exalted, or most humble among those compared.
Loweth; older third-person singular form of 'low'.
Lowing; present participial form of 'low', denoting ongoing action or process.
Humility; a lowly mind free from proud self-exaltation.
In a low manner or degree.
Lowring; present participial form of 'lowr', denoting ongoing action or process.
Lubim; a North African people associated with Libya.
lubim.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: Lubims; the Libyan people named among the nations allied with Egypt.
Lucas; the Greek form of Luke, the beloved physician and companion of Paul.
Lucifer; the day-star figure used in Isaiah's taunt against the king of Babylon.
Lucius; the name of New Testament men, including one at Antioch and one greeted by Paul.
Lucre; gain or profit, often with the sense of greedy or dishonest gain.
Lud; a son in the early genealogies, associated with a people bearing his name.
Ludim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Luhith; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Luke; the beloved physician and companion of Paul, traditionally linked with the third Gospel and Acts.
Lukewarm; moderately warm, and figuratively half-hearted or lacking zeal.
Lump; a compact mass, especially of dough or material not yet divided.
Lunatick; one afflicted with what was thought to vary with the moon, especially severe fits or mental disturbance.
As an adjective, so affected.
Lurk; to lie hidden, remain in secret, or wait concealed.
Lurking; present participial form of 'lurk', denoting ongoing action or process.
Strong desire or craving.
Often a corrupt and unlawful desire, though the bare sense is broader.
Lusted; past tense or past participial form of 'lust'.
Lusts; strongly desires, often in a corrupt or unlawful way.
Lusting; present participial form of 'lust', denoting ongoing action or process.
Lusty; strong, vigorous, or full of force.
Luz; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Lycaonia; an inland region of Asia Minor including places such as Lystra and Derbe.
Lycia; a coastal region in southwestern Asia Minor.
Lydda; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Lydia; the seller of purple of Thyatira who first received Paul at Philippi.
A plural form referring to more than one lydian.
lydians.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Sardis.

definitions: Lying; present participial form of 'ly', denoting ongoing action or process.
Lysanias; the tetrarch of Abilene named by Luke.
A plural form referring to more than one lysia.
Lystra; a city of Lycaonia associated with Paul’s preaching, persecution, and the early life of Timothy.
It is remembered in Acts as a place both of miracle and opposition.
lystra.note: Location: In the interior of Asia Minor in Lycaonia, commonly identified near Hatunsaray in Turkey.

definitions: Maacah; borne by several biblical persons, especially women in royal or family lines; the name also appears in the form Maachah.
Maachah; borne by several biblical persons, especially a royal mother and a woman of David's family, and also the name of a region associated with Aram.
Maachathi; belonging to Maachah or the people of Maachah.
Maachathite; one belonging to Maachah or the Maachathite territory.
maachathite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one maachathit.
maachathites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Maadai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Maadiah; a priestly name in the post-exilic records, related to the forms Maaziah and Moadiah.
Maai; a priest or musician named in the thanksgiving order at Jerusalem.
Maaleh-Acrabbim; a named place or feature mentioned in Scripture.
maaleh-acrabbim.note: Location: Commonly placed at or identified with Ma’ale Akrabbim.

definitions: Maarath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
maarath.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Qufin.

definitions: Maaseiah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially priests, Levites, officers, and post-exilic leaders.
Maasiai; a priestly or Levitical name in the post-exilic records, elsewhere represented by the form Amashai.
Maath; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Maaz; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Maaziah; the name of a priestly course and of post-exilic priests associated with that house.
Macedonia; the northern Greek region into which the gospel first passed in Europe through Paul’s call.
The name may denote the district itself or the churches found there.
macedonia.note: Location: The northern Greek region above Achaia, extending over much of what is now northern Greece and nearby areas.

definitions: Macedonian; belonging to Macedonia or one of its people.
Machbanai; a Gadite warrior who came to David in the wilderness.
Machbenah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
machbenah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Hebra.

definitions: Machi; the father of Geuel, the spy from the tribe of Gad.
Machir; the son of Manasseh from whom a leading clan descended east of Jordan.
Machirites; the clan descended from Machir within the house of Manasseh.
Machnadebai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Machpelah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
machpelah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Cave of the Patriarchs.

definitions: Mad; furious, beside oneself, wildly excited, or mentally disordered according to context.
Madai; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with the Medes.
The past tense or participle of make; formed, done, or caused to be.
Acted as if; gave the appearance of doing so.
Madest; thou madest; you made, formed, or caused.
Madian; the Greek or alternate form of Midian, the desert land associated with Midianite tribes.
madian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Al Bad.

definitions: Madmannah; a southern town in the territory of Judah and Simeon.
madmannah.note: Location: In the Negev south of Judah, in the Beersheba region.

definitions: Madmen; a Moabite place named in Jeremiah's oracle of judgment.
madmen.note: Location: In the region of Moab east of the Dead Sea.

definitions: Madmenah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
The state, quality, or condition of being madnes.
Madon; a northern Canaanite royal city whose king joined the confederacy defeated by Joshua.
madon.note: Location: In Upper Galilee or the far north of Canaan.

definitions: Magbish; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
magbish.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Makhbiyeh.

definitions: Magdala; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
magdala.note: Location: Sometimes identified with eastern side of the Sea of Galilee.

definitions: Magdalene; a designation meaning a woman of Magdala, used especially of Mary Magdalene.
Magdiel; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Magician; one skilled in magical arts, enchantments, or secret arts.
A plural form referring to more than one magician.
Magistrate; a civil ruler, judge, or public officer exercising authority.
A plural form referring to more than one magistrat.
magnificent or splendid.
grandeur, splendor, or stately excellence.
To make great in esteem, praise, or apparent importance.
Magog; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with northern peoples in biblical prophecy.
Magor-Missabib; a symbolic title meaning terror on every side, given by Jeremiah to Pashur.
Magpiash; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Mahalah; a daughter of Ishmael in the patriarchal genealogies.
Mahalaleel; an early patriarch in the line from Seth to Noah.
Mahalath; the name of women in the family lines of Ishmael and David.
Mahali; the Greek or alternate form of Mahli in the Levitical genealogies.
Mahanaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mahanaim.note: Location: Often identified with Tell edh Dhahab el Gharbi.

definitions: Mahaneh-Dan; the camp of Dan associated with the movements of Samson and the Danites.
mahaneh-dan.note: Location: Between Zorah and Eshtaol on the border of Dan and Judah.

definitions: Maharai; one of David's mighty men, a Netophathite.
Mahath; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites named in the histories of temple service and reform.
Mahavite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mahav in Scripture.
Mahazioth; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Maher-Shalal-Hash-Baz; the symbolic name of Isaiah's son, meaning swift the spoil, speedy the prey.
Mahlah; one of the daughters of Zelophehad.
Mahli; a Merarite Levite and the name of the family descending from him.
Mahlites; descendants of Mahli reckoned among the Levitical families.
Mahlon; the son of Elimelech and Naomi, husband of Ruth.
Mahol; the father of Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Darda in the wisdom comparison with Solomon.
A maid; a young woman or girl, and in some contexts a female servant.
The exact force depends on whether the passage emphasizes age, unmarried condition, or service.
Maiden; a young unmarried woman or virgin.
A plural form referring to more than one maiden.
Maids; young women, girls, or female servants according to context.
A maidservant; a female servant or bondwoman attached to a household.
The word emphasizes service and dependence rather than mere youth.
Maidservants; female servants attached to a household.
Mail; armour of linked metal or defensive covering.
Maimed; past tense or past participial form of 'maim'.
Mainsail; the principal sail of a ship.
Maintain; to keep up, support, defend, or continue in force.
Maintained; past tense or past participial form of 'maintain'.
Maintainest; older second-person singular form of 'maintain', used with 'thou'.
support, provision, or the act of upholding.
Greatness, royal dignity, and exalted splendor.
Makaz; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
makaz.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Deir Muheisin.

definitions: Maker; one who makes, forms, or does, and supremely the Creator in many passages.
A plural form referring to more than one maker.
Makest; thou makest; you make, form, or cause.
Makes; forms; causes; brings about.
Makheloth; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
makheloth.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route, often associated with the northern Sinai approach toward the Arabah.

definitions: Making; present participial form of 'mak', denoting ongoing action or process.
Makkedah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
makkedah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Qom.

definitions: Maktesh; a district mentioned in Scripture.
Malachi; the prophet through whom the last book of the Old Testament is given.
Malcham; a form associated with the Ammonite deity Molech or Milcom in prophetic usage.
Malchi-Shua; a son of Saul slain in battle on Mount Gilboa.
Malchiah; borne by several biblical men, especially priests, builders, and officials in the days of Nehemiah and Jeremiah.
Malchiel; a son of Beriah in the tribe of Asher.
Malchielites; descendants of Malchiel reckoned among the families of Asher.
Malchijah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially priests, builders, and royal officials.
Malchiram; a son in the royal line of Jeconiah.
Malchus; the servant of the high priest whose ear Peter cut off.
Male; of the sex that begets, whether of man or beast.
Malefactor; an evildoer or criminal.
A plural form referring to more than one malefactor.
Maleleel; the Greek or alternate form of Mahalaleel, the patriarch in the line from Seth before the flood.
A plural form referring to more than one mal.
Ill will, spite, or settled desire to injure.
Moved by malice; spiteful or evil in intent.
The state, quality, or condition of being maliciousnes.
deep ill will, harmful tendency, or extreme evil.
Mallothi; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
A plural form referring to more than one mallow.
Malluch; borne by several biblical men, especially priestly or post-exilic figures.
Mammon; riches or wealth regarded as an object of trust or devotion.
Mamre; the Amorite ally of Abram associated with the oaks near Hebron.
A man; a human being, or a male person in particular, according to context.
A warrior; a fighting man; a soldier.
Manaen; a Christian teacher at Antioch, brought up with Herod the tetrarch.
Manahath; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Manahethites; a family or settlement group associated with Manahath in Judah.
Manasseh; the son of Joseph, the tribe descended from him, or the king of Judah so named according to context.
Manasses; the Greek form of Manasseh, used especially of Joseph's son and of the king of Judah.
Manassites; members of the tribe of Manasseh.
A plural form referring to more than one mandrak.
Maneh; a weight or monetary measure, related to the mina.
Manger; a feeding trough for cattle or beasts.
Plainly shown; openly revealed; made evident.
the act of making plain, visible, or evident.
Manifold; many in number, varied in kind, or diverse.
Mankind; the human race, humankind collectively.
Manna; the bread from heaven given to Israel in the wilderness, and figuratively divine nourishment.
Way, kind, fashion, or custom of doing or being.
Manoah; a man named in Scripture, father of Samson.
A male servant or bondman.
A plural form referring to more than one manservant.
A plural form referring to more than one mansion.
Manslayer; one who kills another person, especially in the legal sense addressed by the cities of refuge.
A plural form referring to more than one manslayer.
Mantle; a cloak, outer garment, or covering thrown over the shoulders.
A plural form referring to more than one mantl.
Many; numerous in number.
Maoch; father of Achish king of Gath.
Maon; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place-name.
A plural form referring to more than one maonit.
maonites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Main.

definitions: Mar; to spoil, injure, deface, or make less perfect.
Mara; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Elimelech, mother of Chilion.
Marah; a sea or body of water mentioned in Scripture.
marah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Hawarah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Maralah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
maralah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Thorah.

definitions: Maranatha; an Aramaic expression meaning the Lord comes or our Lord, come.
Marble; a hard polished stone used in building or ornament.
March; to go forward in order, especially as troops.
As a noun, an ordered movement or march.
Marched; past tense or past participial form of 'march'.
Marchedst; an old second-person singular form of march, used with thou.
Marcus; a man named in Scripture.
Mareshah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mareshah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Maresha.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one mariner.
A plural form referring to more than one marish.
To observe closely, note, or take heed of.
Also a sign, token, or target.
Markest; older second-person singular form of 'mark', used with 'thou'.
Market; the place of buying and selling, or the trade carried on there.
Marketh; older third-person singular form of 'marks'.
Marketplace; a public place where goods are bought and sold.
A plural form referring to more than one marketplac.
A plural form referring to more than one market.
A plural form referring to more than one mark.
Maroth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
maroth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Qufin.

definitions: Marred; past tense or past participial form of 'mar'.
the covenant state or union of husband and wife.
A plural form referring to more than one marriag.
Married; past tense or past participial form of 'marry'.
Marrieth; older third-person singular form of 'marry'.
Marrow; the soft substance within bones, and figuratively the richest or choicest part.
Marry; to take in marriage, give in marriage, or become joined as husband and wife.
Marrying; present participial form of 'marry', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one mar.
Marsena; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
Mart; a market or place of trade.
Martha; the sister of Mary and Lazarus in Bethany, known from the Gospel accounts of her service and faith.
Martyr; a witness, and especially one who suffers or dies for the truth.
A plural form referring to more than one martyr.
Marvel; a wonder or cause of astonishment.
As a verb, to wonder or be amazed.
Marvelled; past tense or past participial form of 'marvell'.
Wonderful, astonishing, or beyond ordinary expectation.
In a marvellous manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one marvel.
The name of several women in the New Testament, especially Mary the mother of Jesus and Mary Magdalene.
Mash; a son of Aram in the table of nations, elsewhere connected with the form Meshech in some lists.
Mashal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mashal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Masons; workers in stone and builders in that craft.
Masrekah; an Edomite royal city named in the genealogy of the kings of Edom.
masrekah.note: Location: In Edom south or southeast of the Dead Sea, in the hill country toward the Arabah.

definitions: Massa; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a people or territory bearing the name.
Massah; the place of testing where Israel tempted the Lord for water.
massah.note: Location: In the wilderness on Israel's early route from Egypt, traditionally associated with the Sinai region.

definitions: Mast; an old second-person singular form of ma, used with thou.
A master; one who has authority over servants, disciples, or a household.
Masterbuilder; a chief builder, and figuratively one who lays a chief foundation.
A plural form referring to more than one mastery.
Masters; those who possess authority over servants, disciples, households, or workmen according to context.
Mastery; rule, superiority, skill, or self-control.
A plural form referring to more than one mast.
Mate; a companion, fellow, or equal.
As a verb, to join, pair, or match.
Mathusala; the Greek form of Methuselah.
Matred; past tense or past participial form of 'matr'.
Matri; the ancestor of the family from which Saul was taken by lot.
The womb.
Often used in the phrase 'openeth the matrix' for the firstborn issuing from the womb.
Mattan; the priest of Baal slain in the reform under Jehoiada, and also a name in the genealogy of Christ.
Mattanah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mattanah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Mattaniah; especially the king of Judah whose name was changed to Zedekiah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Mattatha; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mattathah; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's singers and one in Luke's genealogy.
Mattathias; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mattenai; the name of several post-exilic men in Ezra's records.
A matter; a thing, cause, business, affair, or case.
A plural form referring to more than one matter.
Matthan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Matthew.
Matthat; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Matthew; the apostle and evangelist, also called Levi, whose name stands at the head of the first Gospel.
Matthias; the disciple chosen to take the place of Judas among the twelve.
Mattithiah; the name of many Levites, singers, gatekeepers, and post-exilic figures in Scripture.
Mattock; a digging or grubbing tool like a hoe or pick.
A plural form referring to more than one mattock.
Maul; a heavy hammer or club used for striking.
Maw; the stomach or inward part used in eating, especially of animals.
To have permission or possibility; perhaps to happen.
May; art permitted or enabled to.
Often expresses permission, possibility, or fitness rather than certainty.
Mazzaroth; a term in Job for the ordered constellations or starry signs of the heavens.
The objective form of the first-person singular pronoun.
It seems to me; in my judgment.
Me-Jarkon; the waters or river region associated with the Yarkon near the coast.
me-jarkon.note: Location: On the coastal plain near the mouth of the Yarkon River north of Joppa.

definitions: Meadow; grassland or low ground fit for pasture or growth.
A plural form referring to more than one meadow.
Meah; a tower-name in Jerusalem, likely linked with the idea of a hundred or a measured extent.
ground grain or flour; also food taken at one time.
Mealtime; the time appointed for eating.
Low in rank or outward state; humble or common.
Also to intend or signify, according to context.
Meanest; older second-person singular form of 'mean', used with 'thou'.
Means; intends; signifies.
Meaning; present participial form of 'mean', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one mean.
Past tense and past participial form of 'mean'.
Intended, signified, or designed.
Mearah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
A measure; a standard or quantity, and also to measure according to context.
Measured; past tense or past participial form of 'measure'.
A plural form referring to more than one measur.
Measuring; present participial form of 'measure', denoting ongoing action or process.
Food in general; nourishment.
Only sometimes flesh specifically.
Mebunnai; one of David's mighty men, commonly identified with Sibbecai.
Mecherathite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mecherath in Scripture.
Medad; one of the elders upon whom the Spirit rested in the wilderness.
Medan; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Meddle; to interfere, mix oneself in a matter, or busy oneself improperly with what does not belong to one.
Meddled; past tense or past participial form of 'meddle'.
Meddleth; older third-person singular form of 'meddle'.
Meddling; present participial form of 'meddle', denoting ongoing action or process.
Mede; one of the people of Media.
Medeba; a plateau city east of the Jordan frequently mentioned in the wars and prophecies concerning Moab.
medeba.note: Location: In the Moabite plateau east of the Dead Sea, at or near modern Madaba.

definitions: Medes; the people of Media, later linked with the Medo-Persian power.
Media; the land of the Medes east of Mesopotamia, later linked with the Medo-Persian power.
media.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell Hagmatana.

definitions: Median; a person of Media or something belonging to the Medes and their realm.
One who stands between parties to reconcile or transact on behalf of both.
Medicine; healing remedy, remedying art, or curative application.
A plural form referring to more than one medicin.
Meditate; to muse, ponder, devise, or speak to oneself thoughtfully according to context.
deep thought, reflection, or devout pondering.
Gentle, lowly, and under restraint rather than self-asserting.
Strength governed, not weakness without courage.
The state, quality, or condition of being meeknes.
Fit, suitable, or proper.
Not chiefly an encounter, though that sense exists in other forms.
Meetest; older second-person singular form of 'meet', used with 'thou'.
Meeteth; older third-person singular form of 'meet'.
Meeting; present participial form of 'meet', denoting ongoing action or process.
Megiddo; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
megiddo.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Megiddo.

definitions: Megiddon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
megiddon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Megiddo.

definitions: Mehetabeel; the wife of Hadar king of Edom.
Mehetabel; the name of women in Scripture, including one in the genealogy of Edom and one in Nehemiah.
Mehida; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Mehir; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Meholathite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Meholath in Scripture.
Mehujael; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Mehuman; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Mehunim; a named people or tribal group appearing in Judah's history and in post-exilic records, commonly linked with the Meunim.
Mehunims; a southern people or settlement group named in Old Testament warfare, reform, and genealogical records.
Mekonah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mekonah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tatrit.

definitions: Melatiah; the Gibeonite who helped repair the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Melchi; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Melchi-Shua; a spelling of Malchi-Shua, the son of Saul.
Melchiah; the form of the name Malchiah used of several men in priestly, royal, and rebuilding contexts.
Melchisedec; the Greek form of Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of the most high God.
Melchizedek; king of Salem and priest of the most high God, later used as a type of Christ's priesthood.
Melea; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Melech; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Melicu; a post-exilic priestly name, also written Malluch.
Melita; the island on which Paul was shipwrecked and shown kindness by the inhabitants.
Melody; sweetness of song or ordered musical sound.
A plural form referring to more than one melon.
Melted; past tense or past participial form of 'melt'.
Melts; dissolves, softens, or loses firmness.
It may also speak of the heart failing with fear or sorrow.
Melting; present participial form of 'melt', denoting ongoing action or process.
Melzar; the steward or overseer set over Daniel and his companions in Babylon.
Member; a part or limb of the body, or one part belonging to a whole.
A plural form referring to more than one member.
something preserving remembrance; also serving to remind.
The faculty of remembering, or remembrance kept in mind.
A plural form referring to more than one memphi.
Memucan; one of the princes who advised Ahasuerus concerning Vashti.
Men; persons of mankind, or males in particular, according to context.
Menahem; king of Israel noted for violence and for tribute paid to Assyria.
Menan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mend; to repair, make better, or restore.
Mending; present participial form of 'mend', denoting ongoing action or process.
Mene; the Aramaic word in Daniel's writing on the wall, signifying that God had numbered the kingdom and finished it.
A plural form referring to more than one menpleaser.
A plural form referring to more than one menservant.
A plural form referring to more than one menstealer.
Ceremonially unclean as in a woman's monthly impurity; defiled or rejected as unclean.
reference by word or name; also to refer to.
Mentioned; past tense or past participial form of 'mention'.
Meonenim; the oak or terebinth landmark near Shechem mentioned in early Israelite narrative.
meonenim.note: Location: Near Shechem in the central hill country, in the vicinity of modern Tell Balatah.

definitions: Meonothai; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Mephaath; a Levitical city on the eastern side of the Jordan in the territory of Reuben.
mephaath.note: Location: In the tableland east of the Dead Sea in the Reubenite-Moabite region, near modern Umm ar-Rasas.

definitions: Mephibosheth; older third-person singular form of 'mephiboshs'.
Merab; the elder daughter of Saul, first promised to David but given instead to Adriel.
Meraiah; the head of a priestly house in the days of Joiakim.
Meraioth; a priestly name in the ancestral line of the high priesthood.
Merari; a man named in Scripture, son of Levi, father of Mahali.
Merarites; the Levitical family descended from Merari, charged with structural parts of the tabernacle.
Merathaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Goods bought and sold; traffic in wares.
A merchant; one who trades in goods for gain.
In Scripture the word may point to commerce broadly or to a trader of wealth and traffic among nations.
Merchantmen; trading men or merchants, especially those engaged in commerce by sea or caravan.
Merchants; those who trade in goods for gain, often in wider commerce.
A plural form referring to more than one mercy.
Disposed to show mercy; compassionate toward misery or guilt.
Mercurius; the Roman god Mercury, identified by the people of Lystra with Paul in Acts.
Compassion shown to the miserable or guilty; pity joined with help or pardon.
When spoken of God, his gracious readiness to spare, forgive, and do good to the undeserving.
Mercyseat; the covering of the ark of the covenant, where atonement blood was sprinkled and where God signified his presence.
Mered; past tense or past participial form of 'mer'.
Meremoth; borne by several biblical men, especially priests or builders in the post-exilic records of Ezra and Nehemiah.
Meres; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
a son of Jonathan, also known as Mephibosheth.
Meribah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
meribah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Meribah-Kadesh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
meribah-kadesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Merodach; the Babylonian deity Marduk, named also in personal names and prophecy.
Merodach-Baladan; king of Babylon who sent to Hezekiah, also written Berodach-Baladan.
Merom; the waters or place of Merom in northern Canaan, remembered for Joshua's victory there.
Meronothite; a person from Meronoth, named especially in post-exilic building records.
meronothite.note: Location: From a place near the Benjamin-Jerusalem district, often linked with the Mizpah region.

definitions: Meroz; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
meroz.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Marus.

definitions: In a merri manner or degree.
Merry; cheerful, glad, lively, or in some contexts given to feasting or strong drink.
Merryhearted; past tense or past participial form of 'merryheart'.
Mesech; the Greek or alternate form of Meshech, a son of Japheth named in the table of nations.
Mesha; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Caleb and a king of Moab.
Meshach; a man named in Scripture.
Meshech; a son of Japheth and the people descending from him, and in prophecy a northern people named with Tubal.
Meshelemiah; a Korhite porter in the temple service, prominent among the gatekeepers in David's ordering.
Meshezabeel; a post-exilic ancestor associated with Pethahiah and the rebuilding records.
Meshillemith; a family ancestor named in the Benjamite or priestly genealogies, related to the form Meshillemoth.
Meshillemoth; the name of men in Scripture, especially among priests and leaders in temple or royal service.
Meshobab; a Simeonite prince in the tribal genealogies.
Meshullam; borne by several biblical men, especially post-exilic priests, Levites, and builders in the days of Ezra and Nehemiah.
Meshullemeth; older third-person singular form of 'meshullems'.
Mesobaite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Mesoba in Scripture.
mesobaite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Mesopotamia; the land between the rivers, the broad region lying between the Tigris and Euphrates.
mesopotamia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Harran.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one mes.
a communication sent from one to another.
A messenger; one sent with a charge, report, command, or tidings.
According to context the term may refer to a human envoy or one acting in a more solemn commission.
Messengers; persons sent with a charge, report, or command.
A plural form referring to more than one mess.
Messiah; the Anointed One, the promised deliverer and king, corresponding to the Greek Christ.
A plural form referring to more than one messia.
Met; the past tense of meet.
Mete; to measure, allot, or deal out.
As a noun in older usage, measure.
Meted; past tense or past participial form of 'met'.
Meteyard; a measuring rod or standard of measure.
Metheg-Ammah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
metheg-ammah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Methusael; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Methuselah; the long-lived patriarch in the line from Adam before the flood.
Meunim; a named people or tribal group appearing in Judah's wars and in the return lists, related to the Mehunim.
Mezahab; the father of Mehetabel in the Edomite royal genealogy.
Miamin; a priestly name in the temple courses and post-exilic lists, related to Mijamin and Miniamin.
Mibhar; the name associated with one of David's mighty men in Chronicles.
Mibsam; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Ishmael and a son of Simeon.
Mibzar; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Micah; especially the prophet who spoke in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Micaiah; a man named in Scripture, son of Imlah.
Mice; small rodents, mentioned both literally and in connection with the plague among the Philistines.
Micha; borne by several biblical men, a form related to the more familiar name Micah.
Michael; the name of several biblical men, and especially the archangel who contends on God's behalf.
Michah; a name used of several men in genealogical and historical records; it corresponds to the form Micah.
Michaiah; borne by several biblical persons, including the prophet also known as Micaiah and members of royal or priestly families.
Michal; especially Saul's daughter who became David's wife, and also the name of other women in Scripture by related form.
A plural form referring to more than one michma.
michmas.note: Location: Often identified with Mukhmas, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Michmash; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Michmethah; a ridge or height named in the tribal border descriptions.
michmethah.note: Location: In the hill country on the border of Ephraim and Manasseh near Shechem.

definitions: Michri; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Midday; noon, the middle of the day.
Middin; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
middin.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Abu Tabaq.

definitions: Middle; the midst, central part, or what lies between the extremes.
Middlemost; an old second-person singular form of middlemo, used with thou.
Midian; a man named in Scripture, son of Abram, father of Abidah.
Midianite; a member of the people of Midian.
midianite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Al Bad.

definitions: Midianites; the people descended from Midian, associated with the desert regions south and east of Canaan and often appearing as traders, neighbours, or enemies of Israel.
Midianitish; belonging to the Midianites or to Midian.
Midnight; the middle of the night.
The middle, center, or inner part among surrounding things.
Midwife; a woman who assists in childbirth.
A plural form referring to more than one midwiv.
Migdal-El; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
migdal-el.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Majdel Selem.

definitions: Migdal-Gad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
migdal-gad.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Mejdeleh.

definitions: Migdol; a frontier stronghold or tower-town, especially on Egypt's northeastern approach.
migdol.note: Location: On Egypt's northeastern frontier toward the Sinai route; more than one fortified site may have borne the name.

definitions: Might; strength, power, or force.
Mightest; thou mightest; you might, could, or would be able to.
Mightier; more mighty, stronger, or of greater power.
A plural form referring to more than one mighty.
Mightiest; older second-person singular form of 'mighty', used with 'thou'.
In a mighti manner or degree.
Mighty; strong, powerful, or valiant.
Migron; a place near Gibeah mentioned in Saul's time and in Isaiah's description of an invading march.
migron.note: Location: Near Gibeah and Michmash in Benjamin on the northern approach to Jerusalem.

definitions: Mijamin; a priestly name borne by a course and by men in the post-exilic temple records.
Mikloth; the name of men in Scripture, including a Benjamite ancestor and one set over a course in David's service.
Mikneiah; a Levite associated with the bearing of the ark and temple song.
Milalai; a priest present in the thanksgiving procession at Jerusalem.
Milcah; the wife of Nahor and also the daughter of Zelophehad.
Milch; giving milk, used of animals that are in milk.
Milcom; the god of the Ammonites, described in Scripture as the abomination of Ammon.
Mildew; a blighting or mould-like disease affecting grain, cloth, or surfaces.
Mile; a measure of distance, especially the Roman mile in New Testament context.
Miletum; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
A plural form referring to more than one miletu.
Milk; the food drawn from the breast or udder, and figuratively simple nourishment or tender instruction.
A mill is the apparatus or place used for grinding grain into meal.
Millet; a small grain used for food.
A plural form referring to more than one million.
Millo; a fortification, terrace, or filled structure associated especially with the stronghold in Jerusalem.
millo.note: Location: In the fortified area of ancient Jerusalem, associated with the City of David and the royal stronghold.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one mill.
A millstone is the stone used in a mill for grinding grain.
In Scripture it may also signify crushing weight or heavy judgment.
A plural form referring to more than one millston.
Mincing; present participial form of 'minc', denoting ongoing action or process.
Mind; understanding, intention, purpose, or inward disposition.
Minded; past tense or past participial form of 'mind'.
Remembering; paying heed; taking thought for.
Minding; present participial form of 'mind', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one mind.
Belonging to me; the first-person singular possessive form, often used absolutely or for emphasis.
Mingle; to mix together, join, or blend.
Mingled; past tense or past participial form of 'mingle'.
Miniamin; the name of men in Scripture, especially priests or officers in the days of Hezekiah and Nehemiah.
Minish; to diminish, lessen, or make smaller.
Minished; past tense or past participial form of 'minish'.
To serve, attend, or supply what is needed.
Whether in practical service or sacred office must be determined from the passage.
Ministering; present participial form of 'minister', denoting ongoing action or process.
Ministers; those who serve, attend, or carry out appointed work, whether in sacred or practical matters according to context.
service, ministry, or attendance in office.
Minni; a kingdom or district named in Jeremiah among those summoned against Babylon.
minni.note: Location: In the Armenian highlands or the region of ancient Mannai north or northeast of Mesopotamia.

definitions: Minnith; a place east of the Jordan noted in Jephthah's victory over Ammon and later for its wheat.
minnith.note: Location: In Ammonite territory east of the Jordan, in the plateau region near Rabbah.

definitions: A minstrel; a player or singer skilled in music.
In Scripture the term often points more to the musician than to the wandering entertainer of later usage.
Minstrels; musicians or singers skilled in music.
Mint; an aromatic herb named among the small garden herbs.
Miphkad; a gate or gateway mentioned in Scripture.
Miracle; a mighty work or sign beyond ordinary power, bearing divine witness.
A plural form referring to more than one miracl.
Mire; soft wet earth, mud, or figuratively defilement and trouble.
Miriam; the sister of Moses and Aaron, a prophetess in Israel.
Mirma; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Gladness, merriment, or festive joy.
Miry; full of mire, muddy, or foul with clay-like filth.
Miscarrying; present participial form of 'miscarry', denoting ongoing action or process.
Mischief; harm, trouble, injury, or wicked intent.
A plural form referring to more than one mischief.
Harmful, hurtful, or full of mischief.
Wretched, afflicted, or pitiable.
In a miserab manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one misery.
Misery; distress, wretchedness, or grievous affliction.
Misgab; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Mishael; especially the companion of Daniel later called Meshach, and also the name of other biblical men.
Mishal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mishal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Misham; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Misheal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
misheal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Mishma; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a line bearing his name.
Mishmannah; one of the Gadite warriors who came to David in the wilderness.
Mishraites; a family or clan named in the Judahite genealogies.
Mispar; a post-exilic person named in the list of those who returned from captivity.
Mispereth; older third-person singular form of 'mispers'.
Misrephoth-Maim; a place near the northern coast to which Joshua pursued the fleeing Canaanite kings.
misrephoth-maim.note: Location: In the far north near the Phoenician coast, south of Sidon near the Litani watershed.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one mis.
Missed; past tense or past participial form of 'miss'.
Missing; present participial form of 'miss', denoting ongoing action or process.
fine vapor or a thin cloud near the ground.
A plural form referring to more than one mistres.
Misused; past tense or past participial form of 'misus'.
Mite; a very small coin of little value.
A plural form referring to more than one mit.
Mithcah; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
mithcah.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route between Sinai and the Arabah.

definitions: Mithnite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mithn in Scripture.
Mithredath; the name of Persian officials in the records of the return and rebuilding.
Mitre; the official head-dress of the high priest or priestly service.
Mitylene; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mitylene.note: Location: Commonly identified with ancient theater at Mitylene.

definitions: Mixed; past tense or past participial form of 'mix'.
Mixt; old form of mixed, meaning mingled or combined.
things mingled together, or a compound so formed.
Mizar; the 'little hill' named in the lament of Psalm 42 near the Jordan-Hermon region.
mizar.note: Location: In the Jordan-Hermon region north of the Sea of Galilee, near the sources of the Jordan.

definitions: Mizpah; a heap or marked pile of stones mentioned in Scripture.
mizpah.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Mizpeh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
mizpeh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell en Nasbeh.

definitions: Mizraim; a son of Ham and ancestor associated with Egypt and its peoples.
Mizzah; a son of Reuel in the line of Esau.
Mnason; an early disciple from Cyprus with whom Paul lodged on the way to Jerusalem.
Moab; the land and people descended from Lot’s son of that name.
According to context it may refer to the country, the nation, or its inhabitants.
moab.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Kerak.

definitions: Moabite; a member of the people of Moab, east of the Dead Sea.
Moabites; the people descended from Moab, dwelling east of the Dead Sea and often appearing as neighbours, tempters, or enemies of Israel.
A plural form referring to more than one moabites.
Moabitish; belonging to Moab or the Moabites.
Moadiah; a priestly name in the post-exilic lists, related to Maadiah and Maaziah.
Mock; to deride, scorn, or make sport of.
As a noun, an object of scorn.
Mocked; past tense or past participial form of 'mock'.
Mocker; one who mocks, scorns, or derides.
A plural form referring to more than one mocker.
Mockest; older second-person singular form of 'mock', used with 'thou'.
Mocketh; older third-person singular form of 'mock'.
Mocking; present participial form of 'mock', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one mocking.
In a moderate manner or degree.
Gentleness, restraint, and a yielding spirit that avoids excess.
Modest; older second-person singular form of 'mod', used with 'thou'.
slightly wet or damp.
Moistened; past tense or past participial form of 'moisten'.
wetness, dampness, or dew-like fluid.
Moladah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
moladah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Waten.

definitions: Mole; the creature so named, or by some interpretations a burrowing or hidden creature mentioned with idols and uncleanness.
Molech; a false god associated with abominable worship, especially the offering of children in fire.
A plural form referring to more than one mol.
Molid; a Simeonite place-name or line-name occurring in the tribal records; when treated as a person-name it refers to that ancestral association.
Mollified; past tense or past participial form of 'mollifi'.
Moloch; the false god to whom children were offered in abominable rites.
Molten; melted by heat and made to flow.
a brief space of time, or weight and importance.
Money; coin, silver, or property reckoned for payment.
A plural form referring to more than one moneychanger.
A plural form referring to more than one monster.
A stated division of time, commonly reckoned by the moon.
In a month manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one monument.
Moon; the lesser light that rules the night, and by extension the monthly cycle marked by it.
A plural form referring to more than one moon.
Morasthite; an inhabitant of Moresheth.
morasthite.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Goded, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Mordecai; the Jew who reared Esther and was advanced in Persia after the downfall of Haman.
Greater in number, measure, or degree.
Moreh; the oak or terebinth landmark near Shechem, and by extension the hill region associated with that name.
moreh.note: Location: Near Shechem in the central hill country, in the vicinity of modern Tell Balatah.

definitions: Besides; further; in addition to this.
Moresheth-Gath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
moresheth-gath.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Goded.

definitions: Moriah; the mount or region associated with Abraham's offering of Isaac and later with the temple mount.
moriah.note: Location: In Jerusalem at the temple mount, and by earlier association the mountain of Abraham's test.

definitions: Morning; the early part of the day from dawn onward.
The morrow; the next day.
Morsel; a small piece of food or portion.
A plural form referring to more than one morsel.
subject to death; also a human being.
the state of being subject to death.
In a mortal manner or degree.
Mortar; a vessel for pounding, or a cementing mixture for building, depending on context.
Morter; mortar, the binding mixture used in building, and figuratively what joins superficially.
Mortgaged; past tense or past participial form of 'mortgag'.
To put to death; to subdue and crush the power of sinful practices.
Mosera; a wilderness station in Israel's journey, associated in Deuteronomy with the area where Aaron died.
mosera.note: Location: On Israel's wilderness route near Mount Hor and the border of Edom, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Moseroth; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary, related to the route near Mosera.
moseroth.note: Location: On the wilderness route near Mount Hor and the Edomite frontier.

definitions: Moses; the lawgiver and servant of God who led Israel out of Egypt.
Greatest in number, measure, or degree.
Mote; a very small particle, speck, or tiny thing.
Moth; the insect that consumes garments, often used as an image of decay.
Motheaten; consumed or spoiled by moths, and figuratively decaying.
A mother; a female parent, ancestress, or one standing in maternal relation.
A plural form referring to more than one mother.
A plural form referring to more than one motion.
Mouldy; spoiled with mould, stale, or decayed.
To mount; to go up or set upon, and also a mount or hill according to context.
A mountain; a high elevation of land greater than an ordinary hill.
In Scripture mountains often mark strongholds, boundaries, remote places, or sites of notable events.
Mountains; high elevations or ranges of land, often contrasted with valleys and plains.
Mounted; past tense or past participial form of 'mount'.
Mounting; present participial form of 'mount', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one mount.
To grieve, lament, or sorrow deeply.
Mourner; one who grieves, laments, or is engaged in mourning.
A plural form referring to more than one mourner.
Mourns; grieves; laments.
In a mournful manner or degree.
Mouse; a small rodent named among unclean creatures and plague imagery.
The bodily organ of speech and eating.
By extension, speech itself, command, opening, edge, or entrance, according to context.
Capable of being moved; not fixed or immovable.
Was stirred, shifted, affected, or caused to go.
Movedst; an old second-person singular form of mov, used with thou.
Mover; one who moves or causes movement.
Moves; changes place; stirs or influences.
Moving; present participial form of 'mov', denoting ongoing action or process.
Mower; one who cuts grass or grain.
A plural form referring to more than one mowing.
Mown; cut down with a scythe or by mowing.
Past participial form of mow.
Moza; a Benjamite ancestor in the line connected with Saul.
Mozah; a town in the inheritance of Benjamin.
mozah.note: Location: West of Jerusalem in the Benjaminite hill country, often associated with the Qalunya area.

definitions: Much; great in amount, measure, or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one muffler.
Mulberry; the tree so named, associated in Scripture with the balsam or mulberry trees near which David was directed.
Mule; the animal bred from an ass and a horse, used for riding or burden.
A plural form referring to more than one mul.
Multiplied; past tense or past participial form of 'multiply'.
Multipliedst; an old second-person singular form of multipli, used with thou.
Multiplieth; older third-person singular form of 'multiply'.
In a multip manner or degree.
Multiplying; present participial form of 'multiply', denoting ongoing action or process.
A multitude; a great number or crowd of persons or things.
A plural form referring to more than one multitud.
fortification, defense, or supplies for war.
A plural form referring to more than one munition.
Muppim; a Benjamite ancestor whose descendants formed a clan; related forms of the name include Shupham and Shephuphan.
Murder; unlawful killing, blood-guiltiness, or the act of killing with malice.
As a verb, to kill murderously.
Murderer; one guilty of murder, whether by deed or in heart according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one murderer.
A plural form referring to more than one murder.
Murmur; to complain in a low voice, grumble, or whisper.
As a noun, such a complaint or low sound.
Murmured; past tense or past participial form of 'murmur'.
A plural form referring to more than one murmurer.
Murmuring; present participial form of 'murmur', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one murmuring.
Murrain; a deadly plague or disease among cattle.
Muse; to meditate deeply, ponder, or murmur in thought.
As a noun in later use, deep thought or inspiration.
Mused; past tense or past participial form of 'muse'.
Mushi; a son of Merari from whom the Mushites were reckoned.
Mushites; descendants of Mushi reckoned among the Levitical families.
pertaining to music or harmony.
Musicians; those skilled in playing instruments, especially for ordered music and song.
Musick; music, melody, or the ordered use of instruments and song.
Musing; present participial form of 'muse', denoting ongoing action or process.
A verb expressing necessity, duty, or what is bound to occur.
Mustard; the mustard plant or its seed, used in Scripture especially for its very small seed and notable growth.
Mustered; past tense or past participial form of 'muster'.
Mustereth; older third-person singular form of 'musters'.
Mutter; to speak in a low indistinct voice, murmur, or utter secretly.
Muttered; past tense or past participial form of 'mutter'.
reciprocal or shared by each toward the other.
Muzzle; a covering for the mouth or snout of an animal.
As a verb, to bind the mouth or restrain from speaking or feeding.
Belonging to me; the first-person singular possessive form.
Myra; a city of Lycia visited in the course of Paul’s voyage as a prisoner.
Its importance in Acts is chiefly as a port and stage in sea travel.
myra.note: Location: On the southern coast of Asia Minor in Lycia, commonly identified near modern Demre in Turkey.

definitions: Myrrh; an aromatic gum or spice used for perfume, anointing, and burial.
Myrtle; the fragrant evergreen shrub so called, used in prophetic and festive imagery.
The reflexive or emphatic form of I: mine own self.
Mysia; the northwestern district of Asia Minor through which Paul and his company passed.
A truth once hidden, now made known by divine revelation.
Not merely something puzzling, but something disclosed by God.
N; the letter n.
Naam; a son of Caleb in the genealogies of Judah.
Naamah; the name of women in Scripture, including the sister of Tubal-cain and the wife of Solomon from Ammon.
Naaman; especially the Syrian captain healed of leprosy by Elisha, and also the name of other biblical men.
Naamathite; the title of Zophar, Job's friend, marking him as a man of Naamath.
naamathite.note: Location: From a place probably in Arabia or the southeast, though the exact site is unknown.

definitions: Naamites; descendants of Naaman reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Naarah; one of the wives of Ashur in the Judahite genealogies.
Naarai; one of David's mighty men.
Naaran; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
naaran.note: Location: Often identified with Tell el Jisr.

definitions: Naarath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
naarath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ein ed Duyuk.

definitions: Naashon; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Naasson; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Nabal; a man named in Scripture, husband of Abigail.
Naboth; a man named in Scripture.
Nachon; a threshingfloor or place-name associated with the transporting of the ark in David's time.
Nachor; the Greek or alternate form of Nahor, the name of Abraham's father and also of his brother.
Nadab; especially the son of Aaron and also a king of Israel, with other biblical men of the same name.
Nagge; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Nahalal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nahalal.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Nahaliel; an encampment of Israel on the approach to Moab east of the Jordan.
nahaliel.note: Location: In the uplands east of the Dead Sea on Israel's route toward Moab, possibly near the Wadi Zerqa Ma'in system.

definitions: Nahallal; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nahallal.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Nahalol; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nahalol.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Naham; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Nahamani; one of the returned exiles named in Nehemiah.
Naharai; the Beerothite armourbearer of Joab and one of David's mighty men.
Nahash; the name of men in Scripture, especially the Ammonite king in the days of Saul and David.
Nahath; borne by several biblical persons in genealogical and Levitical records.
Nahbi; the son of Vophsi and spy of the tribe of Naphtali.
Nahor; the name of Abraham's grandfather and also of his brother, both appearing in the ancestral line before the patriarchal migration.
Nahshon; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Nahum; the prophet of Elkosh who pronounced judgment against Nineveh.
Nail; the nail of the body or a metal spike driven in fastening.
As a verb, to fasten with nails.
Nailing; present participial form of 'nail', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one nail.
Nain; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Naioth; the prophetic dwellings or settlement near Ramah where Samuel stayed with the company of prophets.
naioth.note: Location: Near Ramah in Benjamin, north of Jerusalem.

definitions: Naked; past tense or past participial form of 'nak'.
The state of being uncovered or unclothed.
Often also shameful exposure, especially where modesty, kinship, or ceremonial defilement is involved.
A name; also reputation, authority, character, or that by which a person is declared or known.
Named; past tense or past participial form of 'name'.
In a name manner or degree.
Names; designations, reputations, or persons so named according to context.
Nameth; older third-person singular form of 'name'.
Naomi; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Elimelech, mother of Chilion.
Naphish; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a line bearing his name.
Naphtali; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Guni.
Naphtuhim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Napkin; a cloth for wiping, wrapping, or covering.
Narcissus; the man whose household is mentioned in Paul's greetings to the Romans.
Narrow; strait, confined, limited, or close.
As a verb, to make narrow or straitened.
Narrowed; past tense or past participial form of 'narrow'.
Narrower; more narrow, more confined, or more limited.
In a narrow manner or degree.
Nathan; especially the prophet who rebuked David and spoke the promise concerning his house, and also the name of other biblical men.
Nathan-Melech; an officer of the king mentioned in the reforms of Josiah.
Nathanael; the Israelite in whom was no guile, commonly identified with Bartholomew.
A nation; a people joined by common descent, land, or civil order.
It is often used of the Gentile peoples as distinguished from Israel, though not always.
Belonging by birth to a place or people; home-born.
A person born in a place; one of the original inhabitants.
birth, or the circumstances and place of birth.
according to nature or ordinary birth; also one native to a place.
In a natural manner or degree.
inherent character, native constitution, or the created order.

Nothing, or worthless and of no account.
The state, quality, or condition of being naughtines.
Naughty; wicked, worthless, or morally bad.
Naum; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Navel; the mark at the centre of the belly where the body was joined to the womb.

Navy; a fleet of ships, especially for commerce or war.
No; and more than that; a strong denial or correction.
Nazarene; one from Nazareth, and especially the identifying title applied to Jesus as Jesus of Nazareth.
In some contexts, the name is also used for his followers or for the sect identified with him.
Nazarenes; those identified with Jesus of Nazareth, or in some contexts people of Nazareth.
Nazareth; the town of Galilee where Jesus was brought up.
The name often identifies Him as Jesus of Nazareth.
nazareth.note: Location: In lower Galilee, commonly identified with modern Nazareth in northern Israel.

definitions: Nazarite; one separated by a special vow unto God, marked by abstinence and consecration.
Nazarites; persons specially separated unto God by a vow of consecration.
Neah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
A plural form referring to more than one neapoli.
Near; close to in place, time, or relation.
Nearer; more near in place, degree, or relation.
Neariah; a descendant of David in the royal genealogy.
Nebai; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Nebaioth; the form of the name Nebajoth used for the firstborn son of Ishmael and ancestor of an Arabian people.
Nebajoth; the firstborn son of Ishmael, whose name also stands for the people descending from him.
Neballat; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
neballat.note: Location: Commonly identified with Beit Nabala.

definitions: Nebat; a man named in Scripture, husband of Zeruah, father of Jeroboam.
Nebo; the name of a Babylonian deity and also of persons or places formed from that name.
Nebuchadnezzar; king of Babylon who took Jerusalem and figures prominently in Daniel.
Nebuchadrezzar; a form of the name Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon.
Nebushasban; the Rab-saris or Babylonian officer present at Jerusalem's fall.
Nebuzar-Adan; the captain of the guard of Babylon who oversaw the fall of Jerusalem.
Needful, required, or unavoidable.
A plural form referring to more than one necessity.
need, compulsion, or what cannot be avoided.
Necho; a man named in Scripture.
Neck; the part joining the head to the body, and by extension strength, stubbornness, or subjection according to context.

Necromancer; one who seeks knowledge by forbidden dealings with the dead or occult spirits.
Nedabiah; a son in the royal line of Jeconiah.
Needed; past tense or past participial form of 'need'.
Needest; older second-person singular form of 'need', used with 'thou'.
Needs; requires; stands in need of.
Needed, necessary, or useful for the occasion.
