Needlework; embroidered or sewn work done with the needle.
A plural form referring to more than one need.
Needy; poor, lacking necessary support, or in pressing want.
Sneezings.
Neglect; to disregard, fail to care for, or leave undone.
As a noun, careless omission or disregard.
Neglected; past tense or past participial form of 'neglect'.
Neglecting; present participial form of 'neglect', denoting ongoing action or process.
Careless, slack, or neglectful in duty.
Nehelamite; the title of Shemaiah the false prophet in Jeremiah, marking him as a man of Nehelam.
nehelamite.note: Location: From an otherwise uncertain place-name, sometimes associated with the region north of Jerusalem.

definitions: Nehemiah; the cupbearer to Artaxerxes who rebuilt Jerusalem's wall and governed Judah.
Nehum; a returning leader named in the exile lists, commonly linked with the related forms Rehum or Harim.
Nehushta; the mother of Jehoiachin king of Judah.
Nehushtan; the name given to the brazen serpent when it became an object of idolatrous burning of incense.
Neiel; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
neiel.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Yanin.

definitions: A neighbour; one dwelling near, or any other person regarded in relation and duty.
Neighbours; those dwelling near, or other persons regarded in relation and duty.
Neighed; past tense or past participial form of 'neigh'.
Neighing; present participial form of 'neigh', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one neighing.
Not either; not the one nor the other; and not.
Nekeb; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nekeb.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Adami.

definitions: Nekoda; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile, some with uncertain genealogy.
Nemuel; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Simeon and a Reubenite line also called Jemuel.
Nemuelites; descendants of Nemuel reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Nepheg; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of David and a descendant in Moses' line.
Nephew; in older usage a grandson, descendant, or other near kinsman; not always limited to the modern sense.
A plural form referring to more than one nephew.
Nephish; a son of Ishmael whose name also marks his descendants.
Nephishesim; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Nephthalim; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Guni.
Nephtoah; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
Nephusim; the name of a family of Nethinims or temple servants; it also appears in the form Nephishesim.
Nepthalim; an alternate form of Naphtali and the people descended from him.
Ner; a Benjamite ancestor of Saul's house, known especially as the father of Abner.
Nereus; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
a Babylonian deity worshiped by the people of Cuth.
Nergal-Sharezer; a Babylonian officer or ruler named in connection with the capture of Jerusalem.
Neri; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Neriah; the father of Baruch and Seraiah in the days of Jeremiah.
A bird's dwelling or resting place.
By figure, a settled and often lofty place of security.
A net is a mesh used to catch fish, birds, or prey.
By figure it may signify entanglement, snare, or hidden danger.
Nethaneel; the name of many men in Scripture, especially princes, Levites, and temple officials.
Nethaniah; borne by several biblical men, especially fathers or family heads named in royal, priestly, and post-exilic records.
Nether; lower, beneath, or belonging to the lower part.
Nethermost; an old second-person singular form of nethermo, used with thou.
Nethinims; temple servants given to assist the Levites in sanctuary duties.
Netophah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
netophah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh.

definitions: Netophathi; a person or group belonging to Netophah.
Netophathite; an inhabitant of Netophah.
netophathite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one netophathit.
netophathites.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one net.
A plural form referring to more than one nettl.
Network; a net-like work or pattern of interwoven cords or metal.
A plural form referring to more than one network.
At no time; not ever.
Notwithstanding that; for all that; yet despite what has just been said.
New; fresh, recent, or not formerly existing in that condition.
Newborn; newly born.
As a noun, an infant newly born.
In a new manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being newnes.
A plural form referring to more than one new.
Next; nearest in place, time, order, or relation.
Neziah; the head of a family whose genealogy was uncertain after the exile.
Nezib; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nezib.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Beit Nesib esh Sharqiyyeh.

definitions: Nibhaz; a god of the Avites, named among the foreign deities set up in Samaria.
Nibshan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nibshan.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Maqari.

definitions: Nicanor; one of the seven chosen in Acts to serve the church at Jerusalem.
Nicodemus; the Pharisee and ruler of the Jews who came to Jesus by night and later aided in his burial.
A plural form referring to more than one nicolaitan.
Nicolas; the proselyte of Antioch chosen among the seven in Acts.
A plural form referring to more than one nicopoli.
Niger; the surname of Simeon among the prophets and teachers at Antioch.
Near; close at hand; drawing near.
Night; the time of darkness between evening and morning.
Night; the time of darkness.
A plural form referring to more than one night.
Nimrah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
nimrah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tall Bleibel.

definitions: Nimrim; a wadi or seasonal watercourse mentioned in Scripture.
nimrim.note: Location: In Moab east of the Dead Sea, often linked with Wadi en-Numeirah.

definitions: Nimrod; the mighty hunter before the LORD and founder of an early kingdom after the Flood.
Nimshi; the ancestor of Jehu in the northern royal line.
The number nine.
The number nineteen.
Next after the eighteenth.
The number ninety.
Nineve; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Nineveh; the great city of Assyria, especially known from the preaching of Jonah.
It also appears in prophecy as a symbol of imperial pride brought under judgment.
nineveh.note: Location: On the Tigris in ancient Assyria, commonly linked with the area opposite modern Mosul in Iraq.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one ninevit.
The one next after the eighth; occupying the ninth place.
Nisan; the first month of the sacred Hebrew year after the exile, corresponding to Abib.
Nisroch; the Assyrian deity in whose temple Sennacherib was slain.
Nitre; a mineral substance used for cleansing or effervescing action, not exactly the same as modern nitre in every context.
Not any; none.
Noadiah; the name of a prophetess opposed to Nehemiah and also of a Levite in post-exilic records.
Noah; the righteous man preserved with his family through the flood in the ark.
Nob; the priestly city where David received the shewbread and where Saul later slaughtered the priests.
nob.note: Location: Just north of Jerusalem on the Benjamin ridge, in view of the city.

definitions: Nobah; the man who captured Kenath and called it after his own name.
Noble; of high rank, generous spirit, or honorable quality.
As a noun, one of noble station.
Nobleman; a man of rank or station, one of noble standing.
A plural form referring to more than one nobl.
Nod; the land east of Eden to which Cain went after his judgment.
Nodab; an Arabian or tribal name associated with the descendants of Ishmael.
Noe; the Greek form of Noah.
Nogah; one of the sons of David born in Jerusalem.
Nohah; a Benjamite descendant named in the tribal genealogies.
Noise; sound, cry, tumult, or report.
Noised; past tense or past participial form of 'noise'.
Noisome; hurtful, offensive, or unwholesome.
Non; a man named in Scripture, son of Elishama, father of Joshua.
Not one; no person or thing.
Noon; the middle of the day.
Noonday; the middle or brightest part of the day.
Noontide; noon or midday.
Noph; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Nophah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Nor; and not; neither.
The northern quarter or region.
Northern; belonging to the north or situated toward the north.
Northward; toward the north, or situated on the north side.
Nose; the part of the face used for smelling and breathing, and in some passages the nostril or snout.
A plural form referring to more than one nos.
A plural form referring to more than one nostril.
A word of denial or negation.
Marked, distinguished, or worthy of notice.
Note; a mark, observation, sign, or matter to be remembered.
As a verb, to mark, observe, or take notice.
Noted; past tense or past participial form of 'not'.
Not anything; no thing at all.
Notice; observation, attention, or knowledge taken of a thing.
As a verb, to observe or take note.
Notwithstanding; present participial form of 'notwithstand', denoting ongoing action or process.
Nought; nothing, no value, or what comes to nothing.
Nourish; to feed, sustain, cherish, or cause to grow.
Nourished; past tense or past participial form of 'nourish'.
Nourisher; one who nourishes, sustains, or feeds.
Nourisheth; older third-person singular form of 'nourish'.
Nourishing; present participial form of 'nourish', denoting ongoing action or process.
food, sustenance, or that which supports life.
Novice; one newly entered, inexperienced, or recent in the faith or office.
At this present time; as matters stand.
Number; a count, sum, or company counted.
Counted or reckoned by number.
Numberest; older second-person singular form of 'number', used with 'thou'.
Numbering; present participial form of 'number', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one number.
Nun; a man named in Scripture, son of Elishama, father of Joshua.
Nurse; one who suckles, tends, or brings up a child.
As a verb, to nourish or tend.
Nursed; past tense or past participial form of 'nurse'.
Nursing; present participial form of 'nurse', denoting ongoing action or process.
A foster father; one who nourishes, raises, or protects with paternal care.
upbringing, training, or nourishing care.
A plural form referring to more than one nut.
A plural form referring to more than one nympha.
An exclamation or vocative particle used in address or strong feeling.
Oak; the strong tree so called, often used as a landmark or sacred site marker.
A plural form referring to more than one oak.
Oar; a pole or blade used to row a boat.
A plural form referring to more than one oar.
A solemn appeal to God in confirmation of a word, promise, or testimony.
Obadiah; the name of many men in Scripture, and especially the prophet whose book bears his name.
Obal; a Joktanite descendant in the table of nations; the name also appears in the form Ebal.
Obed; past tense or past participial form of 'ob'.
a Gittite associated with the keeping of the ark, and also the name of Levites in later lists.
Submission to rightful authority; heedful compliance with command.
Submissive to command; willing to obey.
a bow or act of reverence and submission.
To obey; to hear with submission and do what is commanded.
Obeyed; past tense or past participial form of 'obey'.
Obeyedst; an old second-person singular form of obey, used with thou.
Obeyeth; older third-person singular form of 'obey'.
Obeying; present participial form of 'obey', denoting ongoing action or process.
Obil; the Ishmaelite set over David's camels.
Object; a thing set before the mind or eyes, or something aimed at.
As a verb, to oppose or raise an objection.
An oblation; an offering solemnly presented unto God.
The word especially suits what is brought before Him in worship as a dedicated gift.
Oboth; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
oboth.note: Location: Often identified with Ein Weibeh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: not clear or little known; also to darken or make unclear.
darkness, uncertainty, or a low and hidden condition.
careful watching, notice, or observance.
Observe; to watch, heed, keep, or comply with according to context.
Observed; past tense or past participial form of 'observe'.
Observer; one who watches, notices, or in some contexts practices superstitious observing of times.
A plural form referring to more than one observer.
Observest; older second-person singular form of 'observe', used with 'thou'.
Observeth; older third-person singular form of 'observe'.
Obstinate; stubborn, fixed in resistance, or hard to turn.
Obtain; to get, receive, gain, or secure.
Obtained; past tense or past participial form of 'obtain'.
Obtaineth; older third-person singular form of 'obtain'.
Obtaining; present participial form of 'obtain', denoting ongoing action or process.
an opportunity, cause, or event.
Occasioned; past tense or past participial form of 'occasion'.
A plural form referring to more than one occasion.
work, business, or employment.
Occupied; past tense or past participial form of 'occupy'.
A plural form referring to more than one occupier.
Occupieth; older third-person singular form of 'occupy'.
Occupy; to use, possess, engage in business, or keep busy.
Occurring or happening; incidental.
Ocran; the father of Pagiel, prince of Asher.
Odd; not paired, not even in number, or unusual.
Oded; past tense or past participial form of 'od'.
Hateful, offensive, or deserving aversion.
Odour; smell, fragrance, or scent, whether pleasant or otherwise by context.

Of; from, belonging to, concerning, or proceeding out of.
Away; from the place or thing in question.
A cause of stumbling, falling, or displeasure.

To cause to stumble, sin, or take hurt.
Also to commit an offense against another.
Offender; one who commits an offence or causes offence.

Presented, especially as a sacrifice or gift; brought near for acceptance.
Offers; presents; brings forward.

definitions: Ramah; the name of several towns in Scripture, especially the place associated with Samuel and with Rachel's lament by figure.

definitions: Sap; the vital juice of plants and trees.
Saph; a Philistine giant slain by Sibbecai in David's wars; the same giant is given under the related form Sippai.
Saphir; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
saphir.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tell as Sawafir.

definitions: Sapphira; the wife of Ananias who lied to the Holy Ghost and fell dead in Acts.
Sapphire; the precious stone so called, used in descriptions of beauty and glory.
A plural form referring to more than one sapphir.
Sara; the Greek form of Sarah.
Sarah; the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac, first called Sarai.
Sarai; the earlier name of Sarah, wife of Abram before the covenant name-change.
Saraph; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Sardine; a precious stone, commonly linked with sardius or similar red gem terminology in older usage.
Sardis; the city addressed among the seven churches in Revelation.
It is remembered there for having a name to live while needing spiritual awakening.
sardis.note: Location: In western Asia Minor, commonly identified with Sardis near modern Sart in Turkey.

definitions: Sardites; descendants of Sered reckoned among the families of Zebulun.
A plural form referring to more than one sardiu.
Sardonyx; a precious stone of layered reddish and whitish appearance.
Sarepta; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Sargon; a man named in Scripture, father of Adrammelech.
Sarid; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
sarid.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Shadud.

definitions: Saron; the fertile coastal plain better known under the form Sharon.
saron.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Sharon Plain.

definitions: Sarsechim; a Babylonian officer present at the fall of Jerusalem.
Saruch; the Greek form of Serug, an ancestor in the line from Shem to Abraham.
The past tense of sit; was seated, remained, or dwelt in a place.
Satan; the adversary, the chief evil spirit who opposes God and tempts or accuses men.

Satisfieth; older third-person singular form of 'satisfy'.

Delivered, preserved, or rescued from danger, judgment, or ruin.

Scaffold; a raised platform or staging.

Scall; a scurfy or scabby eruption named in the law of leprosy.

Scapegoat; the goat sent away on the Day of Atonement bearing away the confessed sins of the people, and figuratively one made to bear blame for others.

Scarest; older second-person singular form of 'scar', used with 'thou'.

Scattering; present participial form of 'scatter', denoting ongoing action or process.

Scholar; a learner, student, or one instructed by a teacher.
School; a company or setting of instruction and learning.

knowledge or disciplined understanding.
Scoff; to mock with contempt.

A plural form referring to more than one scoffer.

Scornest; older second-person singular form of 'scorn', used with 'thou'.
Scorneth; older third-person singular form of 'scorn'.
Full of scorn; contemptuous.
Scorpion; a venomous creature with a stinging tail, also used figuratively for cruel scourges or torment.
A plural form referring to more than one scorpion.
Scoured; past tense or past participial form of 'scour'.
Scourge; a whip or instrument of punishment, and figuratively a severe judgment or affliction.
As a verb, to whip or chastise.
Scourged; past tense or past participial form of 'scourge'.
A plural form referring to more than one scourg.
Scourgeth; older third-person singular form of 'scourge'.
Scourging; present participial form of 'scourge', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one scourging.
Scrabbled; past tense or past participial form of 'scrabbl'.
Scrape; to rub or draw over a surface so as to remove or gather something.
As a noun, a scratch or awkward difficulty.
Scraped; past tense or past participial form of 'scrape'.

As a noun, such a cry.
A scribe; a writer, copyist, or learned expounder of the law.
In the New Testament the term often refers to one skilled in the Scriptures and associated with teaching or legal interpretation.
Scribes; learned writers, copyists, or teachers of the law.
A small bag, wallet, or satchel for carrying provisions.

A plural form referring to more than one scriptur.
Scroll; a rolled writing or book-form document.
Scum; foul froth, refuse, or impurity gathered on the surface.
Scurvy; vile, contemptible, or base.
Scythian; a member of the Scythian people, used by Paul as an example of a foreign people regarded by the world as especially barbarous.

According to context it may mean the open sea, a great inland water, or a named body of water.
Seafaring; present participial form of 'seafar', denoting ongoing action or process.
a stamp or mark used to secure or authenticate; also to fasten or confirm with such a seal.
Sealed; past tense or past participial form of 'seal'.
Sealest; older second-person singular form of 'seal', used with 'thou'.
Sealeth; older third-person singular form of 'seals'.
Sealing; present participial form of 'seal', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one seal.
Seam; the line where things are joined by sewing or fitting together.
To examine carefully, seek out, or inquire diligently.
Searchest; older second-person singular form of 'search', used with 'thou'.
Searching; present participial form of 'search', denoting ongoing action or process.

Seared; past tense or past participial form of 'sear'.
Seas; large bodies of water, whether open seas or great inland waters according to context.

definitions: Sechu; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.

definitions: Next after the first.

Secret; a hidden matter, and also hidden or private according to context.

safe or free from danger or care; also to make safe or obtain firmly.

safety, assurance, or something pledged as guarantee.
rebellion or incitement against lawful authority.

A plural form referring to more than one seducer.

Seekest; thou seekest; you seek, look for, or pursue after.

Seemed; past tense or past participial form of 'seem'.

definitions: Seirath; the wooded or hilly district to which Ehud escaped after slaying Eglon.
seirath.note: Location: Often located in or identified with hills of Ephraim, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Seize; to take hold of suddenly, lay hold on by force, or grasp.

definitions: Selah; a musical or liturgical term in the Psalms, likely marking a pause or lifting up.

Seleucia; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
seleucia.note: Location: Commonly identified with Seleucia Pieria.

definitions: Self; one's own person, nature, or being.

Sellest; older second-person singular form of 'sell', used with 'thou'.
Selleth; older third-person singular form of 'sell'.

Semachiah; one of the sons of Shemaiah among the gatekeepers.

definitions: Senir; a name used for Hermon by other peoples.

definitions: Sennacherib; a man named in Scripture, father of Adrammelech.

a judgment, decree, or complete saying.

definitions: Sepharad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.

A plural form referring to more than one sepulchr.
Serah; the daughter of Asher, notably preserved in the tribal genealogies.
Seraiah; especially the high priest slain at Riblah, and also the name of various priests or officials in later records.

A plural form referring to more than one sergiu.

slavery, bondage, or a state of service.

Shaharaim; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shahazimah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.

definitions: Shaked; past tense or past participial form of 'shak'.

Not fear of faces, but modest self-restraint.

Shamgar; the judge of Israel who slew six hundred Philistines with an ox goad.
Shamhuth; one of David's captains over the monthly courses.
Shamir; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name and the stone.
Shamma; a name in the genealogies and mighty-men lists, related to the better-known Shammah forms.
Shammah; especially one of David's mighty men, and also the name of a son of Jesse and other biblical men.
Shammai; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Judah.

Shammua; borne by several biblical men, including one of David's sons and one of the spies sent from Israel.
Shamsherai; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.

Shapham; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.

definitions: Sharai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.

definitions: Sharar; the father of Ahiam, one of David's mighty men; the name appears under the form Sacar.

Sharon; the broad fertile plain along the Mediterranean coast, used in Scripture as an image of richness and beauty.

definitions: Sharonite; one belonging to Sharon, the coastal plain so called.
Sharp; keen, cutting, severe, piercing, or intense according to context.

definitions: Shashai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.

A sheaf is a bundle of stalks or grain bound together after reaping.
Sheal; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Shealtiel; the father of Zerubbabel in the royal line after the captivity.
Shear; to cut off with shears, especially wool or hair.

Shear-Jashub; the son of Isaiah whose symbolic name means a remnant shall return.

Sheariah; a Benjamite descendant in the line of Saul.

Sheath; a case or covering for a sword or other blade.

Sheba; the name of men in Scripture, especially the Benjamite rebel who lifted up his hand against David.

definitions: Shebam; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.

Shelah; the name of men in Scripture, especially the son of Judah by Tamar and also an ancestor in the postdiluvian line.
Shelanites; descendants of Shelah reckoned among the families of Judah.
Shelemiah; borne by several biblical men, especially priests, gatekeepers, and officials in Jeremiah's day.
Sheleph; a son of Joktan in the early genealogies.
Shelesh; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Shelomi; the father of Ahihud, prince of the tribe of Asher.
Shelomith; borne by several biblical persons, including a daughter of Dibri and Levites in temple service.
Shelomoth; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and family heads in the genealogies.
Shelter; cover, protection, or refuge.
As a verb, to cover or protect.
Shelumiel; the son of Zurishaddai and prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Shem; a man named in Scripture, son of Noah, father of Asshur.
Shema; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Benjamin and Judah.
Shemaah; the father of a gatekeeper line in the chronicler's records.
Shemaiah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially the prophet who warned Rehoboam and numerous Levites or post-exilic figures.
Shemariah; borne by several biblical men, including one of David's mighty men and post-exilic figures.
Shemeber; king of Zeboiim in the days of Abram.
Shemer; the owner of the hill on which Omri built Samaria.
Shemida; a son of Gilead in the tribe of Manasseh, ancestor of a clan.
Shemidaites; descendants of Shemida reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Sheminith; a musical direction in the psalm titles, probably referring to the eighth or to a lower register.
Shemiramoth; the name of Levites associated with music and the bearing of the ark.
Shemuel; a Hebrew form of Samuel, used of Samuel the prophet and of others bearing the same name.
Shen; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shen.note: Location: Often identified with Burj el Isaneh.

definitions: Shenazar; a son in the royal line descended from Jeconiah.
Shenir; another name for Hermon used by neighboring peoples.
shenir.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Shepham; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shepham.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Hermel.

definitions: Shephathiah; borne by several biblical men, including a son of David and post-exilic leaders.
Shephatiah; a name borne by men in royal and post-exilic records; it corresponds to the form Shephathiah.
A shepherd; one who keeps, feeds, and guides sheep.
The word is also used figuratively of rulers, teachers, and supremely of the Lord as shepherd of His people.
Shepherds; those who keep, feed, and guide sheep.
The word may also be used figuratively of rulers or spiritual guides according to context.
Shephi; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Shepho.
Shepho; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Shephi.
Shephuphan; a Benjamite ancestor whose name appears in related forms such as Muppim and Shupham.
Sherah; a daughter in the line of Ephraim, remembered for building Beth-horon and Uzzen-sherah.
Sherd; a broken fragment of pottery or earthenware.
A plural form referring to more than one sherd.
Sherebiah; a Levite of understanding who served with Ezra and Nehemiah.
Sheresh; a woman of Manasseh named in the genealogies.
Sherezer; the name of men in Scripture, including one sent to inquire at the temple and a son of Sennacherib.
A plural form referring to more than one sheriff.
Sheshach; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Sheshai; one of the sons of Anak at Hebron.
Sheshan; a Judahite ancestor whose line continued through his daughter and the Egyptian Jarha.
Sheshbazzar; a prince of Judah associated with the first return from Babylon and the temple vessels.
Sheth; older third-person singular form of 'she'.
Shethar; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
Shethar-Boznai; a Persian official who wrote concerning the rebuilding of the temple.
Sheva; the scribe in David's court, also called Seraiah or Shavsha in parallel records.
To show; to make plain or visible.
Shewedst; an old second-person singular form of shew, used with thou.
Shewest; thou shewest; you show, make known, or set before the sight.
Shows; makes known; sets before the sight or understanding.
An older spelling of 'showeth.'
Shewing; present participial form of 'shew', denoting ongoing action or process.
Shibboleth; older third-person singular form of 'shibbols'.
Shibmah; a town east of the Jordan known for its vineyards and associated with Reuben and Moab.
shibmah.note: Location: In the tableland east of the Dead Sea near Heshbon.

definitions: Shicron; a border place of Judah in the Shephelah toward Ekron.
shicron.note: Location: In the western border region of Judah toward Philistia and Ekron, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: A shield is a defensive weapon held to ward off blows or missiles.
In Scripture it is also a common figure for protection and defense from God.
A plural form referring to more than one shield.
Shigionoth; a musical or poetic term in Habakkuk, likely denoting an impassioned or irregular style of song.
Shihor; a river name often used of the Nile or its eastern branch.
shihor.note: Location: Usually identified with Nile River.

definitions: Shihor-Libnath; the stream or border district marking part of Asher's southern boundary.
shihor-libnath.note: Location: Usually placed near the mouth of the Kishon River on the Carmel coast, though the exact identification is uncertain.

definitions: Shilhi; the father of Jehoshabeath, wife of Jehoiada the priest.
Shilhim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shilhim.note: Location: Often identified with Tall al Ajjul.

definitions: Shillem; a son of Naphtali whose descendants formed a clan in Israel.
Shillemites; descendants of Shillem reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Shiloah; the gently flowing waters of Jerusalem, especially the channel or pool later called Siloam.
shiloah.note: Location: In Jerusalem on the southeastern side of the city, connected with the Gihon spring and the Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Shiloh; the place where the tabernacle stood in the days before the monarchy, later a warning example in the prophets.
shiloh.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Seilun.

definitions: Shiloni; an ancestral name associated with the Shilonite line in Judah.
Shilonite; an inhabitant of Shiloh, or one belonging to that place.
shilonite.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Seilun.

definitions: Shilonites; people of Shiloh, and in genealogical contexts a Judahite family associated with that name.
Shilshah; one of the sons of Zelophehad in a later genealogical form, or a family name in Judah.
Shimea; especially a brother of David, and also the name of descendants in Judah and Benjamin and other biblical men.
Shimeah; a variant name borne by several men in Scripture, especially in the family of Jesse and David.
Shimeam; a Levite family name in the genealogical records, associated with the line of Gershom.
Shimeath; the Ammonitess mother of one of Joash's assassins.
Shimeathites; a scribal or clan group named among the families of Judah.
Shimei; the name of many men in Scripture, especially the Benjamite who cursed David as he fled from Absalom.
Shimeon; a form of Simeon occurring in genealogical and New Testament records.
Shimhi; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shimi; a genealogical form related to the better-known name Shimei.
Shimites; descendants of Shimi reckoned among the Levitical families.
Shimma; a name in Davidic and Judahite family lines, represented in related forms such as Shammah and Shimeah.
Shimon; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shimrath; a Moabite woman, mother of one of Joash's assassins.
Shimri; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites and tribal figures.
Shimrith; the Moabitess mother of Jozabad, one of the servants who conspired against king Joash.
Shimron; the name of a son of Issachar and also of a Canaanite royal city in Joshua's conquests.
Shimron-Meron; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shimron-meron.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Shimron.

definitions: Shimronites; descendants of Shimron reckoned among the families of Issachar.
Shimshai; the Persian scribe who wrote against Jerusalem in the days of Artaxerxes.
Shinab; king of Admah in the days of Abram.
Shinar; the plain or land associated with Babel and the early Babylonian realm.
shinar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Babylon.

definitions: Shined; past tense or past participial form of 'shin'.
Shines; gives light; appears bright or glorious.
Shining; present participial form of 'shin', denoting ongoing action or process.
Shion; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
A ship; a vessel used for travel upon water.
Shiphi; a Levite name in the temple genealogies.
Shiphmite; the title of Zabdi, who was set over David's vineyards, marking him as a man of Shiphm.
shiphmite.note: Location: From an uncertain place-name, perhaps in the northern hill country or Lebanon-facing region.

definitions: Shiphrah; one of the Hebrew midwives who feared God in Egypt.
Shiphtan; the father of Kemuel, prince of Ephraim.
Shipmaster; the master, captain, or chief officer of a ship.
Shipmen; sailors or men belonging to ships.
Shipping; present participial form of 'ship', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one ship.
Shipwreck; the wreck or loss of a ship.
As a verb, to suffer shipwreck or wreck a ship.
Shisha; the royal scribe or secretary in the early monarchy, represented in related forms such as Shavsha and Sheva.
Shishak; the king of Egypt who came up against Jerusalem in the days of Rehoboam.
Shitrai; the Sharonite set over David's herds in Sharon.
Shittah; the acacia tree or its wood, used especially in tabernacle materials.
Shittim; acacia wood or the acacia trees so called, used especially in tabernacle construction.
A plural form referring to more than one shiver.
Shiza; the father of Adina the Reubenite.
Shoa; a people named by Ezekiel among the proud eastern powers associated with Babylon.
Shobab; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of David and one in the line of Caleb.
Shobach; commander of the host of Hadarezer, defeated by David; the name also appears in the form Shophach.
Shobai; the head of a family of porters among the returned exiles.
Shobal; the name of men in Scripture, including a Horite chief and a Judahite ancestor linked with Kirjath-jearim.
Shobek; the ruler of half part of Keilah who repaired the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Shobi; the son of Nahash of Rabbah who helped sustain David in his distress.
Shocho; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shocho.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shochoh; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shochoh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shock; a heap or gathering of sheaves, especially in harvest imagery.
A plural form referring to more than one shock.
Shoco; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shoco.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shod; furnished with shoes, sandals, or iron.
Past participial form of shoe.
Shoe; footwear for the foot, and in some passages a symbol in legal or covenant acts.
Shoelatchet; the thong or fastening of a sandal or shoe.
A plural form referring to more than one sho.
Shoham; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shomer; father of one of the servants who conspired against king Joash, elsewhere connected with the form Shimrith.
Shone; the past tense of shine.
Shook; the past tense of shake.
Shoot; to cast forth, send forth, spring up, or discharge a weapon.
As a noun, a sprout or branch.
A plural form referring to more than one shooter.
Shooteth; older third-person singular form of 'shoot'.
Shooting; present participial form of 'shoot', denoting ongoing action or process.
Shophach; the Syrian commander defeated by David, whose name also appears under the form Shobach.
Shophan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
shophan.note: Location: Often identified with Rujm Ataruz.

definitions: Shore; the edge or bank of the sea, river, or other water.
Shorn; cut off, especially of hair or wool.
Past participial form of shear.
Short; not long, insufficient in length or measure, or abruptly according to context.
Shortened; past tense or past participial form of 'shorten'.
Shorter; more short in length, measure, or duration.
Soon; in a little time; quickly.
Shot; a discharge or cast from a weapon, and also past tense of shoot in some uses.
A verb of duty, fitness, or expected result.
The shoulder is the upper part of the side where the arm joins the body.
It may also signify bearing, carrying, or a place of strength and responsibility.
A plural form referring to more than one shoulderpiec.
A plural form referring to more than one shoulder.
Shouldest; thou shouldest; you ought to, would be expected to, or would in fitting course.
Shout; to cry aloud, raise a war-cry or acclamation, or the cry itself.
Shouted; past tense or past participial form of 'shout'.
Shouteth; older third-person singular form of 'shout'.
Shouting; present participial form of 'shout', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one shouting.
Shovel; an implement for taking up ashes, earth, or other loose matter.
A plural form referring to more than one shovel.
Shower; a fall of rain, or a plentiful descent of something.
As a verb, to pour down or bestow abundantly.
A plural form referring to more than one shower.
Shows; makes known; displays; causes to be seen.
Shrank; past tense of shrink.
Shred; past tense or past participial form of 'shr'.
A plural form referring to more than one shrin.
Shroud; a covering cloth, especially for burial.
As a verb, to cover or wrap over.
A plural form referring to more than one shrub.
Shua; the name of persons in Scripture, including a Canaanite connected with Judah's marriage and a son of Abraham by Keturah in related form.
Shuah; the name of persons in Genesis and Chronicles, including the father of one of Job's friends by related form.
Shual; an Asherite descendant named in the tribal genealogies.
Shubael; the descendant of Moses placed over the treasures, whose name also appears under the form Shebuel.
Shuham; the form of the name Hushim used for the son of Dan whose descendants formed a clan.
Shuhamites; descendants of Shuham reckoned among the families of Dan.
Shuhite; one belonging to Shuah or the people descended from that line.
Shulamite; the woman addressed in the Song of Solomon, probably linked with Shulem or Shunem in the poetic setting of the book.
Shumathites; a family or clan named among the Judahite houses.
Shun; to avoid, keep away from, or turn aside from.
Shunammite; a woman of Shunem, especially the notable woman in the history of Elisha and also Abishag in another context.
Shunem; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Shuni; a son of Gad from whom the Shunites were reckoned.
Shunites; descendants of Shuni reckoned among the families of Gad.
Shunned; past tense or past participial form of 'shun'.
Shupham; a Benjamite ancestor whose name appears in related forms such as Muppim and Shephuphan.
Shuphamites; descendants of Shupham reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Shuppim; a Benjamite ancestor or clan name in the genealogies.
Shur; the wilderness region on the northeastern border of Egypt through which Israel passed.
shur.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Ar Ruwaysat, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Shushan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
To withhold pity and compassion.
Shuthalhites; descendants of Shuthelah reckoned among the families of Ephraim.
Shuthelah; a son of Ephraim from whom the Shuthelahites were reckoned.
Shuts; closes; keeps from entrance or exit.
Shutting; present participial form of 'shutt', denoting ongoing action or process.
Shuttle; the instrument used in weaving to carry the thread through the warp.
Sia; the name attached to a family of temple servants in the return lists.
Siaha; the name of a family of temple servants; it also appears in the form Sia.
Sibbecai; one of David's mighty men, the Hushathite who slew a Philistine giant.
Sibbechai; one of David's mighty men, whose name also appears under the form Sibbecai.
Sibboleth; older third-person singular form of 'sibbols'.
Sibmah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
sibmah.note: Location: Often identified with Qarn al Qubish.

definitions: Sibraim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
sibraim.note: Location: Commonly identified with between Damascus and Hamath.

definitions: Sichem; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
sichem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Balatah.

definitions: Sick; diseased, weak, or unwell.
A sickle is the curved cutting instrument used in reaping.
In a sick manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being sicknes.
A plural form referring to more than one sickness.
Siddim; the valley associated with the cities later overwhelmed near the Salt Sea.
siddim.note: Location: In the Salt Sea basin, traditionally placed at the southern end of the Dead Sea.

definitions: A side; one part, flank, or aspect of a thing.
A plural form referring to more than one sid.
Sidon; the ancient Phoenician city on the coast, sometimes standing for its people or territory.
The name may also appear in the form Zidon.
sidon.note: Location: On the Phoenician coast, commonly identified with modern Sidon in Lebanon.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one sidonian.
sidonians.note: Location: Usually identified with Sandikli archaeological site in Sidon.

definitions: Siege; the surrounding and pressing of a city in war.
Sieve; a vessel with a perforated bottom used for sifting.
Sift; to separate by passing through a sieve, and figuratively to test or examine closely.
Sifted; past tense or past participial form of 'sift'.
Sigh; to breathe deeply in grief, weariness, longing, or distress.
As a noun, a deep breath or expression of sorrow.
Sighed; past tense or past participial form of 'sigh'.
Sighest; older second-person singular form of 'sigh', used with 'thou'.
Sigheth; older third-person singular form of 'sighs'.
Sighing; present participial form of 'sigh', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one sigh.
Sight; seeing; the act or power of vision; also appearance or open view.
A plural form referring to more than one sight.
A sign; a token, mark, miracle, or indication pointing to something.
Signed; past tense or past participial form of 'sign'.
Signet; a seal-ring or engraved seal used for authority and authentication.
A plural form referring to more than one signet.
meaning or the sense conveyed by a word or sign.
Signified; past tense or past participial form of 'signify'.
Signifieth; older third-person singular form of 'signify'.
Signify; to make known, indicate, or mean.
Signifying; present participial form of 'signify', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one sign.
Sihon; a man named in Scripture.
Sihor; a river name commonly used of the Nile or its border waters.
sihor.note: Location: Usually identified with Nile River.

definitions: Silas; a man named in Scripture.
Stillness or absence of speech.
Also the act of refraining from speech or the bringing of speech to an end.
Still, quiet, or not speaking.
Silk; a fine costly fabric made from silkworm thread.
Silla; a fortified place mentioned in Scripture.
Weak, helpless, or pitiable.
Sometimes simple or easily deceived, not merely playful foolishness.
Siloah; the gently flowing waters of Jerusalem, associated with the pool later called Siloam.
siloah.note: Location: In Jerusalem on the southeastern side of the city, connected with the Gihon spring and the Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Siloam; a pool or reservoir mentioned in Scripture.
siloam.note: Location: Usually identified with Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Silvanus; a man named in Scripture.
Silver; the precious metal, often used for money or vessels.
A plural form referring to more than one silverling.
Silversmith; one who works in silver.
Simeon; especially the son of Jacob and the devout man who received the child Jesus in the temple, and also the name of other biblical men.
Simeonites; the members of the tribe descended from Simeon.
likeness, resemblance, or comparison.
A plural form referring to more than one similitud.
Simon; the name of Peter before he was called Peter, and of others bearing that name according to context.
Inexperienced; easily led; lacking sound judgment.
Not merely plain or uncomplicated.
Singleness, sincerity, or freedom from mixture and guile.
Not merely lack of complexity.
Simri; a Levite or tribal name in the genealogies and temple records.
Sina; the Greek form of Sinai, the mount of the law.
sina.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jebel Musa, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Sinai; the mountain where the law was given through Moses.
The name may also extend to the surrounding wilderness region according to context.
sinai.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Jebel Musa.

definitions: Since; from the time that; because.
Sincere; pure, genuine, without deceit or mixture.
In a sincere manner or degree.
Purity, singleness, and freedom from hypocrisy or mixture.
Sinew; tendon, strength, or that which binds and strengthens.
A plural form referring to more than one sinew.
Marked by sin; guilty, corrupt, or contrary to God's law.
To sing; to utter in song.
Singed; past tense or past participial form of 'sing'.
A singer; one who sings, especially in ordered praise or public song.
In the Old Testament the term often refers to persons appointed for sacred music.
Singers; those who sing, especially persons appointed for praise and sacred music.
Singeth; older third-person singular form of 'sing'.
Singing; present participial form of 'sing', denoting ongoing action or process.
Single; one, simple, unmixed, or undivided.
The state, quality, or condition of being singlenes.
Singular; single, particular, or remarkable beyond the ordinary.
Sinim; a distant land or region named in prophecy.
Its exact identification is uncertain, but the point is of a far country from which people come.
Sinite; one belonging to the Sinite branch named among the Canaanite peoples.
Sink; to go down, settle lower, or descend beneath the surface.
Committed sin; offended against God or right.
Sinner; one guilty of sin, whether generally or as marked by open transgression.
A plural form referring to more than one sinner.
Sinnest; older second-person singular form of 'sinn', used with 'thou'.
Sins; commits sin; acts contrary to God's law.
Sinning; present participial form of 'sinn', denoting ongoing action or process.
Sins; offences, transgressions, or acts contrary to the will of God.
Sion; in Deuteronomy this name is used of Hermon, distinct from Zion in Jerusalem.
sion.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Siphmoth; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
siphmoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around As Samu.

definitions: Sippai; a Philistine giant slain in David's wars; the same giant is given under the related form Saph.
Sir; a respectful form of address used in speaking to a man, master, or superior.
Sirah; a well mentioned in Scripture.
sirah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Sarah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Sirion; the Sidonian name for Hermon.
sirion.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one sir.
Sisamai; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Sisera; the Canaanite captain defeated in the days of Deborah and Barak.
A sister; a female child of the same parents, a near kinswoman, or one so called in spiritual relation.
A plural form referring to more than one sister.
Sit down at table for a meal.
Since; seeing that.
Sitnah; the well over which Isaac's herdsmen contended after leaving Gerar.
sitnah.note: Location: In the Gerar to Beersheba region where Isaac's servants dug wells, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Sittest; thou sittest; you sit or are seated.
It may also mean you dwell or remain in a settled place.
Sits; is seated; remains in a sitting place.
Often also means dwells, abides in rule, or occupies a settled position.
Sitting; present participial form of 'sitt', denoting ongoing action or process.
Situate; placed, located, or set in a certain position.
In older usage, situated or placed.
place, position, or condition.
Sivan; the third month of the post-exilic Hebrew year.
The number six.
Sixscore; a hundred and twenty.
The number sixteen.
Next after the fifteenth.
The one next after the fifth; occupying the sixth place.
The number sixty.
Sixtyfold; sixty times as much, or in a sixtyfold degree.
Size; measure, bulk, or extent.
A plural form referring to more than one sky.
Having skill; artful, able, or expert.
In a skilful manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being skilfulnes.
Skill; knowledge, understanding, or practiced ability in a matter.
Skin; the outer covering of the body, or a hide or skin used as material.
A plural form referring to more than one skin.
Skip; to leap lightly or pass over quickly.
Skipped; past tense or past participial form of 'skip'.
Skippedst; an old second-person singular form of skipp, used with thou.
Skipping; present participial form of 'skip', denoting ongoing action or process.
Skirt; the border, edge, lower part of a garment, or the outer edge of a region.
A plural form referring to more than one skirt.
Skull; the bony frame of the head.
Sky; the visible expanse above the earth, the heavens as seen overhead.
Slack; loose, slow, remiss, or weak in zeal or effort.
As a verb, to loosen or abate.
Slacked; past tense or past participial form of 'slack'.
The state, quality, or condition of being slacknes.
Slander; false and injurious speech against another.
As a verb, to defame by such speech.
Slandered; past tense or past participial form of 'slander'.
A plural form referring to more than one slanderer.
Slanderest; older second-person singular form of 'slander', used with 'thou'.
Slandereth; older third-person singular form of 'slanders'.
In a slanderous manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one slander.
Slang; a stone or missile cast, or a line / device by context in older usage; not chiefly the modern speech sense.
Slaughter; great killing, especially in battle or judgment.
Slave; one held in bondage under the power of another.
A plural form referring to more than one slav.
To kill, especially by violence.
Slayer; one who kills, whether lawfully or unlawfully according to context.
Slayeth; older third-person singular form of 'slay'.
Slaying; present participial form of 'slay', denoting ongoing action or process.
Sleeper; one who sleeps.
Sleepest; older second-person singular form of 'sleep', used with 'thou'.
Sleeps; rests in sleep.
At times used more gently of death.
Sleeping; present participial form of 'sleep', denoting ongoing action or process.
Sleight; craft, cunning, or skill used deceitfully.
Slept; the past tense of sleep, sometimes used figuratively for death.
Slewest; older second-person singular form of 'slew', used with 'thou'.
Slidden; past participial form of slide, often used of backsliding or slipping away.
Slide; to slip, glide, or move smoothly along.
As a noun, a slipping motion or decline.
Slideth; older third-person singular form of 'slide'.
In a slight manner or degree.
Slime; sticky mud, bituminous substance, or foul ooze depending on context.
A plural form referring to more than one slimepit.
Sling; present participial form of 'sl', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one slinger.
A plural form referring to more than one sling.
A plural form referring to more than one slingston.
Slip; to slide, fall away, escape, or move quietly.
As a noun, a falling away, a small cutting, or something slender and easily moved.
Slipped; past tense or past participial form of 'slip'.
Slippery; smooth and apt to cause slipping, and figuratively unsafe or unstable.
Slippeth; older third-person singular form of 'slip'.
A plural form referring to more than one slip.
Lazy, sluggish, or slow in duty.
The state, quality, or condition of being slothfulnes.
Slow; not quick, tardy, or dull in response or motion.
In a slow manner or degree.
Sluggard; a habitually lazy or idle person.
A plural form referring to more than one sluic.
Slumber; sleep, light sleep, or spiritual drowsiness.
As a verb, to sleep lightly or be inactive.
Slumbered; past tense or past participial form of 'slumber'.
Slumbereth; older third-person singular form of 'slumber'.
