Entirely; fully; altogether.
The objective form of who.
Whomsoever; any person whom, whoever in the objective relation.
Whore; a harlot or prostitute, and figuratively one guilty of spiritual unfaithfulness.
As a verb, to commit whoredom.
fornication; in Scripture often spiritual unfaithfulness or idolatry.
A plural form referring to more than one whoredom.
Whoremonger; one given to fornication or unchaste vice.
A plural form referring to more than one whoremonger.
A plural form referring to more than one whor.
Whoring; present participial form of 'whore', denoting ongoing action or process.
Whorish; belonging to whoredom, unchaste, or spiritually adulterous in the prophetic sense.
The possessive form of who.
Whoso; whoever, any person who, or the one who.
Whoever; any person whatsoever without naming which one in particular.
For what reason; wherefore.
Evil in character and conduct; guilty of moral wrong.
In a wicked manner; evilly; with moral wrong or impiety.
Evil conduct, moral wrong, or active ungodliness.
Wide; broad, spacious, far apart, or widely according to context.
The state, quality, or condition of being widenes.
A widow; a woman whose husband is dead and who remains in that condition.
In Scripture the word often carries the added sense of social weakness and special claim to compassion and justice.
the state of being a widow.
Widows; women whose husbands are dead, often viewed in Scripture as specially needing care and justice.
A wife; a married woman in relation to her husband.
Wild; untamed, uncultivated, or growing without human ordering.
A wilderness; a desert, waste, or uncultivated region with little settled habitation.
It often denotes a solitary tract apart from cities and fruitful fields.
Crafty methods, subtle schemes, or deceitful devices.
In a wilful manner or degree.
In a wili manner or degree.
Will; desire, purpose, choice, or determination.
Willeth; older third-person singular form of 'will'.
Willing; present participial form of 'will', denoting ongoing action or process.
In a willing manner or degree.
Willow; the tree so named, especially in riverside imagery and mourning.
A plural form referring to more than one willow.
The older second-person singular form of will.
A plural form referring to more than one wimpl.
Win; to gain, obtain, overcome, or bring over.
Wind; moving air, breath, or spirit according to context.
Winding; present participial form of 'wind', denoting ongoing action or process.
A window is an opening in a wall or roof to admit light and air, often with lattice or covering.
In Scripture it may also be used figuratively of an opening from heaven or toward heaven.
A plural form referring to more than one window.
A plural form referring to more than one wind.
Windy; full of wind, empty in speech, or unstable by context.
Wine; the fermented juice of the grape.
Winebibber; one given to drinking wine excessively.
A plural form referring to more than one winebibber.
A winefat is the vat or receptacle into which the juice of the grape runs or is gathered in pressing.
A plural form referring to more than one winepres.
A plural form referring to more than one winepress.
A plural form referring to more than one win.
Wing; present participial form of 'we', denoting ongoing action or process.
Winged; past tense or past participial form of 'wing'.
Wings; the organs of flight, and by extension edges, extremes, or sheltering cover according to context.
Wink; to close the eye quickly, or figuratively to overlook.
As a noun, a quick closing of the eye.
Winked; past tense or past participial form of 'wink'.
Winketh; older third-person singular form of 'wink'.
Winneth; older third-person singular form of 'win'.
Winnowed; past tense or past participial form of 'winnow'.
Winnoweth; older third-person singular form of 'winnows'.
Winter; the cold season of the year.
Wintered; past tense or past participial form of 'winter'.
Winterhouse; a house or apartment for the cold season.
Wipe; to rub or cleanse by drawing something over a surface, or to blot out.
Wiped; past tense or past participial form of 'wipe'.
Wipeth; older third-person singular form of 'wipe'.
Wiping; present participial form of 'wipe', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one wir.
Right judgment joined with understanding and skill in godly living.
Endued with sound judgment, understanding, and skill in right conduct.
Sometimes prudent in earthly affairs, but often morally and spiritually discerning.
In a wise manner or degree.
Wiser; more wise, more prudent, or more skillful than another.
Wish; to desire, long for, or express a desire.
As a noun, a desire or longing.
Wished; past tense or past participial form of 'wish'.
Wishing; present participial form of 'wish', denoting ongoing action or process.
Knew.
An older past-tense form related to 'wit' in the sense of knowing.
Knew not; did not know.
Wit; understanding, knowledge, or mental perception, especially in the phrase to wit.
As a verb in old use, to know.
Witch; one who practises forbidden magical arts or sorcery.
Witchcraft; sorcery, magical arts, or forbidden dealings with occult power.
A plural form referring to more than one witchcraft.
With; in company with, by means of, or in relation to.
With the rest; besides; together with; at the same time.
Withdraw; to draw back, depart, take away, or keep apart.
Withdrawest; older second-person singular form of 'withdraw', used with 'thou'.
Withdraweth; older third-person singular form of 'withdraw'.
Withdrawn; drawn back, removed, or retired from presence or action.
Withdrew; past tense of withdraw, meaning drew back, retired, or removed.
Dried up, shrunk, or wasted away for lack of life or moisture.
Withers; dries up; wastes away for lack of life or moisture.
Withheld; past tense or past participial form of withhold, meaning held back or refused.
Withheldest; older second-person singular form of 'withheld', used with 'thou'.
Withhold; to hold back, keep in, restrain, or refuse to give.
Withholden; older past participial form of withhold.
Withholdeth; older third-person singular form of 'withhold'.
Within; inside, inwardly, or before the passing of a given time.
Without; outside of; beyond; lacking or destitute of.
A plural form referring to more than one with.
Withstand; to stand against, resist, oppose, or endure against.
Withstood; past tense of withstand, meaning resisted, opposed, or stood firm against.
One who bears testimony, or the testimony itself that is borne.
Also a token, evidence, or public sign that proves or confirms a matter.
Witnessed; past tense or past participial form of 'witness'.
Witnesseth; older third-person singular form of 'witness'.
Witnessing; present participial form of 'witness', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one wit.
In a witting manner or degree.
Witty; skilful, ingenious, or clever; in the KJV often meaning artful or inventive rather than merely humorous.
Wives; married women in relation to their husbands.
Wizard; one thought to possess occult knowledge or magical power.
A plural form referring to more than one wizard.
Woe; sorrow, misery, calamity, or an exclamation announcing grief or judgment.
Full of woe; sorrowful, grievous, or miserable.
A plural form referring to more than one wo.
Wolf; a fierce ravening beast, often used figuratively of cruel or destructive men.
A plural form referring to more than one wolv.
A woman; an adult female, whether wife or not according to context.
Womankind; the female sex collectively, or women in general.
The womb; the place in which an unborn child is conceived and carried.
A plural form referring to more than one womb.
Women; adult females or wives according to context.
A plural form referring to more than one womenservant.
Won; past tense and past participial form of win.
Wonder; amazement, marvel, or something that causes astonishment.
As a verb, to marvel or be astonished.
Wondered; past tense or past participial form of 'wonder'.
Full of wonder; marvelous, astonishing, or extraordinary.
In a wonderful manner or degree.
Wondering; present participial form of 'wonder', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one wonder.
Marvelous, astonishing, or worthy of wonder.
In a wondrous manner or degree.
Wont; accustomed, used, or habitual.
As a noun, habit or custom.
Wood; timber, trees, or wooden material.
A plural form referring to more than one wood.
Woof; the cross threads woven through the warp.
Wool; the fleece or hair of sheep used for clothing and weaving.
Woollen; made of wool, or resembling wool in material.
A word; a saying, message, report, matter spoken, or declared command.
Words; sayings, utterances, messages, or matters spoken.
Work; labor, deed, undertaking, or what is made or done.
Worker; one who labours, works, or performs a task.
A plural form referring to more than one worker.
Works; acts; performs or produces an effect.
Often means operates inwardly or brings something to pass.
Workfellow; a fellow-worker or companion in labour.
Working; present participial form of 'work', denoting ongoing action or process.
Workman; one who labours at a craft, trade, or appointed work.
the quality or product of skilled work.
Workmen; those who labour at a craft, trade, or appointed work.
Works; deeds, labors, or things wrought.
The world; the earth, the inhabited order, or mankind considered together, according to context.
In a world manner or degree.
A plural form referring to more than one world.
A worm is a creeping creature of low and frail kind.
In Scripture it often signifies corruption, consuming decay, or lowliness.
A plural form referring to more than one worm.
Wormwood; a bitter plant, and figuratively bitterness, sorrow, or poisonous judgment.
Worse; more evil, more grievous, or in a lower condition than another.
Honor, reverence, and adoration offered to one deemed worthy.
In Scripture most often the reverent service and homage due unto God.
A worshipper; one who worships or pays reverent service to God or to that which is treated as divine.
Worshippers; those who worship or pay reverent service.
Worshipping; present participial form of 'worship', denoting ongoing action or process.
Worst; an old second-person singular form of wor, used with thou.
Worth; value, price, merit, or desert.
A plural form referring to more than one worthy.
In a worthi manner or degree.
Fit, suitable, deserving, or of corresponding value.
Know; knoweth; knows.
A verb expressing willingness, customary action, or consequence according to context.
I wish that; if only it were so.
Wouldest; thou wouldest; you would, wish, or were inclined to.
Often expresses willingness, desire, or what one would do in a supposed case.
Wound; an injury or hurt inflicted by a blow, cut, or weapon.
As a verb, to inflict such hurt.
Wounded; past tense or past participial form of 'wound'.
Woundedst; an old second-person singular form of wound, used with thou.
Woundeth; older third-person singular form of 'wounds'.
Wounding; present participial form of 'wound', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one wound.
Wove; past tense of weave.
Woven; interlaced into fabric or texture.
Past participial form of weave.
Wrap; to fold or cover by winding around.
As a noun, a covering or wrapper.
Wrapped; past tense or past participial form of 'wrap'.
Strong anger or indignation.
Often righteous judgment when spoken of God.
Full of wrath; angry and fierce.
A plural form referring to more than one wrath.
Wreath; a twisted or woven band, garland, or ring-like ornament.
Wreathed; past tense or past participial form of 'wreath'.
Wreathen; twisted, interwoven, or made in winding form.
A plural form referring to more than one wreath.
Wrest; older second-person singular form of 'wr', used with 'thou'.
Wrestle; to strive bodily with another, or contend earnestly.
As a noun, such a struggle.
Wrestled; past tense or past participial form of 'wrestle'.
A plural form referring to more than one wrestling.
Wretched; past tense or past participial form of 'wretch'.
The state, quality, or condition of being wretchednes.
Wring; present participial form of 'wr', denoting ongoing action or process.
Wringed; past tense or past participial form of 'wring'.
Wringing; present participial form of 'wring', denoting ongoing action or process.
Wrinkle; a small fold or crease.
As a verb, to crease or make wrinkled.
A plural form referring to more than one wrinkl.
To write; to inscribe, record, or set down in writing.
Writer; one who writes, records, or serves as a scribe.
Writest; older second-person singular form of 'write', used with 'thou'.
Writeth; older third-person singular form of 'write'.
Writing; present participial form of 'write', denoting ongoing action or process.
A plural form referring to more than one writing.
Put into writing; recorded in written form.
Injustice, injury, or that which is contrary to right.
Also wrongful or unjust.
Wronged; past tense or past participial form of 'wrong'.
Wrongeth; older third-person singular form of 'wrong'.
In a wrongful manner or degree.
Wrote; the past tense of write.
Angry; filled with wrath.
Worked, performed, or brought about.
Wroughtest; older second-person singular form of 'wrought', used with 'thou'.
Wrung; past tense and past participial form of wring.
Yarn; spun thread prepared for weaving.
The older second-person plural personal pronoun.
Yes; indeed; moreover for stronger affirmation.
A year; the common annual measure of time.
In a year manner or degree.
Yearn; to long deeply, and in older usage to be moved with tender compassion.
Yearned; past tense or past participial form of 'yearn'.
Years; annual measures of time.
Yell; to cry out loudly and harshly.
As a noun, such a cry.
Yelled; past tense or past participial form of 'yell'.
Yellow; of yellow colour.
As a noun, yellow colour or yellowish appearance.
Yes; a word of assent, affirmation, or agreement.
Yesterday; the day before today, or figuratively former time.
Yesternight; last night, or in the night just past.
As yet; still; nevertheless; besides, according to context.
To give, render, or produce.
Also to give way, submit, or surrender.
Yieldeth; older third-person singular form of 'yield'.
Yielding; present participial form of 'yield', denoting ongoing action or process.
A yoke is the frame laid on beasts for drawing, or by figure any burden, service, or subjection borne.
It may signify bondage, discipline, union, or obligation according to context.
Yoked; past tense or past participial form of 'yoke'.
Yokefellow; a companion bound in the same work, relation, or burden.
A plural form referring to more than one yok.
Yonder; over there, at some distance, or that which is farther off.
The second-person pronoun, singular or plural according to context.
You-Ward; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Young; not advanced in age; youthful.
Younger; more young, or the junior of two in age or standing.
Least in age; the most young among those compared.
Belonging to you; the second-person possessive form.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
The reflexive or emphatic form of you: your own selves.
The early part of life; young age; the state of being young.
Belonging to youth; young or characteristic of youth.
A plural form referring to more than one youth.
Zaanaim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zaanaim.note: Location: Often identified with Hanot Taggarim.

definitions: Zaanan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zaanan.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tell ed Duweir.

definitions: Zaanannim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zaanannim.note: Location: Often identified with Hanot Taggarim.

definitions: Zaavan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Zavan.
Zabad; borne by several biblical men, including a conspirator against Joash and various genealogical figures.
Zabbai; the name of post-exilic men, including one who repaired the wall and one who had taken a strange wife.
Zabbud; the son of Nathan, serving as a principal officer and king's friend under Solomon.
Zabdi; borne by several biblical men, especially the ancestor of Achan and other listed Israelites.
Zabdiel; the name of men in Scripture, including a priestly chief and the father of Jashobeam.
Zabud; the son of Nathan, priest and king's friend in Solomon's court.
Zabulon; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Elon.
Zaccai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Zacchaeus; the chief publican of Jericho whom Jesus called and saved.
Zacchur; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites, musicians, and builders after the exile.
Zaccur; borne by several biblical men, especially Levites and covenant signers in the post-exilic community.
Zachariah; the form of the name Zechariah used of many men in Scripture, including prophet, priest, kingly counsellor, and father of John the Baptist.
Zacharias; the Greek form of Zechariah, especially the priest and father of John the Baptist.
Zacher; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies, related in form to Zechariah.
Zadok; the priest who remained faithful to David and whose house held the high-priestly line.
Zaham; a son of Rehoboam in the royal line of Judah.
Zair; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zair.note: Location: Often identified with Al Safi.

definitions: Zalaph; the father of Hanun, who helped repair the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Zalmon; one of David's mighty men, the Ahohite; the same warrior appears under the form Ilai.
Zalmonah; a place of encampment mentioned in Scripture.
zalmonah.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi es Salmaneh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Zalmunna; a Midianite king slain by Gideon after the overthrow of Midian.
Zamzummims; an ancient giant-like people of Ammonite tradition, akin to the Rephaim.
Zanoah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zanoah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Zanu.

definitions: Zaphnath-Paaneah; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Zaphon; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zaphon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Jebel Aqra.

definitions: Zara; the Greek form of Zerah, especially the son of Judah by Tamar.
Zarah; the form of the name Zerah used especially for the son of Judah by Tamar.
Zareah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zareah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one zareathit.
zareathites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zared; past tense or past participial form of 'zar'.
Zarephath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zaretan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zaretan.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zareth-Shahar; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zarhites; descendants of Zerah reckoned among the families of Judah.
Zartanah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zartanah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zarthan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zarthan.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zatthu; the head of a family of returned exiles, a form related to Zattu.
Zattu; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Zavan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Zaavan.
Zaza; a son of Jonathan in the genealogies of Judah.
Fervent eagerness and ardor of spirit for a cause or person.
Fervent, eager, or ardent in devotion and effort.
In a zealous manner or degree.
Zebadiah; the name of many men in Scripture, including Levites, officers, and sons in royal or tribal lines.
Zebah; a Midianite king captured and slain by Gideon.
Zebaim; the name attached especially to the valley of Zeboim and related Benjaminite references.
Zebedee; the father of James and John, the sons of Zebedee.
Zebina; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Zeboiim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zeboiim.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Zeboim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zeboim.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Zebudah; the mother of Jehoiakim king of Judah.
Zebul; the ruler of the city under Abimelech in the narrative of Shechem.
Zebulonite; a member of the tribe of Zebulun.
Zebulun; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Elon.
Zebulunites; the members of the tribe descended from Zebulun.
Zechariah; the name of many men in Scripture, especially the prophet, the father of John the Baptist, and several priests or rulers.
Zedad; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zedekiah; especially the last king of Judah before the fall of Jerusalem, and also the name of other biblical men.
Zeeb; a Midianite prince slain in the days of Gideon.
Zelah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zelah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Salah.

definitions: Zelek; one of David's mighty men, an Ammonite.
Zelophehad; a man of Manasseh whose daughters appealed for an inheritance because he had no sons.
Zelotes; the surname of Simon the apostle, meaning the Zealot.
Zelzah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zelzah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Salah.

definitions: Zemaraim; a mount or ridge in Benjamin, also associated with a nearby settlement name.
zemaraim.note: Location: Generally placed in the area around Ras et Tahuneh; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Zemarite; one belonging to the Zemarite branch named among the Canaanite peoples.
Zemira; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Zenan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zenan.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tell ed Duweir.

definitions: Zenas; the lawyer whom Paul urged Titus to help on his journey.
Zephaniah; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also the priest active in Jeremiah's day.
Zephath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zephath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Masos.

definitions: Zephathah; a valley mentioned in the days of Asa near Mareshah.
Zephi; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Zepho.
Zepho; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir; the name also appears in the form Zephi.
Zephon; a son of Gad whose descendants formed a clan, also written Ziphion.
Zephonites; descendants of Zephon reckoned among the families of Gad.
Zer; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zer.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tell el Khirbeh.

definitions: Zerah; especially the son of Judah by Tamar and also an Edomite chief, with other biblical men of the same name.
Zerahiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially priests and leaders in Ezra's return.
Zered; past tense or past participial form of 'zer'.
zered.note: Location: Usually identified with Wadi al Hasa.

definitions: Zereda; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zereda.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Ghassaneh.

definitions: Zeredathah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zeredathah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zererath; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zererath.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zeresh; the wife of Haman in the book of Esther.
Zereth; older third-person singular form of 'zer'.
Zeri; the form of the name Izri used for one of the sons of Jeduthun set over sacred music.
Zeror; an ancestor in Saul's Benjamite line.
Zeruah; the mother of Jeroboam son of Nebat.
Zerubbabel; leader of the returned exiles and governor in Judah after the captivity, associated with the rebuilding of the temple.
Zeruiah; a woman named in Scripture, daughter of Nahash, mother of Abishai.
Zetham; a son of Laadan in the Levitical genealogies.
Zethan; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Zethar; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Zia; the head of a father's house in the tribe of Gad.
Ziba; a man named in Scripture.
Zibeon; a Horite chief and father of Anah in the line of Seir.
Zibia; the mother of Joash king of Judah.
Zibiah; the name of women in Scripture, including the mother of king Joash.
Zichri; the name of many men in Scripture, especially priests, Levites, warriors, and tribal leaders.
Ziddim; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zidkijah; the father of Chenaanah, bearing a name akin to the better-known form Zedekiah.
Zidon; the son of Canaan in the table of nations, and also the ancient Phoenician city called Sidon.
A plural form referring to more than one zidonian.
zidonians.note: Location: Usually identified with Sandikli archaeological site in Sidon.

definitions: Zif; the second month of the old Hebrew year, associated with the time when Solomon began building the temple.
Ziha; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Ziklag; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
ziklag.note: Location: Often identified with Tell esh Sharia.

definitions: Zillah; one of the wives of Lamech in the line of Cain.
Zilpah; Leah's handmaid and the mother of Gad and Asher.
Zilthai; the name of men in Scripture, especially Benjamite or Manassite warriors who joined David.
Zimmah; the name of Levites and family heads in the genealogies.
Zimran; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Zimri; especially the king of Israel who reigned seven days and the Simeonite slain at Baal-peor, and also the name of other biblical men.
Zin; the wilderness region connected with Kadesh and Israel's wandering.
zin.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Zin Desert.

definitions: Zina; a descendant in the line of Shimei; the name also appears in the form Zizah.
Zion; the hill and city of Jerusalem in its sacred and royal aspect, often standing for God's dwelling and kingdom.
zion.note: Location: Originally the stronghold hill in Jerusalem, then by extension the city and sanctuary centered there.

definitions: Zior; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zior.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Ziph; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place-name.
Ziphah; a woman in the genealogies of Judah.
Ziphion; the form of the name Zephon used for a son of Gad and ancestor of a clan.
A plural form referring to more than one ziphit.
ziphites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Zif.

definitions: Ziphron; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zippor; the father of Balak king of Moab.
Zipporah; the wife of Moses and daughter of Jethro.
Zithri; a son in the line of Uzziel among the Levites.
Ziz; the ascent or pass by which enemy forces approached in the days of Jehoshaphat.
ziz.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Wadi Hasasah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Ziza; borne by several biblical men, including a son of Rehoboam and an Asherite line.
Zizah; the name of men in genealogical records, including a descendant in the line of Shimei.
Zoan; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
Zoar; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the city of Zoar.
Zoba; the form of the name Zobah used for the northern Aramean kingdom opposed by David.
zoba.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Zobah; an Aramean kingdom in the north, often opposed by David in his campaigns.
zobah.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Zobebah; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Zohar; the name of men in Scripture, including a Hittite father-in-law line associated with Ephron and a Simeonite ancestor.
Zoheleth; older third-person singular form of 'zohels'.
zoheleth.note: Location: Generally placed in the area around Bir Ayub; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Zoheth; older third-person singular form of 'zohs'.
Zophah; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Zophai; a Levite ancestor in Samuel's line; the name also appears in the form Zuph.
Zophar; the Naamathite friend of Job who spoke in the debates.
Zophim; a lookout or watch-place name associated with the heights of Pisgah.
zophim.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Tila as Safa, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Zorah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zorah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: A plural form referring to more than one zorathit.
zorathites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zoreah; an ancient city or town named in Scripture.
zoreah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zorites; a family or clan connected with the land of Edom or Seir in genealogical lists.
Zorobabel; the Greek form of Zerubbabel, leader of the returned exiles.
Zuar; the father of Nethaneel, prince of the tribe of Issachar.
Zuph; a Levite ancestor from whom the land of Zuph took its name, elsewhere represented by the form Zophai.
Zur; one of the kings or princes of Midian slain in the days of Moses.
Zuriel; the son of Abihail and prince of the Merarites.
Zurishaddai; the father of Shelumiel, prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Zuzims; an ancient people mentioned with Rephaim-like groups east of Jordan.
